columns.name: Company Name
columns.description: The name of the company developing the radiopharmaceutical product.
columns.notes: The name of the company developing the radiopharmaceutical product. Validation Strategy: Verify the company name matches official corporate records and is correctly associated with the product pipeline.

columns.name: Product / Candidate Name
columns.description: The specific name or code of the radiopharmaceutical product or candidate.
columns.notes: The specific name or code of the radiopharmaceutical product or candidate. Validation Strategy: Confirm the product/candidate name matches official company designations and clinical trial registrations.

columns.name: Target / Indication
columns.description: The biological target (e.g., FAP, SSTR2, PSMA, HER2) and the cancer type or condition it is intended to treat or diagnose.
columns.notes: The biological target (e.g., FAP, SSTR2, PSMA, HER2) and the cancer type or condition it is intended to treat or diagnose. Validation Strategy: Verify the biological target and the specific cancer indication from company pipelines, clinical trial registries, or scientific publications.

columns.name: Therapeutic Agent (Radionuclide & Payload)
columns.description: The radionuclide used for therapy (e.g., Actinium-225, Lutetium-177, Yttrium-90) and the type of payload (e.g., alpha-emitter, beta-emitter, small molecule, peptide, antibody).
columns.notes: The radionuclide used for therapy (e.g., Actinium-225, Lutetium-177, Yttrium-90) and the type of payload (e.g., alpha-emitter, beta-emitter, small molecule, peptide, antibody). Validation Strategy: Confirm the specific therapeutic radionuclide and its associated payload/mechanism of action from official product descriptions or clinical trial details.

columns.name: Diagnostic/Theranostic Agent
columns.description: Indicates if the product is a diagnostic imaging agent, a therapeutic agent, or a theranostic pair (combining both diagnostic and therapeutic capabilities). If diagnostic, specify the imaging isotope (e.g., Copper-64, Gallium-68, Fluorine-18).
columns.notes: Indicates if the product is a diagnostic imaging agent, a therapeutic agent, or a theranostic pair (combining both diagnostic and therapeutic capabilities). If diagnostic, specify the imaging isotope (e.g., Copper-64, Gallium-68, Fluorine-18). Validation Strategy: Determine if the agent is purely diagnostic, purely therapeutic, or a theranostic pair. For diagnostics, identify the imaging radionuclide.

columns.name: Specific Biochemistry & Approach
columns.description: Details on the biochemical mechanism of action, including chelator technology (e.g., Macropa), pharmacokinetic modulation (e.g., Trillium), or specific targeting molecule class (e.g., FAP inhibitor, somatostatin analog, PSMA inhibitor, anti-PD-L1 sdAb).
columns.notes: Details on the biochemical mechanism of action, including chelator technology (e.g., Macropa), pharmacokinetic modulation (e.g., Trillium), or specific targeting molecule class (e.g., FAP inhibitor, somatostatin analog, PSMA inhibitor, anti-PD-L1 sdAb). Validation Strategy: Extract details on the molecular design, chelator chemistry, and any proprietary platforms (like Trillium or Macropa) that define the drug's mechanism.

columns.name: Development Stage
columns.description: The current clinical development phase (e.g., Preclinical, Phase I, Phase II, Phase III, Approved).
columns.notes: The current clinical development phase (e.g., Preclinical, Phase I, Phase II, Phase III, Approved). Validation Strategy: Verify the latest reported clinical trial phase from official company announcements or clinical trial registries (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov).

columns.name: Latest News (Bulleted with Date)
columns.description: A bulleted list of the most recent news and developments related to the product, with dates (latest first).
columns.notes: A bulleted list of the most recent news and developments related to the product, with dates (latest first). Validation Strategy: Compile recent press releases, news articles, and conference presentations, ensuring dates are accurate and listed in reverse chronological order.

columns.name: Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Signs
columns.description: An estimated next key milestone (e.g., 'Phase II initiation Q3 2025', 'IND filing H1 2025') and any signs that the approach might be abandoned (e.g., 'Trial terminated', 'No updates in 2+ years').
columns.notes: An estimated next key milestone (e.g., 'Phase II initiation Q3 2025', 'IND filing H1 2025') and any signs that the approach might be abandoned (e.g., 'Trial terminated', 'No updates in 2+ years'). Validation Strategy: Based on current development stage and news, estimate the next logical milestone. Look for official trial terminations, prolonged silence, or strategic shifts as signs of abandonment.

columns.name: Key Partnerships/Collaborators
columns.description: Major pharmaceutical companies or specialized partners involved in co-development, licensing, manufacturing, or supply agreements for this product.
columns.notes: Major pharmaceutical companies or specialized partners involved in co-development, licensing, manufacturing, or supply agreements for this product. Validation Strategy: Identify and list all significant partners mentioned in relation to the specific product, including their role (e.g., 'Novartis - global license', 'Lantheus - co-development').

Specific Biochemistry & Approach.display_value: PSMA-targeting small molecule labeled with Actiniu...
Specific Biochemistry & Approach.full_value: PSMA-targeting small molecule labeled with Actinium-225 alpha-emitter using the PSMA I&T ligand, which enables theranostic pairing with ^68^Ga-PSMA-I&T for imaging and demonstrates similar pharmacokinetics to ^177^Lu-PSMA-I&T. The alpha-emitter delivers high linear energy transfer radiation (5.8-8.4 MeV per alpha particle) with short tissue penetration (47-85 µm) and high relative biological effectiveness compared to beta-emitters. The therapy involves potential alpha-recoil effects where daughter nuclides (^221^Fr, ^213^Bi) may redistribute, contributing to off-target toxicity in kidneys and salivary glands. The PSMA I&T ligand is used for both imaging and therapy, ensuring identical uptake patterns in tumors and healthy tissue.

comment.original_value: PSMA-targeting small molecule labeled with Actinium-225 alpha-emitter using the PSMA I&T ligand, which enables theranostic pairing with ^68^Ga-PSMA-I&T for imaging and demonstrates similar pharmacokinetics to ^177^Lu-PSMA-I&T [1]. The alpha-emitter delivers high linear energy transfer radiation (5.8-8.4 MeV per alpha particle) with short tissue penetration (47-85 µm) and high relative biological effectiveness compared to beta-emitters [1]. The therapy involves potential alpha-recoil effects where daughter nuclides (^221^Fr, ^213^Bi) may redistribute, contributing to off-target toxicity in kidneys and salivary glands [2]. The PSMA I&T ligand is used for both imaging and therapy, ensuring identical uptake patterns in tumors and healthy tissue [1].
comment.validator_explanation: The current value is comprehensive and accurate, supported by sources describing the PSMA I&T ligand, alpha-emitter properties, and alpha-recoil effects [1][2]

sources.title: Bayer Acquires Noria and PSMA Therapeutics to Expand Pipeline in Prostate Cancer
sources.snippet: Bayer today announced that it has entered into an agreement to acquire Noria Therapeutics Inc. (Noria) and PSMA Therapeutics Inc. Through this acquisition, Bayer will obtain exclusive rights to a differentiated alpha radionuclide investigational compound based on actinium-225 and a small molecule directed towards prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA).

sources.title: Acquisition of Babich-founded Noria helps Bayer pad radiopharmaceutical pipeline
sources.snippet:
June 4, 2021 1:24 AM UTC
A deal unveiled Thursday will help Bayer expand its footprint in alpha-emitting targeted radiopharmaceuticals.
Bayer AG (Xetra:BAYN) announced it will acquire Noria Therapeutics Inc. and its subsidiary PSMA Therapeutics Inc., which have exclusive worldwide rights to targeted radiotherapy and diagnostic technology licensed from Weill Cornell Medicine and Johns Hopkins University, including an actinium 225-labeled, PSMA-targeting small molecule in preclinical development for prostate cancer.

Key Partnerships/Collaborators.display_value: Bayer acquired rights to actinium-225-based cancer...
Key Partnerships/Collaborators.full_value: Bayer acquired rights to actinium-225-based cancer compounds through the June 2021 acquisition of Noria Therapeutics Inc. and PSMA Therapeutics Inc. [3][4]. The acquired companies hold exclusive worldwide rights to technology licensed from Weill Cornell Medicine and Johns Hopkins University [5]. The 225Ac-PSMA I&T trial is an investigator-initiated phase I trial conducted at Erasmus MC in Rotterdam, The Netherlands, with collaboration from the European Commission's Joint Research Centre (JRC) in Karlsruhe, Germany [2].

comment.original_value: Bayer acquired rights to actinium-225-based cancer compounds through the June 2021 acquisition of Noria Therapeutics Inc. and PSMA Therapeutics Inc. [3]. The acquired companies hold exclusive worldwide rights to technology licensed from Weill Cornell Medicine and Johns Hopkins University [3]. The 225Ac-PSMA I&T trial is an investigator-initiated phase I trial conducted at Erasmus MC in Rotterdam, The Netherlands, with collaboration from the European Commission's Joint Research Centre (JRC) in Karlsruhe, Germany [4].
comment.validator_explanation: Bayer's acquisition of Noria/PSMA Therapeutics confirmed by multiple high-confidence sources. Weill Cornell/Johns Hopkins licensing confirmed by medium-confidence source. Erasmus MC/JRC collaboration confirmed by PubMed. All partnership claims are now verified.

sources.title: RadioMedix and Orano Med receive FDA Breakthrough Therapy ...
sources.snippet:
Targeted alpha therapy (TAT) relies on a simple concept: combining the ability of biological molecules to target cancer cells with the short-range and highly energetic cell-killing capabilities of alpha-emitting radioisotopes.
Alpha decay consists of the emission of a helium nucleus (alpha particle) together with very high linear energy transfer and a range emission of only few cell layers, resulting in irreparable double strand DNA breaks in cells adjacent only to area of alpha emission.

sources.title: Press Release: AlphaMedixTM (212Pb-DOTAMTATE) achieved all ...
sources.snippet:
** Positive results from the ALPHAMEDIX-02 phase 2 study (clinical study identifier: NCT05153772) showed AlphaMedix TM ( 212Pb-DOTAMTATE), an investigational somatostatin receptor (SSTR)-Targeted Alpha Therapy using the lead-212 isotope
, met all primary efficacy endpoints and showed clinically meaningful overall response rates (ORR) and prolonged clinical benefits in both peptide receptor radionuclide therapy (PRRT)-naïve and PRRT-exposed patients with unresectable or metastatic SSTR positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs).

sources.snippet:
** Positive results from the ALPHAMEDIX-02 phase 2 study (clinical study identifier: NCT05153772) showed AlphaMedix TM ( 212Pb-DOTAMTATE), an investigational somatostatin receptor (SSTR)-Targeted Alpha Therapy using the lead-212 isotope

Specific Biochemistry & Approach.full_value: Somatostatin receptor-targeting peptide (DOTAMTATE) complex radiolabeled with lead-212 (²¹²Pb), which serves as an in vivo generator of alpha particles for targeted alpha therapy. The DOTAMTATE chelator binds to somatostatin receptor 2 (SSTR2) expressed on neuroendocrine tumor cells, delivering the alpha-emitting payload with high precision. Lead-212 decays through a short-range alpha particle emission chain (via bismuth-212) that causes double-strand DNA breaks in cancer cells while minimizing damage to surrounding healthy tissue [3].

comment.original_value: Somatostatin receptor-targeting peptide (DOTAMTATE) complex radiolabeled with lead-212 (²¹²Pb), which serves as an in vivo generator of alpha particles for targeted alpha therapy [1]. The DOTAMTATE chelator binds to somatostatin receptor 2 (SSTR2) expressed on neuroendocrine tumor cells, delivering the alpha-emitting payload with high precision [2]. Lead-212 decays through a short-range alpha particle emission chain (via bismuth-212) that causes double-strand DNA breaks in cancer cells while minimizing damage to surrounding healthy tissue [2].

sources.title: Perspective Therapeutics Announces Acceptance of VMT-α-NET Data for Presentation at the ASCO Gastrointestinal Cancers Symposium
sources.snippet: SEATTLE, Dec. 04, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Perspective Therapeutics, Inc. ('Perspective' or the 'Company') (NYSE AMERICAN: CATX), a radiopharmaceutical development company pioneering advanced treatments for cancers throughout the body, today announced that updated data on the Company's [212Pb]VMT-α-NET program have been accepted for presentation at the American Society of Clinical Oncology Gastrointestinal Cancers Symposium (ASCO-GI) Congress 2026 taking place from January 8 to 10, 2026 in San Francisco, CA as detailed below. ... Perspective designed [212Pb]VMT-α-NET to target and deliver 212Pb to tumor sites expressing SSTR2. The Company is conducting a multi-center, open-label, dose-escalation, dose-expansion study (clinicaltrials.gov identifier NCT05636618) of [212Pb]VMT-α-NET in patients with unresectable or metastatic SSTR2-positive neuroendocrine tumors who have not received prior radiopharmaceutical therapies (RPT).

sources.title: Convergent Therapeutics Announces First Patient Treated in Phase II Clinical Trial with Lead Therapeutic Candidate and Corporate Updates
sources.snippet:
First Patient Dosed with CONV01-α in Phase II Clinical Trial in Patients with Metastatic Castration Resistant Prostate Cancer ... CAMBRIDGE, Mass., Sept. 24, 2024 /PRNewswire/ -- Convergent Therapeutics Inc., a clinical stage biotechnology company focused on developing next generation radiopharmaceutical therapies for the treatment of prostate cancer and other cancers, today announced that the first patient has been dosed with CONV01-α in the Phase II CONVERGE-01 trial evaluating CONV01-α, Ac-225 rosopatamab tetraxetan, for the treatment of patients with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) PET-positive metastatic castration resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). ... In Part 1, the first 5 participants will receive In-111-rosopatamab tetraxetan to characterize the biodistribution of the radioantibody to target organs and prostate cancer lesions.
Participants will then be enrolled in either Part 2 (dose optimization) or Part 3 (dose escalation) depending on their prior treatment history.
Part 2 will enroll Lu-177-PSMA-radioligand therapy-naïve participants and Part 3 will enroll participants who received prior Lu-177-PSMA-radioligand therapy. ... CONV01-α, Convergent's alpha emitting radioantibody, combines the precision and pharmacokinetics of antibodies with the tumor-killing potential of alpha emitting radionuclides. Specifically, CONV01-α uses a humanized monoclonal antibody targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) which is highly overexpressed in prostate cancer cells. ... Unlike other radioactive sources, alpha particles deliver high-energy radiation over very short distances, thereby minimizing radiation exposure to healthy neighboring cells and tissues.

Therapeutic Agent (Radionuclide & Payloa.full_value: Actinium-225 alpha-emitter conjugated to a humanized monoclonal antibody (rosopatamab/J591)

Part 2 will enroll Lu-177-PSMA-radioligand therapy-naïve participants and Part 3 will enroll participants who received prior Lu-177-PSMA-radioligand therapy. ... Drug Development Veteran Richard Messmann, MD Appointed as Chief Medical Officer to Lead the Development of CONV01-α ... Novo Holdings Invests $40 million in Series A Extension to Advance Clinical Development of Radiopharmaceuticals at Convergent Therapeutics ... Previously conducted academic studies of CONV01-α have shown encouraging therapeutic results in patients with prostate cancer.

sources.title: RTX-240 Monotherapy and in Combination With Pembrolizumab - NCI
sources.snippet:
Open label, multicenter, multidose, first-in-human Phase 1/2 study of RTX-240 monotherapy or in combination of pembrolizumab for the treatment of patients with (1) relapsed/refractory R/R or locally advanced solid tumors (Phase 1/2) or (2) R/R Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML) (Phase 1 only). ... RTX-240 is a cellular therapy that co-expresses 4-1BBL and IL-15TP, a fusion of IL-15 and
IL-15 receptor alpha, with the goal of stimulating the innate and adaptive immune systems

sources.title: Takeaways From Phase 1 Trial of RTX-240 in Solid Tumors
sources.snippet:
It's rather unique…it's actually a red cell therapy, and the key thing to think about red blood cells is that they don't replicate.
So what they've been engineered to do, and these are engineered red blood cells taken from donor cells [from] donor banks, is that they've been engineered to express, in this case, 4-1BBL and IL-15 TP.

Therapeutic Agent (Radionuclide & Payloa.full_value: Actinium-225 alpha-emitter conjugated to FAP-targeted radiotherapeutic

comment.validator_explanation: RTX-240 is an engineered red blood cell therapy, not an Actinium-225 radiopharmaceutical
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator's update incorrectly describes RTX-240's mechanism. The correct therapeutic agent is Actinium-225 labeled to a FAP-targeted compound, as confirmed by the press release.

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics has Successfully Dosed the First Cohort in its Phase 1/2 Study Evaluating a Novel FAP-Targeted Radiopharmaceutical in Patients with Late-Stage Aggressive Sarcomas
sources.snippet: BOSTON, Dec. 16, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, announced today that dosing of the first cohort has been completed in the ATLAS trial, a Phase 1/2 open-label study evaluating the safety, tolerability, and efficacy of the company's lead therapeutic radiopharmaceutical, [Ac-225]-RTX-2358, targeting fibroblast activation protein‑α (FAP) in patients with relapsed or refractory soft tissue sarcomas. ... The ATLAS trial is a Phase 1/2 open-label clinical trial (clinicaltrials.gov identifier, NCT07156565) designed to assess the safety, tolerability, dosimetry, biodistribution, pharmacokinetics, and preliminary anti-tumor activity of [Ac-225]-RTX-2358, a highly selective, FAP targeted radiotherapeutic labeled with Actinium 225, in patients with relapsed or refractory soft tissue sarcomas that express FAP. ... The Phase 2 portion of the study will evaluate the efficacy and safety of [Ac-225]-RTX-2358 in up to 50 patients. [Ac-225]-RTX-2358 is being manufactured by Pharmalogic. [Cu-64]-LNTH-1363S is being provided by Lantheus and manufactured by PharmaLogic.

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics, In Collaboration with Lantheus and ...
sources.snippet:
*A collaboration to advance development of a fibroblast activation protein (FAP)-targeted PET diagnostic compound for a broad array of epithelial-derived cancers.* BOSTON, July 11, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Ratio Therapeutics Inc.
(Ratio), a pharmaceutical company that employs a suite of innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for the monitoring and treatment of cancers today announced, in partnership with Lantheus, a company committed to improving patient outcomes through diagnostics, radiotherapeutics and artificial intelligence solutions that enable clinicians to Find, Fight and ... "We are delighted by our collaboration with Ratio Therapeutics in advancing the clinical development of RTX-1363S as a FAP-targeted diagnostic to the clinic," stated Etienne Montagut, Lantheus' Chief Business Officer. "By leveraging our expertise in radiopharmaceuticals, we aim to contribute to the early detection and accurate monitoring of epithelial-derived cancers.

sources.title: PharmaLogic Announces Successful Supply of First Cohort Dosing for Ratio Therapeutics' Phase 1/2 Study of a Novel FAP-Targeted Radiopharmaceutical in Late-Stage Aggressive Sarcomas
sources.snippet: BRONX, N.Y., Dec. 16, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- PharmaLogic Holdings Corp. ("PharmaLogic" or "the Company"), a leading contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) specialized in radiopharmaceuticals, announced today that it has successfully supplied the first cohort dosing of Ratio Therapeutics' investigational FAP-targeted radiopharmaceutical, [Ac-225]-RTX-2358, for the ATLAS trial.

sources.title: MC-339 / Novartis - LARVOL DELTA
sources.snippet: "ETN029 (also known as MC339) is a novel macrocyclic peptide that binds to DLL3 (Fig 4) designed to achieve high, rapid uptake and retention in tumor tissue and fast clearance of the unbound compound via the renal route...Chemotherapy was administered for a total of three days (cisplatin 2.5 mg/kg IP on Day 13; etoposide 3.5 mg/kg IP on Days 13, 14, and 15)... ... In CDX models (NCI-H69, SHP-77), a single dose of [225Ac]Ac-ETN029 (0.35-1.4 µCi) resulted in robust tumor regression and prolonged survival. ... The acquisition brings together Mariana Oncology’s innovative radiopharmaceutical pipeline and platform with the proven clinical development and commercialization expertise of Novartis....The company’s lead program, MC-339, is a novel radioligand therapy (RLT) designed to target small cell lung cancer."

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- May 2, 2024: Novartis announces agreement to acquire Mariana Oncology for $1 billion upfront plus up to $750 million in milestone payments, gaining access to MC-339 and other RLT programs [1]
- September 13, 2024: Pharmacodynamics data from preclinical studies of MC-339 in small cell lung cancer presented at the 49th European Society for Medical Oncology Congress (ESMO 2024) [2]
- 2023: Mariana Oncology closes $175 million Series B financing to advance pipeline including MC-339, with plans to enter clinical trials in 2024 [3]
- September 7, 2023: Mariana Oncology plans a clinical trial for small cell lung cancer in 2024 [2]

comment.original_value: -  Phase 1 trial initiated (NCT06102421, 2023) -  First patient dosed (2024)
comment.validator_explanation: Original news items were incomplete and contained incorrect trial identifier (NCT06102421). Updated with comprehensive, chronologically ordered news from authoritative sources including the Novartis acquisition (May 2024), ESMO 2024 data presentation, and 2023 financing. The original 'Phase 1 trial initiated (NCT06102421, 2023)' claim is incorrect - no clinical trial registration for MC-339 was found, and the program remained preclinical through 2024.
comment.qc_reasoning: Major update: replaced incorrect/incomplete news with comprehensive, chronologically ordered items from authoritative sources, correcting false trial identifier.

sources.title: Novartis enters agreement to acquire Mariana Oncology ...
sources.snippet: **Basel, May 2, 2024 **– Novartis today announced that it has entered into an agreement to acquire Mariana Oncology, a preclinical-stage biotechnology company based in Watertown, Massachusetts focused on developing novel radioligand therapies (RLTs) to treat cancers with high unmet patient need. ... The acquisition encompasses a robust portfolio of RLT programs spanning lead optimization to early development across a range of solid tumor indications such as breast, prostate and lung cancer – including development candidate MC-339, an actinium-based RLT being investigated in small cell lung cancer. ... Under the terms of the agreement, Novartis will make an upfront payment of USD 1 billion and additional USD 750 million in payments upon completion of pre-specified milestones.

comment.key_citation: [V1] Clarity receives FDA Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA (0.85): "Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA for positron emission tomography (PET) imaging of prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) positive prostate cancer lesions with suspected metastasis who are candidates for initial definitive therapy. ... The completed Phase I PROPELLER study demonstrated favourable safety and efficacy results in patients with prostate cancer prior to radical prostatectomy. ... In parallel, the Phase I/II trial, COBRA, 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA was found to be safe and highly effective in detecting prostate cancer lesions in patients with BCR. ... Driven by the compelling findings from the PROPELLER study, Clarity commenced a registrational Phase III trial in this patient population, CLARIFY, where recruitment is ongoing. ... **Clarity’s Executive Chairperson, Dr Alan Taylor, commented,** “Receiving Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA is a significant milestone, especially as we are actively recruiting into our first registrational Phase III trial, CLARIFY, and preparing for an End of Phase meeting with the FDA for a second pivotal Phase III trial with this product." (https://www.claritypharmaceuticals.com/news/fast-track/)

sources.title: Clarity receives FDA Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA
sources.snippet: Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA for positron emission tomography (PET) imaging of prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) positive prostate cancer lesions with suspected metastasis who are candidates for initial definitive therapy. ... The completed Phase I PROPELLER study demonstrated favourable safety and efficacy results in patients with prostate cancer prior to radical prostatectomy. ... In parallel, the Phase I/II trial, COBRA, 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA was found to be safe and highly effective in detecting prostate cancer lesions in patients with BCR. ... Driven by the compelling findings from the PROPELLER study, Clarity commenced a registrational Phase III trial in this patient population, CLARIFY, where recruitment is ongoing. ... **Clarity’s Executive Chairperson, Dr Alan Taylor, commented,** “Receiving Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA is a significant milestone, especially as we are actively recruiting into our first registrational Phase III trial, CLARIFY, and preparing for an End of Phase meeting with the FDA for a second pivotal Phase III trial with this product.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: - Jan 18, 2026: First patient dosed with satoreoti...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- Jan 18, 2026: First patient dosed with satoreotide therapy in extensive stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) study at Murdoch University Health Center in Australia [1]
- Oct 14, 2025: Ariceum Therapeutics and Nusano sign multi-isotope supply agreement for actinium-225 (Ac-225) and lutetium-177 (Lu-177) to support satoreotide development [2]
- Feb 6, 2025: FDA grants orphan drug designation to Ariceum's proprietary radiopharmaceutical cancer therapy [3]
- Jan 13-14, 2025: Ariceum and ITM Isotope Technologies Munich SE execute supply agreement for non-carrier-added Actinium-225 (Ac-225) to support satoreotide development [4]
- Dec 5, 2024: Ariceum and Eckert & Ziegler sign global supply agreement for medical radionuclides Actinium-225 (Ac-225) and Lutetium-177 (Lu-177) for satoreotide clinical studies and commercial phases [5]
- Sep 28, 2023: Phase I/II trial results published showing 95% disease control rate (36 of 38 patients) and 21% partial response rate (8 of 38 patients) with [¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-satoreotide tetraxetan in advanced neuroendocrine tumors [6]
- Sep 18, 2023: Phase I/II study published in European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging evaluating safety and efficacy of [¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-satoreotide tetraxetan in 40 patients with progressive neuroendocrine tumors [7]
- Sep 7, 2023: Ariceum doses first patient with satoreotide in Phase Ib study in small cell lung cancer [8]
- Jun 1, 2023: Ariceum expands pipeline with acquisition of Theragnostics Ltd, obtaining additional therapeutic and diagnostic assets [9]

(0.95): "ProstACT GLOBAL (ClinicalTrials.gov ID: NCT04876651) is the first Phase III trial to evaluate TLX591 in adult patients with PSMA-positive metastatic castrate-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) administered together with Standard of Care (SoC, androgen receptor inhibition or taxanes) versus SoC alone. ... To date, 242 patients have been treated across eight Phase I and II studies of TLX591, including Telix’s ProstACT SELECT study (ClinicalTrials.gov ID: NCT04786847), which confirmed the clinical validity of Telix’s optimal fractionated dosing and product safety profile. ... ProstACT GLOBAL has an overall target enrolment of ~400 patients, with the first dose successfully administered at GenesisCare’s centre at the St John of God Hospital Murdoch campus in Australia.[4]" (https://telixpharma.com/news-views/first-patient-dosed-in-phase-iii-prostact-global-study-of-antibody-based-prostate-cancer-therapy-candidate-tlx591/)

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Dec 19, 2023: Positive topline data from Phase 3...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Dec 19, 2023: Positive topline data from Phase 3 SPLASH trial (NCT04647526) announced, showing median radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) of 9.5 months vs. 6 months for ARPI (29% reduction in risk) [1]
• Dec 4, 2023: Eli Lilly extended tender offer deadline for acquisition; 24.75% of Point shares tendered [2]
• Oct 3, 2023: Eli Lilly announced agreement to acquire POINT Biopharma for $12.50 per share (~$1.4B) [3]
• 2023: Phase 3 SPLASH trial completed enrollment of 412 patients [1]

comment.original_value: -  Phase 3 SPLASH trial completed enrollment (2023) -  Acquired by Eli Lilly (2023)
comment.validator_explanation: Updated with 2023 topline data readout and acquisition details from high-confidence sources. Original lacked specific dates and details.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator significantly improved from vague bullets to detailed, dated entries with specific outcomes, greatly enhancing information value.

sources.title: POINT's prostate cancer trial meets primary endpoint as ...
sources.snippet: US-based Lantheus and POINT Biopharma have announced positive topline data from the Phase III trial investigating prostate cancer therapy 177Lu-PNT2002, as Eli Lilly extends its tender offer deadline. ... The study demonstrated a statistically significant median radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) of 9.5 months with 177Lu-PNT2002 compared to six months with ARPI, indicating a 29% reduction in the risk of radiographic progression or death. ... The multicentre, randomised, open-label SPLASH trial (NCT04647526) enrolled 412 PSMA-expressing patients across the US, Canada, Europe, and the UK, with 84.6% of control arm patients crossing over to receive 177-Lu-PNT2002 upon disease progression.

sources.title: Novartis' Pluvicto readout is causing trouble for Eli Lilly's $1.4B ...
sources.snippet:
Fast forward to Monday, Dec. 4, Lilly pushed back the deadline of its tender offer for Point’s shares for a second time.
This time, only 24.75% of Point shares had been tendered, even lower than the 26.45% Lilly previously collected as of Nov. 16.

sources.title: Lilly to Acquire POINT Biopharma to Expand Oncology Capabilities into Next-Generation Radioligand Therapies
sources.snippet: Lilly will commence a tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of POINT for a purchase price of $12.50 per share in cash (an aggregate of approximately $1.4 billion) payable at closing.

sources.snippet: RayzeBio, Inc., a targeted radiopharmaceutical company developing an innovative pipeline against validated solid tumor targets, today announced significant progress in clinical development of its lead product candidate, RYZ101. RYZ101 is an investigational, targeted radiopharmaceutical drug designed to deliver Actinium-225 (Ac225), a highly potent alpha-emitting radioisotope, to solid tumors expressing the somatostatin receptor (SSTR). - The Company is enrolling patients in the Phase 1b portion of the ACTION-1 Phase 1b/3 trial of RYZ101 (NCT05477576). ... RYZ101 is an investigational targeted radiopharmaceutical therapy, designed to deliver a highly potent radioisotope, Actinium-225 (Ac225), to tumors expressing SSTR2. RYZ101 is being evaluated in clinical studies for patients with SSTR+ GEP-NETs who have previously been treated with Lu177-based somatostatin therapies and also in patients with extensive stage small cell lung cancer.

comment.validator_explanation: The original value 'AKT-028' appears to be incorrect. Extensive search across multiple authoritative sources confirms Aktis Oncology's pipeline consists of Ac-AKY-1189 (Nectin-4 targeting, currently in Phase 1b) and Ac-AKY-2519 (B7-H3 targeting, IND filing planned 2026) [3][4][5]. No product designated 'AKT-028' exists in Aktis Oncology's documented pipeline. The lead candidate AKY-1189 is the 225Ac-based miniprotein radiopharmaceutical currently enrolling ~150 patients in Phase 1b trials [3][6]
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator correctly identified the lead candidate. Original 'AKT-028' appears to be an error. Multiple authoritative sources confirm Aktis Oncology's pipeline includes Ac-AKY-1189 and Ac-AKY-2519, with no mention of 'AKT-028'.

comment.key_citation: [V1] Phase 1 solid tumor biotech Aktis Oncology files for a $100 ... (0.85): "Aktis Oncology, a Phase 1 biotech developing targeted radiopharmaceuticals to treat solid tumors, filed on Friday with the SEC to raise up to $100 million in an initial public offering. Aktis Oncology is a clinical-stage oncology company developing alpha-emitting radiopharmaceuticals against prevalent solid tumors. Its pipeline features Ac-AKY-1189 for Nectin-4-expressing cancers, now enrolling about 150 patients in a multi-site US Phase 1b trial with preliminary results expected in the first quarter of 2027, and Ac-AKY-2519 targeting B7-H3-expressing tumors with an IND filing planned for 2026. It also manages multiple domestic and international isotope supply partnerships, invests in an internal cGMP facility set to be operational in 2026, and holds a discovery collaboration for additional miniprotein radioconjugate programs." (https://www.renaissancecapital.com/IPO-Center/News/115711/Phase-1-solid-tumor-biotech-Aktis-Oncology-files-for-a-$100-million-IPO)

sources.title: Phase 1 solid tumor biotech Aktis Oncology files for a $100 ...

Aktis Oncology is a clinical-stage oncology company developing alpha-emitting radiopharmaceuticals against prevalent solid tumors. Its pipeline features Ac-AKY-1189 for Nectin-4-expressing cancers, now enrolling about 150 patients in a multi-site US Phase 1b trial with preliminary results expected in the first quarter of 2027, and Ac-AKY-2519 targeting B7-H3-expressing tumors with an IND filing planned for 2026. It also manages multiple domestic and international isotope supply partnerships, invests in an internal cGMP facility set to be operational in 2026, and holds a discovery collaboration for additional miniprotein radioconjugate programs.

(0.85): "Aktis Oncology, a Phase 1 biotech developing targeted radiopharmaceuticals to treat solid tumors, filed on Friday with the SEC to raise up to $100 million in an initial public offering. Aktis Oncology is a clinical-stage oncology company developing alpha-emitting radiopharmaceuticals against prevalent solid tumors. Its pipeline features Ac-AKY-1189 for Nectin-4-expressing cancers, now enrolling about 150 patients in a multi-site US Phase 1b trial with preliminary results expected in the first quarter of 2027, and Ac-AKY-2519 targeting B7-H3-expressing tumors with an IND filing planned for 2026. It also manages multiple domestic and international isotope supply partnerships, invests in an internal cGMP facility set to be operational in 2026, and holds a discovery collaboration for additional miniprotein radioconjugate programs." (https://www.renaissancecapital.com/IPO-Center/News/115711/Phase-1-solid-tumor-biotech-Aktis-Oncology-files-for-a-$100-million-IPO)

- Oct 3, 2023: Eli Lilly announces definitive agreement to acquire POINT Biopharma for approximately $1.4 billion [2]
- 2023: Phase 3 SPLASH trial fully enrolled (NCT04647526) [3]

comment.key_citation: [V1] POINT's prostate cancer trial meets primary endpoint as ... (0.85): "US-based Lantheus and POINT Biopharma have announced positive topline data from the Phase III trial investigating prostate cancer therapy 177Lu-PNT2002, as Eli Lilly extends its tender offer deadline. ... The study demonstrated a statistically significant median radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) of 9.5 months with 177Lu-PNT2002 compared to six months with ARPI, indicating a 29% reduction in the risk of radiographic progression or death." sources.title: Perspective Therapeutics Announces Acceptance of VMT-α-NET Data for Presentation at the ASCO Gastrointestinal Cancers Symposium
sources.snippet: SEATTLE, Dec. 04, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Perspective Therapeutics, Inc. ('Perspective' or the 'Company') (NYSE AMERICAN: CATX), a radiopharmaceutical development company pioneering advanced treatments for cancers throughout the body, today announced that updated data on the Company's [212Pb]VMT-α-NET program have been accepted for presentation at the American Society of Clinical Oncology Gastrointestinal Cancers Symposium (ASCO-GI) Congress 2026 taking place from January 8 to 10, 2026 in San Francisco, CA as detailed below.

sources.title: Perspective Therapeutics Announces Acceptance of VMT-α-NET Data for Presentation at the ASCO Gastrointestinal Cancers Symposium
sources.snippet: SEATTLE, Dec. 04, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Perspective Therapeutics, Inc. ('Perspective' or the 'Company') (NYSE AMERICAN: CATX), a radiopharmaceutical development company pioneering advanced treatments for cancers throughout the body, today announced that updated data on the Company's [212Pb]VMT-α-NET program have been accepted for presentation at the American Society of Clinical Oncology Gastrointestinal Cancers Symposium (ASCO-GI) Congress 2026 taking place from January 8 to 10, 2026 in San Francisco, CA as detailed below.

Target / Indication.full_value: SSTR2; Neuroendocrine Tumors

comment.validator_explanation: Confirmed as SSTR2+ neuroendocrine tumors in clinical trial descriptions [1] and study overviews [2].
comment.key_citation: [V1] Antitumor Activity Seen With Targeted Alpha Therapy [212Pb]VMT-α ... (0.85): "**phase 1/2a trial** 212Pb]VMT-α-NET, an investigational targeted alpha-particle therapy, exhibited preliminary antitumor activity in patients with advanced somatostatin receptor type 2-positive (SSTR2+) neuroendocrine tumors (NETs), including evidence of durable disease control and deepening tumor responses. 1 These updated data were presented recently at the 2026 American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) Gastrointestinal Cancers Symposium." (https://www.targetedonc.com/view/antitumor-activity-seen-with-targeted-alpha-therapy-212pb-vmt---net-in-sstr2-nets)

sources.title: Study Details | NCT05636618 | Targeted Alpha-Particle Therapy for ...
sources.snippet: [212Pb] VMT-a-NET is a targeted alpha therapy agent designed to enhance the precision and effectiveness of cancer treatment by delivering a highly potent form of radiation directly to cancer cells.

sources.title: [203/212Pb]Pb-VMT-α-NET as a novel theranostic agent for targeted ...
sources.snippet: A novel development in the field of TAT for NETs is the use of the theranostic pair [^203^Pb]Pb-VMT-α-NET and [^212^Pb]Pb-VMT-α-NET (Fig. 1). ... Because of its elemental identity with the therapeutic agent ^212^Pb, it is suitable for the determination of image-derived individualized normal organ and tumor dosimetry-based treatment plans [3]. ^212^Pb (t~1/2~ = 10.6 h) itself decays via beta decay to ^212^Bi, which further decays via 36% alpha decay (6.1 MeV) to ^208^Tl and 64% beta decay to ^212^Po, followed by 100% alpha decay (8.8 MeV) to ^208^Pb.

sources.title: Study Details | Somatostatin-Receptors (SSTR)-Agonist [212Pb]VMT-alpha-NET in Metastatic or Inoperable SSTR+ Gastrointestinal Neuroendocrine Tumor and Pheochromocytoma/Paraganglioma Previously Treated With Systemic Targeted Radioligand Therapy
sources.snippet: The drug has 2 components: a protein to bind to SSTR and a radioactive agent to kill the cancer cells. ... - [203Pb]VMT-alpha-NET is the chemically identical imaging surrogate for [212Pb]VMT-alpha-NET and has the same mechanism of action via binding to SSTR2. The nuclide 203Pb contained in [203Pb]VMT-alpha-NET emits gamma radiation suitable for single-photon emission computerized tomography (SPECT) imaging. ... - Phase I: To determine the maximal tolerated dose (MTD) of [212Pb]VMT-alpha-NET using a 3+3 dose escalation design in GI NET and PPGL in a re-treatment setting.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- Jan 16, 2026: Promising antitumor activity with 39% objective response rate in cohort 2 presented at ASCO GI Symposium [1]
- Dec 4, 2025: VMT-α-NET data accepted for presentation at ASCO Gastrointestinal Cancers Symposium [2]
- Feb 27, 2025: Interim Phase 1/2a safety and efficacy results published showing no DLTs up to 5 mCi dose [3]
- 2023: IND cleared for Phase 1/2a trial (NCT05636618) [4]
- 2023: Acquired Viewpoint Molecular Targeting

sources.title: Perspective Therapeutics Enters into Strategic Agreements with ...
sources.snippet: *Lantheus will have the option to negotiate an exclusive license to Perspective Therapeutics’ Pb212-VMT-α-NET* *Lantheus will have the option to* *co-fund and negotiate an exclusive license for certain early-stage therapeutic candidates targeting prostate cancer using Perspective’s lead platform technology* *Lantheus agrees to invest up to approximately $33 million in Perspective Therapeutics equity subject to Perspective Therapeutics’ completion of a qualifying third-party financing transaction* *Perspective Therapeutics agrees to acquire radiopharmaceutical manufacturing facility in Somerset, New Jersey*

sources.title: Perspective Therapeutics, Inc. Enters into Strategic Agreements with Lantheus Including Equity Investment, Co-Development Options, and Expansion of Manufacturing Capability
sources.snippet:
For an upfront payment of $28 million in cash, Lantheus will obtain an exclusive option to negotiate for an exclusive license to Perspective?
s [212Pb]VMT-a-NET, a clinical stage alpha therapy developed for the treatment of neuroendocrine tumors, and a right to co-fund the IND-enabling studies for early-stage therapeutic candidates targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) and gastrin releasing peptide receptor (GRPR) and, prior to IND filing, a right to negotiate for an exclusive license to such candidates.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: - Oct 23, 2018: Phase I clinical trial (NCT0372474...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- Oct 23, 2018: Phase I clinical trial (NCT03724747) initiated to evaluate safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and antitumor activity of BAY 2315497 (Thorium-227 labeled antibody-chelator conjugate) alone and in combination with darolutamide in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) [1].
- 2020: Preclinical efficacy of PSMA-targeted thorium-227 conjugate (PSMA-TTC/BAY 2315497) published, showing strong antitumor activity in prostate cancer models and supporting ongoing clinical evaluation [2].
- 2023: Antibody-based targeted thorium-227 conjugates, including BAY 2315497, noted as having entered early clinical testing [3].
- 2024: Clinical trial status for NCT03724747 reported as 'COMPLETED' [4][5].
- 2025: BAY 2315497 described as a novel, antibody-based targeted alpha therapy (TAT) consisting of thorium-227 complexed by a 3,2-HOPO chelator linked to a fully human PSMA-targeting antibody [6].

comment.original_value:
- Phase I trial initiated in 2023
- Part of Bayer's targeted alpha therapy platform

sources.title: Bayer and Lilly make radioligand progress - Oncology Pipeline
sources.snippet: Bayer in June 2021 acquired two companies, Noria and PSMA Therapeutics, and their work on actinium has yielded BAY 3563254. The work is relevant in light of Novartis’s plans to expand its anti-PSMA portfolio beyond Pluvicto; today the Swiss group's pipeline includes the phase 1 assets AAA802 (225-Ac-PSMA-R2, derived from Advanced Accelerated Applications) and AAA817 (225-Ac-PSMA-617, from Endocyte).

The work is relevant in light of Novartis’s plans to expand its anti-PSMA portfolio beyond Pluvicto; today the Swiss group's pipeline includes the phase 1 assets AAA802 (225-Ac-PSMA-R2, derived from Advanced Accelerated Applications) and AAA817 (225-Ac-PSMA-617, from Endocyte).

sources.title: Fibroblast activation protein (FAP)-targeted radionuclide ...
sources.snippet:
A key tool in the pro-tumorigenic role of CAFs is the fibroblast activation protein (FAP), which is a type II transmembrane serine protease that cleaves peptide hormones [3].
Generally, FAP is overexpressed on the CAFs of over 90% of epithelial tumors such as breast, colorectal, head and neck, lung, ovarian, and pancreatic adenocarcinomas [2, 3].
Owing to limited FAP expression in normal tissues, it has been identified as a “pan-tumoral” target for the molecular imaging and targeted therapy of cancer [4].

sources.title: Eli Lilly's strategy in motion: Beyond diabetes and obesity - Labiotech.eu
sources.snippet:
Taken together, these moves sketch Eli Lilly's intent on broadening its pipeline strategy beyond its incretin franchise while shoring up multiple growth pillars. 



2023-2024: Laying the groundwork in radiopharma 



Lilly’s ambitions in oncology have gone beyond antibodies and small molecules to embrace radiopharmaceuticals, an area gaining traction at the moment.

sources.title: Radionetics Oncology Enters Into Strategic Agreement With Lilly
sources.snippet: **SAN DIEGO, CA - JULY 1, 2024** - Radionetics Oncology a biotechnology company discovering and developing novel small molecule G protein coupled receptor (GPCR) targeted radiopharmaceuticals to treat a broad range of solid tumors, announced today the formation of a strategic relationship with Eli Lilly and Company to take forward Radionetics’ proprietary GPCR targeting small molecule radiopharmaceuticals.

sources.title: AKTIS ONCOLOGY ENTERS INTO STRATEGIC COLLABORATION ...
sources.snippet: BOSTON, Mass. – May 21, 2024 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Aktis Oncology, a biotechnology company discovering and developing novel targeted radiopharmaceuticals to treat a broad range of solid tumors, today announced a multi-target discovery collaboration agreement with Eli Lilly and Company to generate anticancer radiopharmaceuticals using Aktis Oncology’s novel miniprotein technology platform.

sources.title: Initial clinical experience with [177Lu]Lu-PNT2002 radioligand ...
sources.snippet: [^177^Lu]Lu-PNT2002 consists of a beta-emitting [^177^Lu]lutetium radioisotope chelated to a small-molecule ligand (also known as PSMA-Imaging & Therapy [I&T]) that targets PSMA using a glutamate urea-based pharmacophore, enabling targeted delivery of radiation to prostate cancer cells, causing DNA damage and ultimately cancer cell death. [^177^Lu]Lu-PSMA-I&T used as an investigational agent has demonstrated promising anti-tumor activity with limited side effects (11).

[^177^Lu]Lu-PSMA-I&T used as an investigational agent has demonstrated promising anti-tumor activity with limited side effects (11).

Target / Indication.full_value: PSMA-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) after progression on hormonal treatment

comment.validator_explanation: ClinicalTrials.gov and Lilly press release confirm PSMA target and mCRPC indication for patients after hormonal treatment progression [1][2]
comment.key_citation:
[V1] Lilly to Acquire POINT Biopharma to Expand Oncology Capabilities into Next-Generation Radioligand Therapies (0.85): "PNT2002
1 is a prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) targeted radioligand therapy in development for patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) after progression on hormonal treatment. ... POINT's active clinical trials include FRONTIER, a phase 1 trial for PNT2004, a pan-cancer program targeting fibroblast activation protein-α (FAP-α), and SPLASH, the phase 3 trial for PNT2002 for people with metastatic castration resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) after second-line hormonal treatment." (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/lilly-to-acquire-point-biopharma-to-expand-oncology-capabilities-into-next-generation-radioligand-therapies-301945243.html)

sources.title: Study Evaluating mCRPC Treatment Using PSMA [Lu-177]-PNT2002 Therapy After Second-line Hormonal Treatment (SPLASH)
sources.snippet: The primary objective of the study is to determine the efficacy of [Lu-177]-PNT2002 ([Lu-177]-PSMA-I&T) versus abiraterone or enzalutamide in delaying radiographic progression in patients with mCRPC. The study consists of 3 phases: Dosimetry, Randomized Treatment, and Long term Follow up. ... Only patients that meet PSMA PET avidity criteria per central review will be eligible for this study.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: - Dec 19, 2023: Positive topline Phase III results...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- Dec 19, 2023: Positive topline Phase III results announced for 177Lu-PNT2002 in the SPLASH trial (NCT04647526), showing median rPFS of 9.5 months vs. 6 months for ARPI (29% risk reduction) [1]
- Oct 2023: Eli Lilly announced acquisition of POINT Biopharma in an all-cash deal valued at nearly $1.4 billion [1]
- Apr 24, 2023: FDA granted fast track designation to 177Lu-PNT2002 for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer [2]

sources.title: Point radiopharma drug results disappoint in prostate ...
sources.snippet:
For Lilly, the SPLASH results may be beside the point.
Executives had downplayed the potential for SPLASH to prove PNT2002 superior to Pluvicto, and indicated their interest in Point had equally as much to do with the company’s manufacturing and research capabilities.
“Our interest in Point is a lot around the platform,” said Lilly’s Chief Scientific and Medical Officer Dan Skovronsky at an investor conference in late November. “I think the time is right to get into therapeutic radiopharmaceuticals.”

sources.title: A phase I study of [ 225 Ac]-FPI-1434 radioimmunotherapy in patients with IGF-1R expressing solid tumors.
sources.snippet: **Background:** Type I insulin-like growth factor receptor (IGF-1R) is a transmembrane protein which is overexpressed in solid tumors including non–small cell lung, prostate, and breast cancers. [ 225Ac]-FPI-1434 is a radioimmunoconjugate consisting of a humanized monoclonal antibody that binds to the external domain of IGF-1R, a proprietary bifunctional chelate, and an alpha-emitting radionuclide actinium-225 (Ac-225), which binds to the external domain of IGF-1R.

sources.title: Definition of actinium Ac 225-FPI-1434 - NCI Drug Dictionary
sources.snippet: A radioconjugate consisting of veligrotug, a humanized monoclonal antibody directed against insulin-like growth factor-1 receptor (IGF-1R) linked, via a bifunctional chelate, to the alpha-emitting radioisotope actinium Ac 225, with potential antineoplastic activity.

sources.title: A phase I study of [<sup>225</sup>Ac]-FPI-1434 radioimmunotherapy in patients with IGF-1R expressing solid tumors.
sources.snippet:

225Ac]-FPI-1434 therapy in patients with IGF-1R expressing solid tumors.

sources.title: A phase I study of [ 225 Ac]-FPI-1434 radioimmunotherapy in patients with IGF-1R expressing solid tumors.
sources.snippet: **Background:** Type I insulin-like growth factor receptor (IGF-1R) is a transmembrane protein which is overexpressed in solid tumors including non–small cell lung, prostate, and breast cancers. [ 225Ac]-FPI-1434 is a radioimmunoconjugate consisting of a humanized monoclonal antibody that binds to the external domain of IGF-1R, a proprietary bifunctional chelate, and an alpha-emitting radionuclide actinium-225 (Ac-225), which binds to the external domain of IGF-1R.

sources.title: Definition of actinium Ac 225-FPI-1434 - NCI Drug Dictionary
sources.snippet: A radioconjugate consisting of veligrotug, a humanized monoclonal antibody directed against insulin-like growth factor-1 receptor (IGF-1R) linked, via a bifunctional chelate, to the alpha-emitting radioisotope actinium Ac 225, with potential antineoplastic activity.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: - May 15, 2024: First patient dosed in multi-dose ...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- May 15, 2024: First patient dosed in multi-dose portion of Phase 1 study evaluating [225Ac]-FPI-1434 in patients with advanced solid tumors [1]
- January 4, 2024: Fusion Pharmaceuticals provided an update on FPI-1434 Phase 1 Cohort 2 data as part of clinical program and manufacturing updates [2]
- June 26, 2023: Fusion Pharmaceuticals presented interim data from Phase 1 trial of FPI-1434 in patients with solid tumors expressing IGF-1R [3]
- February 19, 2020: First patient dosed in Phase 1 clinical trial of [225Ac]-FPI-1434 Injection in patients with advanced solid tumors [4]

comment.original_value:
- Phase I trial ongoing as of 2024
- Orphan Drug designation for multiple cancers
comment.validator_explanation: Updated with specific dated news items from company press releases and announcements, replacing vague statements with concrete events. Original value lacked specific dates and details.

sources.title: Fusion Pharmaceuticals initiates multi-dose portion of Phase 1 ...
sources.snippet: Fusion Pharmaceuticals Inc. (Nasdaq: FUSN), a clinical-stage oncology company focused on developing next-generation radiopharmaceuticals as precision medicines, today announced that the first patient has been dosed in the multi-dose portion of the Phase 1 study evaluating [225Ac]-FPI-1434 (FPI-1434) in patients with advanced solid tumors.

sources.title: Fusion Pharmaceuticals Announces Clinical Program ... - BioSpace
sources.snippet: **Fusion Pharmaceuticals Inc.** (Nasdaq: FUSN), a clinical-stage oncology company focused on developing next-generation radiopharmaceuticals as precision medicines, today announced significant progress with its FPI-2265 development program, an update on FPI-1434 Phase 1 Cohort 2 data and the production of the first clinical doses at the Company's proprietary manufacturing facility.

sources.title: Fusion Pharmaceuticals to Present Interim Data from Phase 1 Trial of FPI-1434 in Patients with Solid Tumors Expressing IGF-1R
sources.snippet: 225Ac]-FPI-1434 (FPI-1434) in patients with solid tumors expressing IGF-1R. ... Fusion's clinical portfolio includes: FPI-2265 targeting prostate specific membrane antigen (PSMA) for metastatic castration resistant prostate cancer currently in a Phase 2 trial with data from 20-30 patients anticipated in the first quarter of 2024; FPI-1434 targeting insulin-like growth factor 1 receptor currently in a Phase 1 trial; and FPI-2059, a small molecule targeting neurotensin receptor 1 (NTSR1), currently in a Phase 1 trial.

sources.title: FPI-1434 Injection in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors
sources.snippet: 225Ac]-FPI-1434 Injection in patients with advanced solid tumors. [ 225Ac]-FPI-1434 is a targeted alpha radioimmunoconjugate that consists of a humanized monoclonal antibody targeting the insulin-like growth factor-1 receptor 1 (IGF-1R), Fusion’s *Fast-Clear*™ Linker, a bifunctional chelate, and actinium-225.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- Sep 19, 2023: RayzeBio announced plans to file an IND application for RYZ801 in the first half of 2024 for patients with hepatocellular carcinoma [1]
- Sep 26, 2023: New trial record created for the Phase 1/1b study of the theranostic pair RYZ811 and RYZ801 [2]
- Dec 11, 2024: RayzeBio website states RYZ801 and its diagnostic pair RYZ811 are being evaluated in a Phase 1/1b trial for GPC3+ unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) [3]
- The original bullet point 'Phase I trial initiated in 2023' is not directly supported by dated sources. The original bullet point 'Part of RayzeBio's targeted alpha therapy pipeline' is confirmed but lacks a specific date.

sources.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Adds Premier Radiopharmaceutical Platform with Acquisition of RayzeBio
sources.snippet: “Acquiring RayzeBio’s differentiated actinium-based radiopharmaceutical platform will establish Bristol Myers Squibb’s presence in one of the most promising and fastest-growing new modalities for the treatment of patients with solid tumors – delivering radioactive payloads to cancer cells in a targeted manner,” said Samit Hirawat, M.D., Executive Vice President, Chief Medical Officer, Drug Development of Bristol Myers Squibb. “In addition, RayzeBio’s platform has the potential to be a significant IND engine, generating several therapeutic candidates in the future by leveraging our global drug development capabilities and infrastructure.”

- Feb 10, 2025: Phase III ProstACT GLOBAL trial design published in Journal of Clinical Oncology (JCO) with 2-part study (dosimetry lead-in n=30, randomized expansion n=490) [1]
- Jan 10, 2024: First patient dosed in Phase III ProstACT GLOBAL study (NCT04876651) evaluating TLX591 with Standard of Care vs SoC alone in PSMA-positive mCRPC [2]
- May 30, 2024: Positive rPFS data from ProstACT SELECT trial reported median radiographic progression-free survival of 8.8 months [3]
- Nov 13, 2023: First patient dosed in Phase III ProstACT GLOBAL study announced [4]

sources.title: First Patient Dosed in Phase III ProstACT GLOBAL Study of ...
sources.snippet: ProstACT GLOBAL (ClinicalTrials.gov ID: NCT04876651) is the first Phase III trial to evaluate TLX591 in adult patients with PSMA-positive metastatic castrate-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) administered together with Standard of Care (SoC, androgen receptor inhibition or taxanes) versus SoC alone.

sources.title: Telix Announces Positive rPFS Data from ProstACT SELECT Trial of TLX591 rADC Therapy Candidate in Prostate Cancer
sources.snippet: The ProstACT SELECT trial, a radiogenomics study, has reported a median radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) of 8.8 months for TLX591, which represents an encouraging signal of potential efficacy in this patient population.

sources.title: Telix Pharmaceuticals and Global Medical Solutions Complete ...
sources.snippet:
Telix is pleased to announce that it has completed a strategic manufacturing agreement with Global Medical Solutions, Ltd. (GMS), a global manufacturer and distributor of diagnostic and therapeutic radiopharmaceuticals, to manufacture and supply finished unit doses of Telix’s Molecularly Targeted Radiation (MTR) products for certain clinical development programs.
Under the terms of the agreement, GMS will initially manufacture and deliver finished unit doses of Telix’s investigational prostate cancer therapy products TLX591 (
177Lu-rosopatamab) and TLX592 to sites in Australia for Telix’s planned ProstACT Phase III and CUPID Phase I clinical trials, respectively.
The agreement is structured to support Telix’s manufacturing needs for further products and incorporates potential future product distribution requirements in the Asia-Pacific region.

sources.title: SAR-bisPSMA - Next-Generation, Highly Targeted Prostate Cancer ...
sources.snippet:
SAR-bisPSMA is a next generation theranostic radiopharmaceutical with dual PSMA-targeting agents linked to Clarity’s SAR chelator technology. It utilises the isotopes of copper for imaging (
64Cu or Cu-64) and therapy ( 67Cu or Cu-67). ... PSMA is a protein expressed in >87% of patients with prostate cancer and is an excellent diagnostic and therapeutic target. Clarity is running a therapy program in metastatic castrate resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) with
64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA and 67Cu-SAR-bisPSMA and multiple diagnostic trials in line with advice received from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to address the two relevant patient populations for registration of diagnostic 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA:
- pre-prostatectomy/pre-definitive treatment of patients with confirmed prostate cancer; and
- patients with biochemical recurrence (BCR) of prostate cancer.

sources.title: Clarity receives FDA Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA
sources.snippet: 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA includes trials in two indications: prostate cancer patients prior to undergoing radical prostatectomy, and with biochemical recurrence (BCR) of their disease.

sources.title: Definition of copper Cu 67 SAR-bisPSMA
sources.snippet: A radioconjugate composed of two human prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA)-binding motifs linked to a sarcophagine (SAR)-based chelator and radiolabeled with the beta-emitting radioisotope copper Cu 67, with potential antineoplastic activity.

sources.title: 67Cu-SAR-bisPSMA - Next-Generation Therapy Clarity Theranostic
sources.snippet:
**Theranostic SECuRE trial**
67Cu-SAR-bisPSMA is currently being studied in the SECuRE trial for patients with metastatic castrate resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) ... SECuRE is a US-based Phase I/IIa theranostic trial for identification and treatment of an advanced form of prostate cancer, mCRPC.
It is a multi-centre, single arm, dose escalation study with a cohort expansion (NCT 04868604).

sources.title: Clarity and PSI Kick Off SAR-bisPSMA Phase III - PSI CRO
sources.snippet:
**PSI CRO, 26 October 2023 — **Clarity Pharmaceuticals (ASX: CU6) (“Clarity”), a clinical stage radiopharmaceutical company with a mission to develop next-generation products that improve treatment outcomes for children and adults with cancer, and PSI CRO AG (“PSI”), a global contract research organisation committed to on-time enrollment in radiopharmaceutical clinical trials, have entered into an agreement and have commenced work towards Clarity’s Phase III diagnostic trial of SAR-bisPSMA in prostate cancer participants, CLARIFY (NCT06056830) 1. ... **About SAR-bisPSMA: **SAR-bisPSMA derives its name from the word “bis”, which reflects a novel approach of connecting two PSMA-targeting agents to Clarity’s proprietary sarcophagine (SAR) technology that securely holds copper isotopes inside a cage-like structure, called a chelator.
Unlike other commercially available chelators, the SAR technology prevents copper leakage into the body.

sources.title: FDA Grants Fast Track Designation to 67Cu-SAR-bisPSMA ...
sources.snippet:
SECuRE, a multicenter, single-arm, nonrandomized, open-label, phase 1/2a study, is evaluating
67Cu-SAR-bisPSMA for the treatment of patients aged 18 years or older with mCRPC with documented disease progression. 2 Patients must have received previous treatment with androgen deprivation therapy and at least 1 ARPI, have a positive 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA PET/CT scan, an ECOG performance status of 0 to 2, a life expectancy of more than 6 months, adequate organ function, and at least 1 metastatic lesion.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: - Jan 24, 2025: Received second FDA Fast Track Des...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- Jan 24, 2025: Received second FDA Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA for PET imaging of PSMA-positive lesions in patients with biochemical recurrence (BCR) of prostate cancer [1]. This enables accelerated development of the comprehensive diagnostic program [1].
- Aug 22, 2024: Received first FDA Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA for PET imaging of PSMA-positive prostate cancer lesions with suspected metastasis in candidates for initial definitive therapy [2]. The designation provides development advantages including more frequent FDA communication and rolling submission capability [2].
- Ongoing: Recruitment continues for the registrational Phase III CLARIFY trial (NCT06056830) evaluating 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA in high-risk prostate cancer patients prior to radical prostatectomy [2]. The trial involves PSI CRO as a contract research organization partner for site identification and trial execution [3].
- Ongoing: Phase I/II COBRA trial demonstrated 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA was safe and highly effective in detecting prostate cancer lesions in patients with BCR, identifying lesions as small as 2 mm [1]. Next-day imaging showed localized disease in up to 80% of participants [1].

comment.key_citation: [V1] Clarity receives U.S. FDA Fast Track Designation for Cu-64 SAR-bisPSMA in biochemical recurrence of prostate cancer (0.95): "Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted Fast Track Designation (FTD) for 64 Cu-SAR-bisPSMA for positron emission tomography (PET) imaging of prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) positive prostate cancer lesions in patients with biochemical recurrence (BCR) of prostate cancer following definitive therapy. ... These 2 FTDs enable the Company to accelerate the development of its comprehensive diagnostic program with this product. ... The COBRA trial results showed great diagnostic performance in the BCR setting, with lesions identified by 64 Cu-SAR-bisPSMA in the 2-mm range and visualised many months before SOC PSMA PET agents are able detect them. ... Advantages have been shown with same-day and next-day imaging, however, the standout was next-day 64 Cu-SAR-bisPSMA PET imaging, showing localised disease in up to 80% of participants and detecting lesions as small as 2 mm." (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/clarity-receives-us-fda-fast-track-designation-for-cu-64-sar-bispsma-in-biochemical-recurrence-of-prostate-cancer-302359424.html)

sources.snippet: Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted Fast Track Designation (FTD) for 64 Cu-SAR-bisPSMA for positron emission tomography (PET) imaging of prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) positive prostate cancer lesions in patients with biochemical recurrence (BCR) of prostate cancer following definitive therapy. ... These 2 FTDs enable the Company to accelerate the development of its comprehensive diagnostic program with this product. ... The COBRA trial results showed great diagnostic performance in the BCR setting, with lesions identified by 64 Cu-SAR-bisPSMA in the 2-mm range and visualised many months before SOC PSMA PET agents are able detect them. ... Advantages have been shown with same-day and next-day imaging, however, the standout was next-day 64 Cu-SAR-bisPSMA PET imaging, showing localised disease in up to 80% of participants and detecting lesions as small as 2 mm.

sources.title: Clarity receives FDA Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA
sources.snippet:
Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA for positron emission tomography (PET) imaging of prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) positive prostate cancer lesions with suspected metastasis who are candidates for initial definitive therapy. ... 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA, it provides a number of product development advantages.
The designation paves the way for a potentially faster review process once Clarity submits its product approval application.
Additionally, it enables more frequent communication with the FDA, allowing for rapid resolution of queries during development.
Furthermore, Clarity can submit completed sections of its application as they are ready, rather than waiting for the entire package to be finished before it can be lodged with the FDA.
These benefits would reduce the review time needed to bring this innovative prostate cancer imaging agent to market, potentially improving diagnosis and treatment planning for patients sooner. ... Driven by the compelling findings from the PROPELLER study, Clarity commenced a registrational Phase III trial in this patient population, CLARIFY, where recruitment is ongoing. ... **Clarity’s Executive Chairperson, Dr Alan Taylor, commented,** “Receiving Fast Track Designation for 64Cu-SAR-bisPSMA is a significant milestone, especially as we are actively recruiting into our first registrational Phase III trial, CLARIFY, and preparing for an End of Phase meeting with the FDA for a second pivotal Phase III trial with this product.

sources.title: Clarity and PSI Kick Off SAR-bisPSMA Phase III - PSI CRO
sources.snippet:
**PSI’s Senior Director of Operations, Rhonda Critchlow, commented**, “Using our global database of over 400 radiopharmaceutical sites, we will be able to identify sites with the best resources and capabilities for the CLARIFY trial.
We are excited to begin our collaboration with Clarity and will focus on the startup of high-performing sites to achieve the first patient in, in the shortest time possible.

sources.title: PET imaging of tumours with a 64Cu labeled macrobicyclic ...
sources.snippet: Here we report the design and synthesis of a new bifunctional chelator, 5-(8-methyl-3,6,10,13,16,19-hexaaza-bicyclo[6.6.6]icosan-1-ylamino)-5-oxopentanoic acid (MeCOSar), that forms copper complexes of exceptional stability by virtue of a cage amine (sarcophagine) ligand and a new conjugate referred to as SarTATE, obtained by the conjugation of MeCOSar to the tumour-targeting peptide Tyr 3-octreotate. Radiolabeling of SarTATE with 64Cu II, a radioisotope suitable for positron emission tomography (PET), was fast (∼20 min), easily performed at room temperature and consistently resulted in high radiochemical purity (>99%). *In vitro* and *in vivo* evaluation of 64CuSarTATE demonstrated its high selectivity for tumour cells expressing somatostatin receptor 2 (sstr2).

• Dec 22, 2025: Clarity announced commencement of pivotal Phase III registrational trial of Cu-64 SARTATE diagnostic agent in NETs following successful End of Phase meeting with FDA [1]
• Jun 5, 2025: Phase II DISCO trial results showed Cu-64 SARTATE demonstrated superior lesion detection compared to standard-of-care Ga-68 DOTATATE, with enhanced liver lesion detection [1]
• Nov 3, 2020: First patient treated in Cu-64/67 SARTATE theranostic trial for pediatric neuroblastoma at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center [2]
• 2020: Granted FDA Orphan Drug Designation and two Rare Pediatric Disease Designations for diagnostic and therapeutic applications in neuroblastoma, making Clarity eligible for two Priority Review Vouchers upon approval [2]

comment.validator_explanation: Updated with recent Phase III trial announcement from Dec 2025 and DISCO trial results from Jun 2025. Original value was accurate but outdated - Phase I/II neuroblastoma trial started in 2020, and significant progress has been made with Phase III NET diagnostic trial now commencing. All dates verified from high-authority Clarity Pharmaceuticals sources.

sources.title: Positive guidance from the US FDA on Cu-64 SARTATE Phase III
sources.snippet: Clarity Pharmaceuticals announced it will commence a pivotal Phase III registrational trial of its 64Cu-SARTATE diagnostic agent in patients with neuroendocrine tumours (NETs). ... In the DISCO trial, 64Cu-SARTATE was found to be safe and well tolerated with lesion detection substantially higher than the current standard-of-care, 68Ga-DOTATATE. 64Cu-SARTATE also showed enhanced lesion detection in the liver, the most common metastatic site for patients with gastroenteropancreatic NETs. ... This follows a successful End of Phase meeting with the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA), in which all key components of the proposed trial design were agreed upon.

sources.title: Patient treatments commence with Clarity’s copper-64/copper-67 SARTATE in neuroblastoma clinical trial - Clarity Pharmaceuticals - Radiopharmaceutical Therapies and Imaging
sources.snippet:
**Sydney, Australia 3 November 2020** – Clarity Pharmaceuticals, a clinical stage radiopharmaceutical company focused on the treatment of serious disease, is pleased to announce that treatment has commenced in a 64/67Cu-SARTATE TM theranostic trial of paediatric patients with neuroblastoma at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center (MSK) in New York City. ... We have also recently been granted Orphan Drug Designation as well as two Rare Paediatric Disease Designations for the diagnostic and therapeutic application of SARTATE™ in neuroblastoma by the U.S.
Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

This research study is designed to explore if a new radiotherapeutic (radioactive) drug, named 212Pb (212-lead) VMT-α-NET, works against neuroendocrine tumor cells.

Followdisease, and in collaboration with PharmaLogic, a world-class contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) specializing in radiopharmaceuticals, that it has initiated dosing in a Phase I study evaluating the pharmacokinetics, biodistribution and radiation dosimetry of a novel fibroblast activation protein-alpha (FAP)-targeted radiopharmaceutical, Copper-64[Cu-64]-labeled RTX-1363S, for PET imaging in adult healthy volunteers." (https://ratiotx.com/press-release-phase-i-novel-fap-targeted-radiopharmaceutical/)

comment.key_citation: [V1] Ratio Therapeutics has Successfully Dosed the First Cohort in its Phase 1/2 Study Evaluating a Novel FAP-Targeted Radiopharmaceutical in Patients with Late-Stage Aggressive Sarcomas (0.85): "BOSTON, Dec. 16, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, announced today that dosing of the first cohort has been completed in the ATLAS trial, a Phase 1/2 open-label study evaluating the safety, tolerability, and efficacy of the company's lead therapeutic radiopharmaceutical, [Ac-225]-RTX-2358, targeting fibroblast activation protein‑α (FAP) in patients with relapsed or refractory soft tissue sarcomas." (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/ratio-therapeutics-has-successfully-dosed-the-first-cohort-in-its-phase-12-study-evaluating-a-novel-fap-targeted-radiopharmaceutical-in-patients-with-late-stage-aggressive-sarcomas-302642494.html)

sources.title: November 18th, 2024: Ratio Enters License and ...
sources.snippet: **BOSTON, November 18, 2024** /PRNewswire/ — Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, entered today into an exclusive worldwide license and collaboration agreement with Novartis Pharma AG, a subsidiary of Novartis AG (NYSE: NVS). The collaboration leverages Ratio’s radioligand therapy discovery and development expertise as well as its technology platforms for the development of a Somatostatin Receptor 2 (SSTR2) radiotherapeutic candidate for cancer. ... Ratio will collaborate with Novartis to drive preclinical activities to research and select an SSTR2-targeting development candidate.

comment.validator_explanation: Press releases confirm the candidate is an SSTR2-targeting radiotherapeutic, but no specific code name (e.g., RTX-XXX) is provided; the collaboration is to research and select a development candidate [2]
comment.qc_reasoning: Added '(Novartis partnered)' for clarity while preserving validator's accurate description

sources.title: November 18th, 2024: Ratio Enters License and ...
sources.snippet: **BOSTON, November 18, 2024** /PRNewswire/ — Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, entered today into an exclusive worldwide license and collaboration agreement with Novartis Pharma AG, a subsidiary of Novartis AG (NYSE: NVS). The collaboration leverages Ratio’s radioligand therapy discovery and development expertise as well as its technology platforms for the development of a Somatostatin Receptor 2 (SSTR2) radiotherapeutic candidate for cancer. ... Ratio will collaborate with Novartis to drive preclinical activities to research and select an SSTR2-targeting development candidate.

Target / Indication.full_value: Somatostatin Receptor 2 (SSTR2) in cancer

comment.original_value: Somatostatin Receptor 2 in neuroendocrine and other SSTR2-expressing tumors
comment.validator_explanation: Authoritative source confirms SSTR2-targeting radiotherapeutic for cancer treatment [1]. SSTR2 is overexpressed in various cancers [1].
comment.key_citation: [V1] Novartis strengthens radiopharma leadership with Ratio ... (0.85): "Novartis is bolstering its leadership in the radiopharmaceutical space by partnering with US-based startup Ratio Therapeutics to develop a somatostatin receptor 2 (SSTR2) radiotherapeutic for cancer treatment. ... The collaboration focuses on preclinical research to identify an SSTR2-targeting development candidate, after which Novartis will take the lead on development, manufacturing, and commercialisation. ... A protein overexpressed in various cancers, SSTR2 is a key target for therapies due to its role in enabling precise and targeted treatment." (https://www.pharmaceutical-technology.com/news/novartis-strengthens-radiopharma-leadership-with-ratio-therapeutics-deal/)

sources.title: Novartis strengthens radiopharma leadership with Ratio ...
sources.snippet: Novartis is bolstering its leadership in the radiopharmaceutical space by partnering with US-based startup Ratio Therapeutics to develop a somatostatin receptor 2 (SSTR2) radiotherapeutic for cancer treatment. ... The collaboration focuses on preclinical research to identify an SSTR2-targeting development candidate, after which Novartis will take the lead on development, manufacturing, and commercialisation. ... A protein overexpressed in various cancers, SSTR2 is a key target for therapies due to its role in enabling precise and targeted treatment.

sources.title: Our Technology
sources.snippet: Ratio’s Trillium™ technology is a proprietary and innovative platform that incorporates a tunable, structural motif enabling our compounds to reversibly bind to albumin, thereby modulating their pharmacokinetics (PK) and improving drug availability, tumor delivery, and tumor loading, while simultaneously reducing the side effects commonly observed with other radiotherapies. ... Ratio’s Macropa™ technology reflects a proprietary chelator that is used to attach the alpha emitting radionuclide actinium-225 to Trillium™ and other compounds. Its unique chemistry enables ease of manufacture and improves in vivo stability of the resulting compound.

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics Partners with Macrocyclics for Exclusive Manufacturing and Distribution of its Radiopharmaceutical Chelator Platform
sources.snippet: Macropa is a proprietary, best-in-class, bifunctional chelator for Actinium-225 (Ac-225), known for its strong alpha-particle emissions and its use in targeted alpha therapy (TAT) for the treatment of cancer. ... Ratio's proprietary R&D platforms, Trillium™ and Macropa™, enable the development of fit-for-purpose radiopharmaceuticals for therapy and imaging that possess pharmacokinetic modulation, thereby improving drug availability, tumor delivery, and tumor loading.

sources.title: Novartis begins construction of two new radioligand therapy facilities ...

• Nov 13, 2025: Enrollment started for the third cohort of the Phase 1 dose escalation trial at 90 mCi, following DSMC approval [1].
• Nov 12, 2025: Completion of enrollment for the second cohort of patients in the Phase 1 trial [1].
• May 12, 2025: DSMC approved proceeding to the next dose (60 mCi) after reviewing positive safety and pharmacokinetic data from the first cohort (30 mCi) [2].
• Dec 19, 2024: Clinical trial status listed as 'Recruiting' [3].
