sources.title: Radiopharm Theranostics Accelerates 177Lu-RAD204 ...
sources.snippet: of high unmet medical need, announced today that it has achieved a key milestone in its ongoing clinical development program for its clinical-stage radiotherapeutic asset, 177Lu-RAD204, as the Data and Safety Monitoring Committee (DSMC) has approved to proceed to the next dose in its Phase 1 clinical trial in patients with PD-L1 positive advanced cancers ... comment.key_citation: [V1] Radiopharm Theranostics Doses First Patient in Phase 1 ‘HEAT’ Trial of 177Lu-RAD202 for Treatment of Advanced HER2-Positive Solid Tumors (0.85): "*Phase 1* *1* * First-In-Human study designed to assess safety* *, * *tolerability* *, right dose for Phase 2 and early signs of efficacy* * of 177Lu-RAD20* *2* * in individuals with * *advanced HER2-positive solid * *tumors* ... The open-label Phase 1 “HEAT’ clinical trial is a dose escalation trial of 177Lu-RAD202 that is designed to determine the recommended Phase 2 dose and to evaluate the safety and preliminary clinical activity of this novel radiotherapeutic in individuals with HER2-expressing advanced cancers. ... RAD202 is a proprietary single domain antibody that targets HER2. sources.title: NanoMab Technology Limited signs licensing agreements with Radiopharm Theranostics and Full Life Technologies (HK) Ltd.
sources.snippet:
th, 2022 – **NanoMab Technology Limited**, a privately held biopharmaceutical company focussing on cancer precision therapies is delighted to announce that over the course of the last two quarters it has taken the next steps forward in its commitment to developing next generation pharmaceuticals by forming exclusive licensing agreements with Radiopharm Theranostics (ASX: RAD) and Full Life Technologies (HK) Ltd.
The agreements grant RAD full rights to develop three of NanoMab’s single-domain antibody assets: NM-02, NM-03, and NM-04; respectively targeting HER-2 (breast cancer), TROP-2 (triple negative breast cancer), and PTK7 (multiple solid tumours) biomarkers.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Nov 17-19, 2025: Phase 2b trial reaches 50% enro...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Nov 17-19, 2025: Phase 2b trial reaches 50% enrollment milestone (15 of 30 patients) with topline data expected H1 2026 [1][2]
• Sep 2, 2025: Strategic collaboration announced with BAMF Health for manufacturing and dosing of 18F-RAD101 for Phase 2b study [3]
• Jun 11-19, 2025: FDA grants Fast Track designation for brain metastases imaging [4]
• Apr 28-29, 2025: First patient dosed in U.S. Phase 2b imaging study [5][6]
• Jan 29, 2025: Successful detection of brain metastases demonstrated in 22-patient study using RAD101 [7]
• Active, recruiting (NCT06777433) [8]

comment.validator_explanation: Found comprehensive recent news from 2025 with specific dates and milestones including enrollment updates, FDA designation, first patient dosing, and manufacturing partnership. All sourced from high-confidence press releases and company announcements.

sources.title: Radiopharm Theranostics Announces 50% Enrollment in Phase 2b ...
sources.snippet: “Enrolling 50% of the patients in our Phase 2b study of RAD101 is a meaningful achievement for our RAD101 development program that brings us closer to demonstrating its clinical benefit in distinguishing between tumor recurrence and radiation necrosis in patients with brain metastasis following anticancer treatments, like SRS/radiation,” said Riccardo Canevari, CEO and Managing Director of Radiopharm Theranostics. “Meeting this half-way milestone, along with our continuing momentum in patient enrollment, gives us further confidence in our plans to complete enrollment and share topline results in the first half of 2026.”

sources.title: Radiopharm Theranostics Announces 50% Enrollment in Phase 2b Clinical Trial of RAD101 Imaging in Brain Metastases

'Meeting this half-way milestone, along with our continuing momentum in patient enrollment, gives us further confidence in our plans to complete enrollment and share topline results in the first half of 2026.'

sources.title: RAD
sources.snippet:

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: - Jan 19, 2024: Novartis presents Phase III NETTER...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- Jan 19, 2024: Novartis presents Phase III NETTER-2 trial results at ASCO GI showing Lutathera plus octreotide LAR reduced risk of disease progression or death by 72% as first-line therapy in patients with SSTR+ grade 2/3 advanced GEP-NETs versus high-dose octreotide LAR alone [1]. Median PFS was 22.8 months vs 8.5 months (HR 0.28) [1], and objective response rate was 43% vs 9.3% [1].
- Sep 25, 2023: Novartis announces NETTER-2 trial met primary endpoint of improvement in PFS and key secondary endpoint of ORR in first-line treatment of grade 2/3 advanced GEP-NETs [2], marking Lutathera as the first radioligand therapy to demonstrate clinically meaningful benefit in a first-line setting [2].
- Jan 22, 2024: NETRF reports NETTER-2 trial shows Lutathera as first-line treatment significantly reduces disease progression or death, with median PFS extended to 22.8 months vs 8.5 months [3], representing the first positive Phase 3 study for a radioligand therapy in a first-line setting [3].
- Oct 20, 2025: FDA label update confirms Lutathera indication for adult and pediatric patients 12 years and older with SSTR+ GEP-NETs [4], with recommended dosage of 7.4 GBq every 8 weeks for 4 doses [4].

comment.original_value: -  Primary analysis of NETTER-2 indicates it could be considered a new standard of care in first-line therapy (2025)
comment.validator_explanation: Updated with multiple recent news items from authoritative sources including Novartis press releases, NETRF reports, and FDA labeling. Original value had only one bullet point with 2025 date; expanded with specific dates and details from 2023-2025.

sources.snippet:
Although the experience of [^177^Lu]Lu-DOTATATE is more limited in BCs than GEP-NET, off-label use can be considered with promising results [30,31].
A prospective phase II trial in 34 patients with stage IV BCs treated with cumulative activity of 18.5–27.8 GBq in four or five cycles of [^177^Lu]Lu-DOTATATE documented a DCR of 80% (6% complete response, 27% partial response, and 47% stable disease) and a median PFS of 20 months [32].
In this study, negative prognostic factors were AC histology, tumor thyroid transcription factor-1 (TTF-1) expression, and a positive [^18^F]FDG PET imaging.
Comparable results (median PFS of 17 months) were reported in a group of patients with diffuse extrahepatic metastases treated with RLT after several lines of therapies [33]. ... A DCR equal to 86% was reached, including five patients with ORR (36%).
Of note, 62% of symptomatic patients required a dose reduction of antihypertensive medications, and 57% reported a subjective therapeutic benefit in terms of tumor-related symptoms.
Comprehensive PFS was 39 months, including two patients with early recurrence (2 and 5 months post-RLT, respectively) and one patient with remarkable tumor downsizing that allowed a second-step curative liver surgery.
...
According to the authors, this result may be related both to DOTATATE’s higher affinity for SSTR2 (which is the type most overexpressed in PPGLs) and to the longer half-life of ^177^Lu and, consequently, prolonged residence time within the tumor lesions.
Moreover, this study reports that sympathetic functioning PPGLs were associated to a shorter median PFS compared to non-functioning PPGLs. ... Overexpression of SSTR2 potentially allows RLT with [^177^Lu]Lu-DOTATATE, although this treatment is not currently approved for meningiomas due to the lack of large-scale randomized trials [66].
Nevertheless, in the future, RLT could hopefully carve out a role in recurrent or in unresectable meningiomas after other standard therapies have failed.
...
Several studies confirmed the poor outcome for high grade meningiomas treated with RLT [68,71].
Conversely, Minczeles et al. [72] documented both a decline in tumor growth rate and a relatively good disease control in 15 patients who received [^177^Lu]Lu-DOTATATE, with low systemic toxicity.
Probably, baseline SSTR2 expression may play a key role in the correct selection of patients with aggressive meningiomas to address to RLT.
An increase in treatment efficacy may be achieved via intra-arterial RLT injection, providing a significant tracer accumulation and promising improvement for the salvage treatment of meningioma patients [73,74].
Moreover, aggressive meningiomas may benefit of the combination of RLT with external beam radiotherapy [75,76], although further studies are necessary to determine its efficacy and survival improvements.

Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.display_value: Regulatory submission for first-line GEP-NET indic...
Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.full_value: Regulatory submission for first-line GEP-NET indication based on NETTER-2 data expected in 2024-2025; further expansion into additional NET subtypes (bronchial carcinoids, pheochromocytoma/paraganglioma, meningioma) and combination therapies under investigation [5]. No signs of abandonment - Novartis continues to expand manufacturing capabilities with new facilities in Indianapolis (awaiting FDA approval), Millburn (US), Zaragoza (Spain), Ivrea (Italy), and planned sites in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, China [1][2].

comment.original_value: Further expansion of indications, real-world evidence generation.
comment.validator_explanation: Based on NETTER-2 trial completion and Novartis's stated plans for regulatory submissions. Manufacturing expansion indicates continued commitment. Original value was vague; provided more specific milestone estimate and evidence against abandonment.

sources.title: Novartis radioligand therapy Lutathera® demonstrated ...
sources.snippet: *Phase III NETTER-2 trial met primary endpoint of improvement in progression-free survival (PFS) and key secondary endpoint of objective response rate (ORR) in patients with Grade 2 and 3 advanced gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) who received first line treatment with Lutathera* *®* *in combination with long-acting octreotide, versus high-dose long-acting octreotide alone* *1,* *2* *Lutathera is the first radioligand therapy (RLT) to demonstrate clinically meaningful benefit in a first line ... The company also continues to support the increasing demand for RLTs with expansions at existing manufacturing sites in the U.S. and Europe and a new state-of-the-art facility in Indianapolis, U.S., which is awaiting FDA approval*

sources.title: NETTER-2 Trial Shows Lutathera® as First-Line Treatment ...
sources.snippet: The trial is the first positive Phase 3 study for a radioligand therapy in a first-line setting (i.e., as a first treatment option). ... During the trial, Lutathera plus long-acting octreotide significantly extended median progression-free survival (PFS) of study participants to 22.8 months compared to 8.5 months with octreotide alone.

Key Partnerships/Collaborators.display_value: Novartis (sole developer and manufacturer) - Lutat...
Key Partnerships/Collaborators.full_value: Novartis (sole developer and manufacturer) - Lutathera is developed and manufactured exclusively by Novartis through its global radioligand therapy network including sites in Millburn (US), Zaragoza (Spain), Ivrea (Italy), and new facility in Indianapolis (US) [1]. No external co-development, licensing, or manufacturing partnerships identified for Lutathera specifically, though Novartis collaborates broadly in radiopharmaceutical research.

comment.validator_explanation: No evidence of external partnerships for Lutathera found in searches. Novartis appears to maintain full control over development, manufacturing, and commercialization through its internal capabilities and expanding global network.

comment.validator_explanation: RYZ101 is developed by RayzeBio, not Novartis. Novartis develops Lutathera and Pluvicto, but RYZ101 is a RayzeBio asset [1]
comment.qc_reasoning: Original value 'Novartis' is incorrect. All authoritative sources confirm RYZ101 was developed by RayzeBio, acquired by Bristol Myers Squibb in Feb 2024. No evidence links Novartis to this asset.

sources.title: Bristol Myers' RayzeBio halts radiotherapy trial enrollment after isotope runs scarce
sources.snippet: Thanks to a shortage of the isotope actinium, RayzeBio has temporarily halted new patient enrollment in its ACTION-1 phase 3 study, a RayzeBio spokesperson confirmed over email.

Target / Indication.full_value: Somatostatin receptor (SSTR)-positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) in patients who have progressed after treatment with lutetium-177 (Lu-177) labeled somatostatin analog therapy [1][2]

comment.original_value: SSTR+ NETs (patients progressed after ^177Lu-labeled somatostatin analogs)
comment.validator_explanation: Multiple high-confidence sources confirm the indication: SSTR+ GEP-NETs post-Lu-177 therapy. The current value is accurate but can be slightly expanded for clarity.
comment.key_citation: [V1] Bristol Myers' RayzeBio halts radiotherapy trial enrollment after isotope runs scarce (0.85): "Thanks to a shortage of the isotope actinium, RayzeBio has temporarily halted new patient enrollment in its ACTION-1 phase 3 study, a RayzeBio spokesperson confirmed over email. The trial is assessing the ability of RayzeBio’s lead radiotherapy candidate, RYZ101, to help treat somatostatin receptor (SSTR) expressing gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) in patients who have already received treatment with lutetium-177 based somatostatin therapies. RYZ101 and the rest of RayzeBio’s platform leverages an alpha-emitting isotope called actinium-225 (Ac225)." (https://www.fiercepharma.com/pharma/bms-and-rayzebio-halt-radiotherapy-trial-enrollment-after-isotope-runs-scarce)

sources.title: Bristol Myers' RayzeBio halts radiotherapy trial enrollment after isotope runs scarce
sources.snippet: Thanks to a shortage of the isotope actinium, RayzeBio has temporarily halted new patient enrollment in its ACTION-1 phase 3 study, a RayzeBio spokesperson confirmed over email. The trial is assessing the ability of RayzeBio’s lead radiotherapy candidate, RYZ101, to help treat somatostatin receptor (SSTR) expressing gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) in patients who have already received treatment with lutetium-177 based somatostatin therapies. RYZ101 and the rest of RayzeBio’s platform leverages an alpha-emitting isotope called actinium-225 (Ac225).

sources.title: RayzeBio Announces First Patient Dosed in Phase 3 ACTION-1 ...
sources.snippet: **SAN DIEGO, California, May 31, 2023** – RayzeBio, Inc., a targeted radiopharmaceutical company developing an innovative pipeline against validated solid tumor targets, today announced that the first patient has been dosed in the Phase 3 trial of RYZ101 in patients with SSTR+ gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) that have progressed following prior Lutetium-177 labelled somatostatin analogue therapy. ... RYZ101 is an investigational targeted radiopharmaceutical therapy, designed to deliver a highly potent radioisotope, Actinium-225 (Ac225), to tumors expressing SSTR2. RYZ101 is being evaluated in clinical studies for patients with SSTR+ GEP-NETs who have previously been treated with Lu177-based somatostatin therapies and also in patients with newly diagnosed extensive stage small cell lung cancer.

Diagnostic/Theranostic Agent.full_value: Therapeutic radioligand therapy (Alpha-PRRT). Diagnostic imaging with Gallium-68 DOTATATE PET is used for patient selection.

comment.validator_explanation: The current value 'Theranostic (potential for diagnostic pair)' is imprecise. The product RYZ101 is a therapeutic agent. Clinical trials use the diagnostic agent Ga-68 DOTATATE for patient stratification, but this is a standard-of-care diagnostic, not a co-developed theranostic pair. The evidence shows a functional theranostic *approach* but not a formal paired agent.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator's update is correct direction but overly verbose. Simplified to clarify it's a therapeutic agent using standard-of-care diagnostic for patient selection, not a formal theranostic pair.

sources.title: Bristol Myers' RayzeBio halts radiotherapy trial enrollment after isotope runs scarce
sources.snippet: Thanks to a shortage of the isotope actinium, RayzeBio has temporarily halted new patient enrollment in its ACTION-1 phase 3 study, a RayzeBio spokesperson confirmed over email. The trial is assessing the ability of RayzeBio’s lead radiotherapy candidate, RYZ101, to help treat somatostatin receptor (SSTR) expressing gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) in patients who have already received treatment with lutetium-177 based somatostatin therapies. RYZ101 and the rest of RayzeBio’s platform leverages an alpha-emitting isotope called actinium-225 (Ac225).

sources.title: RayzeBio Announces First Patient Dosed in Phase 3 ACTION-1 ...
sources.snippet: **SAN DIEGO, California, May 31, 2023** – RayzeBio, Inc., a targeted radiopharmaceutical company developing an innovative pipeline against validated solid tumor targets, today announced that the first patient has been dosed in the Phase 3 trial of RYZ101 in patients with SSTR+ gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) that have progressed following prior Lutetium-177 labelled somatostatin analogue therapy. ... RYZ101 is an investigational targeted radiopharmaceutical therapy, designed to deliver a highly potent radioisotope, Actinium-225 (Ac225), to tumors expressing SSTR2. RYZ101 is being evaluated in clinical studies for patients with SSTR+ GEP-NETs who have previously been treated with Lu177-based somatostatin therapies and also in patients with newly diagnosed extensive stage small cell lung cancer.

sources.snippet: Patients receive Ga 68-DOTATATE IV and undergo PET scan or somatostatin receptor (SSTR) PET scan, computed tomography (CT) scan, MRI and undergo blood and urine sample collection and may undergo echocardiography or multigated acquisition (MUGA) scan throughout the study. ... - Positive 68Ga-DOTATATE uptake on PET/CT at baseline, defined as target lesion uptake higher than the background with SUV ratios adjusted to the liver uptake (Krenning score ≥ 2)

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • February 26, 2024: Bristol Myers Squibb complete...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• February 26, 2024: Bristol Myers Squibb completed acquisition of RayzeBio for $4.1 billion ($62.50 per share), making RayzeBio a wholly owned subsidiary of BMS. RYZ101 and the entire radiopharmaceutical platform now belong to Bristol Myers Squibb [1]
• December 26, 2023: Bristol Myers Squibb and RayzeBio announced definitive merger agreement for BMS to acquire RayzeBio for $62.50 per share in cash, total equity value of approximately $4.1 billion [2]
• October 24, 2023: FDA selected RYZ101 (Actinium-225 DOTATATE) into the inaugural Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls (CMC) Development and Readiness Pilot (CDRP) program to expedite commercial manufacturing readiness for RYZ101 in GEP-NETs [3]
• May 31, 2023: First patient dosed in Phase 3 ACTION-1 trial evaluating RYZ101 in patients with SSTR+ gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) that have progressed following prior Lutetium-177 labelled somatostatin analogue therapy. The global Phase 3 trial is expected to enroll 210 patients randomized 1:1 between RYZ101 and investigator's choice of standard of care (everolimus, sunitinib, or high-dose long-acting SSAs) with progression-free survival as the primary endpoint [4]
• September 13, 2022: RayzeBio announced advancement of RYZ101 into clinical development for two cancer indications, including enrollment in the Phase 1b portion of the ACTION-1 Phase 1b/3 trial (NCT05477576) for SSTR+ GEP-NETs and IND clearance from FDA for first-line treatment of patients with SSTR+ extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) in combination with standard of care [5]

comment.original_value: -  First part of study to determine safety, PK, and RP3D (ongoing)
comment.validator_explanation: Original value was severely outdated and incomplete, lacking the critical acquisition by Bristol Myers Squibb in February 2024. Updated with comprehensive, dated news from authoritative company press releases showing: (1) BMS acquisition completion Feb 2024, (2) merger announcement Dec 2023, (3) FDA CDRP program selection Oct 2023, (4) Phase 3 trial initiation May 2023, and (5) Phase 1b advancement Sep 2022. All dates verified from high-reliability primary sources (p=0.95).
comment.key_citation:
[V1] Bristol Myers Squibb Completes Acquisition of RayzeBio, Adding Differentiated Actinium-Based Radiopharmaceutical Platform (0.95): "Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE: BMY) announced today that it has successfully completed its acquisition of RayzeBio, Inc. (NASDAQ: RYZB).
With the completion of the acquisition, RayzeBio shares have ceased trading on the NASDAQ Global Market and RayzeBio is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Bristol Myers Squibb. ... A Phase 3 clinical trial is currently enrolling patients to evaluate RYZ101 in patients with SSTR-positive GEP-NETs who have previously been treated with lutetium-177 based somatostatin therapies. ... A Phase 1b clinical trial is also currently enrolling patients to evaluate RYZ101 as a first-line treatment of ES-SCLC in combination with standard-of-care therapy. The platform has the potential to be a significant IND engine to generate a number of candidates and comes with a state-of-the-art RPT manufacturing facility, which is expected to begin operating in the first half of 2024. Bristol Myers Squibb’s previously announced tender offer to acquire all of the outstanding shares of RayzeBio common stock for a purchase price of $62.50 per share in cash, or approximately $4.1 billion, expired at one minute after 11:59 p.m., Eastern Time on February 22, 2024." (https://news.bms.com/news/details/2024/Bristol-Myers-Squibb-Completes-Acquisition-of-RayzeBio-Adding-Differentiated-Actinium-Based-Radiopharmaceutical-Platform/default.aspx)

sources.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Completes Acquisition of RayzeBio, Adding Differentiated Actinium-Based Radiopharmaceutical Platform
sources.snippet:
Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE: BMY) announced today that it has successfully completed its acquisition of RayzeBio, Inc. (NASDAQ: RYZB).
With the completion of the acquisition, RayzeBio shares have ceased trading on the NASDAQ Global Market and RayzeBio is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Bristol Myers Squibb. ... A Phase 3 clinical trial is currently enrolling patients to evaluate RYZ101 in patients with SSTR-positive GEP-NETs who have previously been treated with lutetium-177 based somatostatin therapies. ... A Phase 1b clinical trial is also currently enrolling patients to evaluate RYZ101 as a first-line treatment of ES-SCLC in combination with standard-of-care therapy. The platform has the potential to be a significant IND engine to generate a number of candidates and comes with a state-of-the-art RPT manufacturing facility, which is expected to begin operating in the first half of 2024. Bristol Myers Squibb’s previously announced tender offer to acquire all of the outstanding shares of RayzeBio common stock for a purchase price of $62.50 per share in cash, or approximately $4.1 billion, expired at one minute after 11:59 p.m., Eastern Time on February 22, 2024.

sources.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Adds Premier Radiopharmaceutical Platform with Acquisition of RayzeBio
sources.snippet: Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE: BMY) and RayzeBio, Inc. (NASDAQ: RYZB) today announced a definitive merger agreement under which Bristol Myers Squibb will acquire RayzeBio for $62.50 per share in cash, for a total equity value of approximately $4.1 billion, or $3.6 billion net of estimated cash acquired. ... RayzeBio is completing construction of a state-of-the-art in-house manufacturing facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, and GMP drug production is expected to begin in the first half of 2024.

sources.title: FDA selects RayzeBio to Participate in the Inaugural CDRP Program to Expedite Commercial Manufacturing Readiness for RYZ101 in GEP-NETs
sources.snippet: SAN DIEGO, Oct. 24, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- RayzeBio, Inc. (Nasdaq: RYZB), a targeted radiopharmaceutical company developing an innovative pipeline against validated solid tumor targets, today announced the FDA has selected RYZ101 (Actinium-225 DOTATATE) into the inaugural Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls (CMC) Development and Readiness Pilot (CDRP) program.

sources.title: RayzeBio Announces First Patient Dosed in Phase 3 ACTION-1 ...
sources.snippet:
**SAN DIEGO, California, May 31, 2023** – RayzeBio, Inc., a targeted radiopharmaceutical company developing an innovative pipeline against validated solid tumor targets, today announced that the first patient has been dosed in the Phase 3 trial of RYZ101 in patients with SSTR+ gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) that have progressed following prior Lutetium-177 labelled somatostatin analogue therapy. ... The Phase 3 trial is a global study and expected to enroll 210 patients, randomized 1:1 between RYZ101 and investigator’s choice of standard of care (SOC), which includes everolimus, sunitinib, or high-dose long-acting SSAs. The primary endpoint for the trial is progression free survival (PFS). ... Ac225 for the study was provided by multiple sources including the U.S.
Department of Energy Isotope Program.

sources.title: RayzeBio Advances RYZ101, a Targeted Radiopharmaceutical ...
sources.snippet:
- The Company is enrolling patients in the Phase 1b portion of the ACTION-1 Phase 1b/3 trial of RYZ101 (NCT05477576).
Safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of RYZ101 will be evaluated in patients SSTR+ gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) who have progressed on Lu-177 labelled somatostatin analogue therapy. ... - Additionally, RayzeBio received Investigational New Drug Application (IND) clearance from the U.S.
Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to investigate RYZ101 for first-line treatment of patients with SSTR+ extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) in combination with standard of care.

Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.display_value: Phase 3 ACTION-1 trial enrollment completion and p...
Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.full_value: Phase 3 ACTION-1 trial enrollment completion and progression-free survival (PFS) results expected (trial actively enrolling as of May 2023 with target of 210 patients) [1][4]. Additional Phase 1b trial results for RYZ101 in combination with standard-of-care therapy for first-line treatment of ES-SCLC (trial actively enrolling) [1]. Manufacturing facility in Indianapolis expected to begin GMP drug production in first half of 2024 [2]. No signs of abandonment - multiple active clinical trials across different indications including GEP-NETs, ER+/HER2-negative breast cancer expressing SSTRs, ES-SCLC, and progressive/recurrent intracranial meningioma [6]. Bristol Myers Squibb's $4.1 billion acquisition in February 2024 demonstrates strong commitment to the program [1].

comment.original_value: Completion of Phase 1b, initiation of Phase 3 comparison with standard of care.

Updated with comprehensive milestone information including: (1) Phase 3 enrollment completion and PFS results as next major milestone, (2) Phase 1b ES-SCLC trial ongoing, (3) manufacturing facility production start H1 2024, (4) multiple active trials across indications, and (5) BMS acquisition as strong evidence against abandonment. All claims verified from high-reliability sources.

Note: The table context lists 'Company Name: Novartis' but all authoritative sources confirm RayzeBio (now Bristol Myers Squibb) as the developer. No evidence of Novartis involvement in RYZ101 development was found; Novartis has separate radiopharmaceutical collaborations with other companies like Ratio Therapeutics for different SSTR2-targeting candidates [7].

Bristol Myers Squibb’s previously announced tender offer to acquire all of the outstanding shares of RayzeBio common stock for a purchase price of $62.50 per share in cash, or approximately $4.1 billion, expired at one minute after 11:59 p.m., Eastern Time on February 22, 2024." (https://news.bms.com/news/details/2024/Bristol-Myers-Squibb-Completes-Acquisition-of-RayzeBio-Adding-Differentiated-Actinium-Based-Radiopharmaceutical-Platform/default.aspx)

sources.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Completes Acquisition of RayzeBio, Adding Differentiated Actinium-Based Radiopharmaceutical Platform
sources.snippet:
Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE: BMY) announced today that it has successfully completed its acquisition of RayzeBio, Inc. (NASDAQ: RYZB).
With the completion of the acquisition, RayzeBio shares have ceased trading on the NASDAQ Global Market and RayzeBio is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Bristol Myers Squibb. ... A Phase 3 clinical trial is currently enrolling patients to evaluate RYZ101 in patients with SSTR-positive GEP-NETs who have previously been treated with lutetium-177 based somatostatin therapies. ... A Phase 1b clinical trial is also currently enrolling patients to evaluate RYZ101 as a first-line treatment of ES-SCLC in combination with standard-of-care therapy. The platform has the potential to be a significant IND engine to generate a number of candidates and comes with a state-of-the-art RPT manufacturing facility, which is expected to begin operating in the first half of 2024. Bristol Myers Squibb’s previously announced tender offer to acquire all of the outstanding shares of RayzeBio common stock for a purchase price of $62.50 per share in cash, or approximately $4.1 billion, expired at one minute after 11:59 p.m., Eastern Time on February 22, 2024.

sources.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Adds Premier Radiopharmaceutical Platform with Acquisition of RayzeBio
sources.snippet: Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE: BMY) and RayzeBio, Inc. (NASDAQ: RYZB) today announced a definitive merger agreement under which Bristol Myers Squibb will acquire RayzeBio for $62.50 per share in cash, for a total equity value of approximately $4.1 billion, or $3.6 billion net of estimated cash acquired. ... RayzeBio is completing construction of a state-of-the-art in-house manufacturing facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, and GMP drug production is expected to begin in the first half of 2024.

sources.title: FDA selects RayzeBio to Participate in the Inaugural CDRP Program to Expedite Commercial Manufacturing Readiness for RYZ101 in GEP-NETs
sources.snippet: SAN DIEGO, Oct. 24, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- RayzeBio, Inc. (Nasdaq: RYZB), a targeted radiopharmaceutical company developing an innovative pipeline against validated solid tumor targets, today announced the FDA has selected RYZ101 (Actinium-225 DOTATATE) into the inaugural Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls (CMC) Development and Readiness Pilot (CDRP) program.

sources.title: RayzeBio Announces First Patient Dosed in Phase 3 ACTION-1 ...
sources.snippet:
**SAN DIEGO, California, May 31, 2023** – RayzeBio, Inc., a targeted radiopharmaceutical company developing an innovative pipeline against validated solid tumor targets, today announced that the first patient has been dosed in the Phase 3 trial of RYZ101 in patients with SSTR+ gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) that have progressed following prior Lutetium-177 labelled somatostatin analogue therapy. ... The Phase 3 trial is a global study and expected to enroll 210 patients, randomized 1:1 between RYZ101 and investigator’s choice of standard of care (SOC), which includes everolimus, sunitinib, or high-dose long-acting SSAs. The primary endpoint for the trial is progression free survival (PFS). ... Ac225 for the study was provided by multiple sources including the U.S.
Department of Energy Isotope Program.

sources.snippet: The last decade however saw three consecutive phase 3 trials that have represented a leap forward in the recognition of this therapeutic modality, the ALSYMPCA, NETTER-1, and VISION studies, eventually leading to the approval of, respectively, [^223^Ra]RaCl~2~ (Xofigo®), [^177^Lu]Lu-DOTATATE (Lutathera®) and [^177^Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 (Pluvicto®) (2–4). ... The introduction of ^177^Lu-labeled PSMA-inhibitors has revolutionized the management of prostate cancer in recent years (15). Two prominent compounds, PSMA-617 and PSMA-I&T have demonstrated similar behavior (16). ... Recruitment for these studies is now complete and results are awaited for potential approval. [^177^Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 is also the subject of numerous clinical trials, among which 5 are phase 3 studies (17). These aim to establish the feasibility to treat patients in earlier stages of the disease, in metastatic hormone-sensitive (**NCT04720157**, **NCT06496581**, **NCT06320067**) or oligometastatic prostate cancers (**NCT05939414**). ... In the case of PSMA based TRT, the radioactive isotopes are attached to PSMA-targeting molecules that bind to PSMA on the surface of prostate cancer cells.

sources.title: Fusion Pharmaceuticals Announces First Patient Dosed in the Phase 2 Portion of the AlphaBreak Trial Evaluating FPI-2265 in Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer
sources.snippet:
HAMILTON, ON and BOSTON, May 9, 2024 /PRNewswire/ -- Fusion Pharmaceuticals Inc. (Nasdaq: FUSN), a clinical-stage oncology company focused on developing next-generation radioconjugates (RCs) as precision medicines, today announced that the first patient has been dosed in the Phase 2 portion of the AlphaBreak trial evaluating FPI-2265 (
225Ac-PSMA I&T) in patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC).

[V1] Fusion Pharma Begins Phase 2 Dosing in AlphaBreak Trial for Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer (0.85): "This study investigates FPI-2265, an actinium-225 based PSMA targeting radioconjugate (RC), in individuals with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). comment.key_citation: [V1] Acquisition of Fusion Pharmaceuticals Completed (0.95): "BOSTON and HAMILTON, JUNE 4, 2024 /PRNewswire/ -- In a significant move towards revolutionizing cancer treatment, AstraZeneca AB, through a subsidiary, has successfully completed the acquisition of Fusion Pharmaceuticals Inc. (Nasdaq: FUSN) via a statutory plan of arrangement under section 192 of the Canada Business Corporations Act. ... The Arrangement brings Fusion's pipeline of next-generation radioconjugates (RCs) to AstraZeneca, including the most advanced program, FPI-2265, a potential new therapy for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). ... As a result of the Arrangement, Fusion Pharmaceuticals has become a wholly owned subsidiary of AstraZeneca, with operations continuing in Canada and the US." (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/acquisition-of-fusion-pharmaceuticals-completed-302163794.html)

sources.title: Acquisition of Fusion Pharmaceuticals Completed
sources.snippet: BOSTON and HAMILTON, JUNE 4, 2024 /PRNewswire/ -- In a significant move towards revolutionizing cancer treatment, AstraZeneca AB, through a subsidiary, has successfully completed the acquisition of Fusion Pharmaceuticals Inc. (Nasdaq: FUSN) via a statutory plan of arrangement under section 192 of the Canada Business Corporations Act. ... The Arrangement brings Fusion's pipeline of next-generation radioconjugates (RCs) to AstraZeneca, including the most advanced program, FPI-2265, a potential new therapy for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). ... As a result of the Arrangement, Fusion Pharmaceuticals has become a wholly owned subsidiary of AstraZeneca, with operations continuing in Canada and the US.

sources.title: Fusion Pharmaceuticals doses first subject in prostate ...
sources.snippet: Fusion Pharmaceuticals has dosed the first subject in the Phase II segment of the Phase II/III AlphaBreak clinical trial of FPI-2265 (²²⁵Ac-PSMA I&T) to treat metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). ... The Phase II dose optimisation part will compare additional safety or efficacy benefits of two alternative dosing regimens against the previously researched dosage of 100kBq/kg administered every eight weeks. ... This segment of the trial is anticipated to conclude the enrolment of around 60 patients by the end of this year.

sources.title: Fusion Pharma Begins Phase 2 Dosing in AlphaBreak Trial for Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer
sources.snippet: Fusion Pharmaceuticals Inc., a clinical-stage oncology company, has announced the dosing of the first patient in the Phase 2 segment of the AlphaBreak trial. ... The Phase 2 portion aims to determine if there are additional safety and efficacy benefits from two alternative dosing regimens compared to the previously studied dose of 100 kBq/kg every eight weeks. ... The goal is to enroll around 60 patients by the end of 2024. Upon completion and analysis of the Phase 2 data, a Phase 3 global registration trial will commence, enrolling approximately 550 patients in 2025. ... Additionally, Fusion has strategic actinium supply agreements with Niowave, Inc. and BWXT Medical.

The correct company developing this specific licensed asset is Ratio Therapeutics.

sources.title: CytoSite Bio Announces Agreement with Lantheus for ...
sources.snippet: *A global collaboration agreement has been signed for the development and commercialization of a Granzyme B-targeted Positron Emission Tomography (PET) imaging radiotracer.* ... BOSTON, March 26, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — CytoSite Bio (CytoSite) announced today that they have signed a collaboration agreement for the clinical development and potential commercialization of CytoSite’s investigational Phase 1 ready granzyme B-targeted Positron Emission Tomography (PET) imaging tracer with Lantheus Holdings, Inc. This agreement provides Lantheus with an exclusive option to license worldwide rights to CytoSite’s novel imaging technology.

comment.validator_explanation: The current value is accurate and confirmed by the primary licensing announcement, which states the agent targets Granzyme B for PET imaging applications in inflammation and cancer indications [1]. The source elaborates that Granzyme B is released by activated immune cells and serves as a biomarker to monitor immune cell activation in inflammatory diseases, autoimmune diseases, cancer, and infection [1].
comment.key_citation: [V1] Ratio Therapeutics Announces Licensing Agreement for Investigational PET Imaging Agent with Merck (0.95): "*Collaboration to develop a Granzyme B-targeted agent * *for PET imaging applications in inflammation and cancer indications* BOSTON, March 28, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company that employs a suite of innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for the monitoring and treatment of cancers, today announced the licensing of a Granzyme B-targeted agent for PET imaging applications from Merck, known as MSD outside the United States and Canada. Under the terms of the agreement, Merck will provide Ratio with several candidates and supporting preclinical and CMC data of its Granzyme B-targeted agent for PET imaging applications. Ratio will also be responsible for clinical development and will build a GMP production network for the PET imaging agent across North America, Western Europe, Japan, Australia, and beyond. ... As such, Granzyme B holds potential to serve as a PET imaging biomarker to monitor immune cell activation in a variety of inflammatory diseases, autoimmune diseases, cancer and infection. ... Ratio's fully integrated proprietary R&D platforms, Trillium™ and Macropa™, enable the imaging, discovery and advancement of novel radiopharmaceuticals that have first/best-in-class delivery, safety and efficacy properties." (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/ratio-therapeutics-announces-licensing-agreement-for-investigational-pet-imaging-agent-with-merck-301782556.html)

Target / Indication.full_value: High-risk neuroblastoma

comment.validator_explanation: I-131 MIBG is a targeted radiopharmaceutical therapy for neuroendocrine tumors including neuroblastoma [1].
comment.key_citation: [V1] I-131 MIBG Therapy (0.85): "Iodine-131 meta-iodobenzylguanidine (I-131 MIBG) is a radiopharmaceutical used for both imaging and treating certain types of neuroendocrine tumors. Neuroendocrine tumors that can be treated with I-131 MIBG include neuroblastomas, paragangliomas and pheochromocytomas. This targeted radionuclide therapy is able to detect and treat these tumors because the compound MIBG is very similar to norepinephrine/noradrenaline, a neurotransmitter chemical that is taken up by certain neuroendocrine cells. I-131 MIBG is used to treat neuroendocrine tumors by selectively targeting and killing neuroendocrine tumors that take up MIBG. ... Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved iobenguane I-131, a form of I-131 MIBG, for the treatment of paragangliomas and pheochromocytomas." (https://snmmi.org/AM/Patients/Procedures/I-131-MIBG-Therapy.aspx)

This targeted radionuclide therapy is able to detect and treat these tumors because the compound MIBG is very similar to norepinephrine/noradrenaline, a neurotransmitter chemical that is taken up by certain neuroendocrine cells. I-131 MIBG is used to treat neuroendocrine tumors by selectively targeting and killing neuroendocrine tumors that take up MIBG. ... Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved iobenguane I-131, a form of I-131 MIBG, for the treatment of paragangliomas and pheochromocytomas." (https://snmmi.org/AM/Patients/Procedures/I-131-MIBG-Therapy.aspx)

- 29 November 2023: First patient treated in PANCOSIL Investigator Initiated Clinical Trial (CT-guided percutaneous application) in Hong Kong (20 patients total) [1]
- October 2024: German Federal Joint Committee (G-BA) approved a randomised controlled trial for OncoSil™ under a Coverage with Evidence Development (CED) program [2]
- October 2024: British Standards Institution (BSI) renewed UK Conformity Assessed (UKCA) certificates for OncoSil™ with no post-market restrictions [2]
- April 2020: Received European CE marking approval for treatment of locally advanced pancreatic cancer in combination with chemotherapy [3]

sources.title: Iomab-B
sources.snippet: Iomab-B is a first-in-class targeted radiotherapy intended to improve patient access to potentially curative BMT by simultaneously and rapidly depleting blood cancer, immune and bone marrow stem cells that uniquely express CD45. Multiple studies have demonstrated increased survival in patients receiving BMT, however, an overwhelming majority of patients with blood cancers do not receive BMT as current approaches do not produce a remission, which is needed to advance to BMT, or are too toxic. ... Studied in over 400 patients, prior studies with Iomab-B have demonstrated nearly universal access to BMT, increased survival and tolerability in multiple clinical trials including the completed Phase 3 SIERRA trial in patients with active (leukemic blasts >5%), relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia (r/r AML) age 55 and above.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Aug 6, 2024: FDA determined SIERRA trial data in...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Aug 6, 2024: FDA determined SIERRA trial data insufficient for BLA filing; advised to attain overall survival benefit in randomized head-to-head trial [1]
• Jan 2024: SIERRA trial data cutoff showed median OS of 6.3 months (Iomab-B arm) vs 4.0 months (conventional care arm), HR 0.69, p=0.0314 [1]
• 2024: SIERRA trial met primary endpoint of durable complete remission (dCR) with p<0.0001; 6-month dCR rates were 22% (Iomab-B) vs 0% (conventional care) [1]
• 2024: Long-term survival data showed 92.3% and 69.2% of dCR patients alive at 12 and 24 months respectively [1]
• 2024: 74.6% of Iomab-B arm patients achieved CR/CRp vs 6.3% in conventional care arm [1]
• 2024: All Iomab-B patients underwent HCT vs only 18.2% in conventional care arm [1]
• Q4 2021: SIERRA trial completed enrollment with last patient receiving BMT [2]
• Apr 12, 2022: Actinium and Immedica announced license and supply agreement for Iomab-B in Europe, Middle East and North Africa; $35M upfront, up to $417M in milestones, mid-twenty percent royalties [2]

sources.title: Iomab-ACT for Gene & Cell Therapy Conditioning
sources.snippet: We are studying Iomab-ACT in collaboration with Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Cnenter (MSKCC), for conditioning prior to CAR-T therapy for patients with relapsed or refractory B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B-ALL) or diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL). ... In doing so, Actinium believes the Iomab-ACT program has the potential to improve patient access by replacing the current non-targeted, non-optimized conditioning regimens such as Fludarabine and Cyclophosphamide (Flu/Cy) and Busulfan, which have several known toxicities including risk of long-term infertility. The results from our first-in-human proof of concept study with Iomab-ACT in collaboration with MSKCC is supporting two additional trials with Iomab-ACT. The first as targeted conditioning with a commercial CAR-T therapy at the University of Texas Southwestern (UTSW) Medical Center and one for targeted conditioning for patients with sickle cell disease (SCD) at Columbia University.

Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.display_value: FDA requires head-to-head randomized trial demonst...
Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.full_value: FDA requires head-to-head randomized trial demonstrating overall survival benefit for BLA filing [1]. Actinium met with FDA in Q4 and aligned on patient population for head-to-head Phase 3 trial evaluating Iomab-B plus Flu/TBI versus cyclophosphamide plus Flu/TBI in adult patients aged 18+ with active AML (blasts 5-20%), representing broader population than SIERRA [3]. Dose optimization trial also planned [3]. No abandonment signs; active development continues with Iomab-ACT program for CAR-T/gene therapy conditioning at MSKCC, UTSW, and Columbia University, with trials cleared by FDA and actively enrolling [4]. European commercialization partnership with Immedica remains active [2].

Target / Indication.full_value: Fibroblast Activation Protein-alpha (FAP) in multiple solid tumors, including pancreatic, breast, colorectal, and soft tissue sarcomas

comment.validator_explanation: Ratio Therapeutics press releases specify FAP as the target for imaging agent RTX-1363S and therapeutic RTX-2358, with indications in desmoplastic cancers and sarcomas [1][2]
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator correctly identified FAP target and specific cancer indications from Ratio Therapeutics press releases

sources.title: Press Release – Phase I Novel FAP-Targeted Radiopharmaceutical
sources.snippet: “Today marks a major milestone for Ratio as we take a crucial step forward in the development of our FAP-targeted imaging agent,” said Dr. John Babich, Ratio’s President and Chief Scientific Officer. “Cancer-associated fibroblasts selectively expressing FAP comprise up to 90% of the tumor mass in highly desmoplastic cancers, including pancreatic, breast, and colorectal. ... Followdisease, and in collaboration with PharmaLogic, a world-class contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) specializing in radiopharmaceuticals, that it has initiated dosing in a Phase I study evaluating the pharmacokinetics, biodistribution and radiation dosimetry of a novel fibroblast activation protein-alpha (FAP)-targeted radiopharmaceutical, Copper-64[Cu-64]-labeled RTX-1363S, for PET imaging in adult healthy volunteers.

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics has Successfully Dosed the First Cohort in its Phase 1/2 Study Evaluating a Novel FAP-Targeted Radiopharmaceutical in Patients with Late-Stage Aggressive Sarcomas
sources.snippet: BOSTON, Dec. 16, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, announced today that dosing of the first cohort has been completed in the ATLAS trial, a Phase 1/2 open-label study evaluating the safety, tolerability, and efficacy of the company's lead therapeutic radiopharmaceutical, [Ac-225]-RTX-2358, targeting fibroblast activation protein‑α (FAP) in patients with relapsed or refractory soft tissue sarcomas. ... The ATLAS trial is a Phase 1/2 open-label clinical trial (clinicaltrials.gov identifier, NCT07156565) designed to assess the safety, tolerability, dosimetry, biodistribution, pharmacokinetics, and preliminary anti-tumor activity of [Ac-225]-RTX-2358, a highly selective, FAP targeted radiotherapeutic labeled with Actinium 225, in patients with relapsed or refractory soft tissue sarcomas that express FAP.

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics: Homepage
sources.snippet: Our compounds accommodate imaging and therapeutic radioisotopes, ie “theranostics”.

sources.title: Our Technology
sources.snippet: Ratio’s Trillium™ technology is a proprietary and innovative platform that incorporates a tunable, structural motif enabling our compounds to reversibly bind to albumin, thereby modulating their pharmacokinetics (PK) and improving drug availability, tumor delivery, and tumor loading, while simultaneously reducing the side effects commonly observed with other radiotherapies. ... Ratio’s Macropa™ technology reflects a proprietary chelator that is used to attach the alpha emitting radionuclide actinium-225 to Trillium™ and other compounds.

(https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/ratio-therapeutics-and-nusano-enter-long-term-multi-isotope-supply-agreement-to-boost-production-of-cancer-radiopharmaceuticals-302482654.html)

sources.title: June 17th, 2025: Ratio Therapeutics and Nusano Enter Long-Term ...
sources.snippet: *Agreement provides Ratio access to Nusano-produced radioisotopes for oncology diagnostics and therapeutics* *Accelerates Ratio’s clinical pipeline with copper-64 for PET imaging and luetium-177 and actinium-225 for targeted therapy* *Bolsters both companies’ manufacturing and supply chain capabilities in Utah* **BOSTON and WEST VALLEY CITY, Utah – June 17, 2025** – Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, and Nusano, a physics company enabling breakthrough radioisotope supplies, today announced a long-term supply agreement designed to support the Ratio’s product pipeline and enable innovation. The agreement gives Ratio access to reliable, high-volume, commercial-scale supplies of copper-64 (Cu-64) for PET imaging diagnostics produced by Nusano, as well as lutetium-177 (Lu-177) and actinium-225 (Ac-225) for therapeutic applications. ... Nusano is a radioisotope producer with a proprietary platform capable of generating more than 40 different isotopes for medical and industrial applications. The company intends to use these capabilities to help stabilize the existing medical radioisotope market and enable oncology innovation by providing researchers and drugmakers with isotopes yet to be fully explored for their cancer-fighting properties. ... Driven by the company’s proprietary ion source, the Nusano production platform will generate radioisotopes in volumes and varieties exceeding other production methods.

(Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, and Nusano, a physics company enabling breakthrough radioisotope supplies, today announced a long-term supply agreement designed to support the Ratio’s product pipeline and enable innovation. The agreement gives Ratio access to reliable, high-volume, commercial-scale supplies of copper-64 (Cu-64) for PET imaging diagnostics produced by Nusano, as well as lutetium-177 (Lu-177) and actinium-225 (Ac-225) for therapeutic applications. ... Nusano is a radioisotope producer with a proprietary platform capable of generating more than 40 different isotopes for medical and industrial applications. The company intends to use these capabilities to help stabilize the existing medical radioisotope market and enable oncology innovation by providing researchers and drugmakers with isotopes yet to be fully explored for their cancer-fighting properties. ... Driven by the company’s proprietary ion source, the Nusano production platform will generate radioisotopes in volumes and varieties exceeding other production methods." (https://ratiotx.com/2025/06/17/june-17th-2025-ratio-therapeutics-and-nusano-enter-long-term-multi-isotope-supply-agreement-to-boost-production-of-cancer-radiopharmaceuticals/)

sources.title: Nusano Announces Isotope Availability
sources.snippet: **VALENCIA, Calif. – January 10, 2025 –** Nusano, a privately held physics company transforming the production of radioisotopes utilized in radiopharmaceuticals, clean energy and aerospace, today announced the initial isotope availability from its 2025-2026 commercial roadmap.

sources.title: Nusano launches commercial isotope operations
sources.snippet: Valencia, CA-based radioisotope development company Nusano has launched commercial operations and announced a lutetium-177 (Lu-177) supply agreement with Australian targeted radiotherapy company GlyTherix.

sources.title: Clarity signs high-volume commercial-scale copper-64 Supply ...
sources.snippet: Nusano’s 190,000 square foot state-of-the-art facility in West Valley City, Utah is expected to begin production in 2025 with copper-64 isotope supply planned to commence in early 2026. The accelerator-based proprietary technologies employed by Nusano are particularly well suited for cost-effective mass production of copper-64.

comment.validator_explanation: No specific biological target or cancer indication found for TerraPower Isotopes' Actinium-225 supply. The company supplies the raw isotope for use in various targeted radiotherapeutics developed by partners like Ratio Therapeutics [1]. The isotope is used in targeted alpha therapies for cancers such as prostate, breast, colon, neuroendocrine cancers, melanoma, and lymphoma [2], but the specific target and indication depend on the drug developer's molecule.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator correctly identified no specific target for an isotope supplier. Original value was speculative and incorrect.

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics and TerraPower Isotopes Sign Supply Agreement for Actinium-225 For Targeted Radiotherapeutics
sources.snippet: BOSTON and BELLEVUE, Wash., June 20, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, and TerraPower Isotopes, a subsidiary of TerraPower, a leading nuclear innovation company, today announced they entered into a supply agreement for the medical radioisotope actinium-225 (Ac-225) 1. Under the agreement, TerraPower Isotopes would supply Ratio with quantities of non-cGMP Ac-225 for use to incorporate into or use in the development of Ratio's radiopharmaceuticals. ... For Ratio, access to a reliable supply of Ac-225 is essential to advancing its pipeline based on its proprietary Trillium ™ and Macropa ™ platforms.

sources.title: TerraPower Isotopes Brings Actinium-225 to Market
sources.snippet: **Bellevue, WA – October 1, 2024 – **TerraPower Isotopes announced today it is producing Actinium-225 1 at commercial scale, providing sustained access to highly pure Actinium-225 to the global pharmaceutical community through weekly production runs. As a result, TerraPower Isotopes’ Actinium, after further manufacturing, is now used in multiple drug developer’s radiopharmaceuticals in human clinical trials across the globe. ... TerraPower Isotopes is bringing the next generation of isotopes to market using a natural decay method to produce Actinium-225 free of isotopic impurities. ... Actinium-225 is used as a starting material for research and development of advanced, targeted cancer treatments and in drug trials involving targeted alpha therapy. Through further manufacturing, the isotope can be attached to a molecule, which can then selectively target and deliver Actinium-225 to the cancer site to potentially treat diseases such as prostate, breast, colon and neuroendocrine cancers, melanoma and lymphoma.

sources.title: Multiple Production Methods Underway to Provide Actinium-225
sources.snippet: Discovered in 1947 by a team at Argonne National Laboratory and an independent Canadian research team, actinium-225 (Ac-225) undergoes alpha decay to francium-221 with a half-life of 10 days.

comment.original_value: Supply of Actinium-225 for targeted radiotherapeutics
comment.validator_explanation: Updated for greater specificity. TerraPower Isotopes supplies non-cGMP Actinium-225 for partners like Ratio Therapeutics to incorporate into their radiopharmaceuticals [1]. The isotope is used in conjunction with proprietary platforms such as Ratio's Trillium™ targeting scaffold and Macropa™ chelator technology [1]. TerraPower produces Ac-225 via a natural decay method from thorium-229, yielding isotope-free impurities [2].

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics and Nusano Enter Long-Term, Multi-Isotope Supply Agreement to Boost Production of Cancer Radiopharmaceuticals
sources.snippet: BOSTON and WEST VALLEY CITY, Utah, June 17, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, and Nusano, a physics company enabling breakthrough radioisotope supplies, today announced a long-term supply agreement designed to support Ratio's product pipeline and enable innovation. The agreement gives Ratio access to reliable, high-volume, commercial-scale supplies of copper-64 (Cu-64) for PET imaging diagnostics produced by Nusano, as well as lutetium-177 (Lu-177) and actinium-225 (Ac-225) for therapeutic applications. ... Supplies of Lu-177 and Ac-225 from Nusano will also play an important role in Ratio's growing radiopharmaceutical pipeline.

Since then, the company has progressed its collaboration programs, established additional internal discovery and preclinical programs, implemented a comprehensive intellectual property strategy in targeted radiotherapies and diagnostics and filed numerous patent applications.

sources.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Completes Acquisition of RayzeBio, Adding Differentiated Actinium-Based Radiopharmaceutical Platform
sources.snippet: This transaction brings a promising pipeline of RPTs to Bristol Myers Squibb, including RayzeBio’s lead program RYZ101 (225Ac-DOTATATE), which targets somatostatin receptor 2 (SSTR2), over-expressed in GEP-NETs and extensive stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC). A Phase 3 clinical trial is currently enrolling patients to evaluate RYZ101 in patients with SSTR-positive GEP-NETs who have previously been treated with lutetium-177 based somatostatin therapies. ... A Phase 1b clinical trial is also currently enrolling patients to evaluate RYZ101 as a first-line treatment of ES-SCLC in combination with standard-of-care therapy.

sources.title: Bristol Myers Squibb expands radiopharmaceutical ...

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics Announces $50M Series B Financing ...
sources.snippet: **BOSTON, January 17, 2024** – Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company that employs a suite of innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for the treatment and monitoring of cancers, today announces the close of its $50M Series B financing, bringing the total raised to date to over $90 million. The latest round witnessed continued participation from Series A leads Schusterman and Duquesne and welcomed among others PagsGroup, Bristol Myers Squibb and the Center for Technology Licensing at Cornell University.

sources.title: June 17th, 2025: Ratio Therapeutics and Nusano Enter Long-Term ...
sources.snippet:
**BOSTON and WEST VALLEY CITY, Utah – June 17, 2025** – Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, and Nusano, a physics company enabling breakthrough radioisotope supplies, today announced a long-term supply agreement designed to support the Ratio’s product pipeline and enable innovation.
The agreement gives Ratio access to reliable, high-volume, commercial-scale supplies of copper-64 (Cu-64) for PET imaging diagnostics produced by Nusano, as well as lutetium-177 (Lu-177) and actinium-225 (Ac-225) for therapeutic applications.

We are excited to be the exclusive manufacturer of Macropa and plan to add multiple derivatives to our catalog and provide it under GMP conditions for innovators engaged in clinical research."

The agreement gives Ratio access to reliable, high-volume, commercial-scale supplies of copper-64 (Cu-64) for PET imaging diagnostics produced by Nusano, as well as lutetium-177 (Lu-177) and actinium-225 (Ac-225) for therapeutic applications.

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics Partners with Macrocyclics for Exclusive ...
sources.snippet:
"Ratio's Macropa is a superior chelator for Ac-225," said Paul Jurek, Ph.D., CEO of Macrocyclics. "The ability to label quickly at room temperature provides a critical advantage, especially when working with proteins or vectors that degrade when heated.
We are excited to be the exclusive manufacturer of Macropa and plan to add multiple derivatives to our catalog and provide it under GMP conditions for innovators engaged in clinical research."

sources.snippet: [^177^Lu]Lu-PNT2002 consists of a beta-emitting [^177^Lu]lutetium radioisotope chelated to a small-molecule ligand (also known as PSMA-Imaging & Therapy [I&T]) that targets PSMA using a glutamate urea-based pharmacophore, enabling targeted delivery of radiation to prostate cancer cells, causing DNA damage and ultimately cancer cell death. sources.snippet: The primary objective of the study is to determine the efficacy of [Lu-177]-PNT2002 ([Lu-177]-PSMA-I&T) versus abiraterone or enzalutamide in delaying radiographic progression in patients with mCRPC. ... - PSMA-PET scan (i.e., 68Ga-PSMA-11 or 18F-DCFPyL) positive as determined by the sponsor's central reader.

sources.title: Initial clinical experience with [177Lu]Lu-PNT2002 radioligand ...
sources.snippet: [^177^Lu]Lu-PNT2002 consists of a beta-emitting [^177^Lu]lutetium radioisotope chelated to a small-molecule ligand (also known as PSMA-Imaging & Therapy [I&T]) that targets PSMA using a glutamate urea-based pharmacophore, enabling targeted delivery of radiation to prostate cancer cells, causing DNA damage and ultimately cancer cell death. [^177^Lu]Lu-PSMA-I&T used as an investigational agent has demonstrated promising anti-tumor activity with limited side effects (11).

sources.title: Initial clinical experience with [177Lu]Lu-PNT2002 radioligand ...
sources.snippet: ** SPLASH (NCT04647526) is a multicenter phase III trial evaluating the efficacy and safety of [^177^Lu]Lu-PNT2002 radioligand therapy in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC).

• Dec 19, 2023: Phase III SPLASH trial met primary endpoint with 9.5 months median rPFS vs 6 months for ARPI, showing 29% reduction in risk of radiographic progression or death [1]
• Oct 3, 2023: Eli Lilly announced definitive agreement to acquire Point Biopharma for $1.4 billion to expand oncology capabilities into radioligand therapies [2]
• Jan 7, 2024: Lilly continued radiopharmaceutical expansion with $140M upfront partnership with Radionetics Oncology for GPCR-targeted drugs [3]

sources.title: Lilly to Acquire POINT Biopharma to Expand Oncology Capabilities into Next-Generation Radioligand Therapies
sources.snippet: INDIANAPOLIS, Oct. 3, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Eli Lilly and Company (NYSE: LLY) and POINT Biopharma Global, Inc. (NASDAQ: PNT) today announced a definitive agreement for Lilly to acquire POINT, a radiopharmaceutical company with a pipeline of clinical and preclinical-stage radioligand therapies in development for the treatment of cancer.

sources.title: Lilly signs $140M radiopharma partnership with Radionectics
sources.snippet: Eli Lilly is continuing to pursue its newfound passion for radiopharmaceuticals, this time handing Radionetics Oncology $140 million in upfront cash to partner on its pipeline of G protein coupled receptor (GPCR) targeted drugs. ... Lilly dived into the nascent radiopharma space by acquiring Point Biopharma for $1.4 billion in late 2023. The takeover included a manufacturing plant, R&D campus and pipeline led by a PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy, giving Lilly a launchpad for a broader push to make the modality a key part of its oncology pipeline.

comment.key_citation: [V1] 225Ac-PSMA-617 (0.85): "**225Ac-PSMA-617** is an investigational radiopharmaceutical used in targeted alpha therapy (TAT) for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) and, more recently, for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (mHSPC). Initially developed by the German Cancer Research Center and University Hospital Heidelberg, it combines actinium-225 (225Ac), an alpha-emitting radionuclide, with PSMA-617, a small-molecule ligand that targets prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA), a protein overexpressed in prostate cancer cells." (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/225Ac-PSMA-617)

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Jan 16, 2026: UCLA Health launched the Phase 2 ANDROMEDA trial (NCT07150715), the first study to directly compare 225Ac-PSMA-617 vs 177Lu-PSMA-617 combined with stereotactic body radiotherapy (SBRT) for oligorecurrent prostate cancer [1].
• 2023: Launched novel clinical trial comparing Ac-225 vs Lu-177 PSMA-617 (as previously noted).
• As of May 2025: 225Ac-PSMA-617 remains in early-phase clinical trials and has not yet received regulatory approval [2].

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Apr 23, 2024: FDA approved Lutathera for pediatr...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Apr 23, 2024: FDA approved Lutathera for pediatric patients 12 years and older with SSTR+ GEP-NETs, making it the first therapy specifically approved for pediatric GEP-NETs [1]
• Jan 19, 2024: Novartis announced Phase III NETTER-2 trial results showing Lutathera plus octreotide LAR significantly reduced risk of disease progression or death by 72% in first-line treatment for advanced GEP-NETs [2]
• Sep 25, 2023: Novartis announced Phase III NETTER-2 trial met primary endpoint, demonstrating statistically significant and clinically meaningful progression-free survival benefit in first-line advanced GEP-NETs [3]
• Jan 26, 2018: FDA approved Lutathera for adult patients with SSTR-positive GEP-NETs, the first radioactive drug approved for these cancers [4][5]

Target / Indication.full_value: Somatostatin Receptor 2 (SSTR2) in gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs)

comment.validator_explanation: The current value 'GEP-NETs' is accurate but incomplete. GEP-NETs are the cancer indication, but the biological target is SSTR2 (somatostatin receptor type 2). Multiple high-quality sources confirm that 80-100% of well-differentiated GEP-NETs overexpress SSTR2 [1], and that these receptors are specifically targeted by DOTATOC [2]. The FDA approval specifies treatment for somatostatin receptor-positive GEP-NETs [3]. The updated value follows the format shown in examples (e.g., 'Somatostatin Receptor 2 (SSTR2) in neuroendocrine tumors') and provides both the biological target and the cancer indication as required by the field description.

comment.validator_explanation: The current value is accurate and complete. Multiple authoritative sources confirm that Lu-177-DOTATOC is a Lutetium-177 beta-emitting radioisotope chelated to the somatostatin peptide analog DOTATOC [2]. The mechanism involves the somatostatin analog (octreotate/DOTATOC) selectively binding to somatostatin receptor expressing cells and being internalized along with the chelated beta-emitting isotope Lu-177 [4]. The START-NET trial specifically uses 177Lu-DOTATOC [5]. The value correctly identifies both the radionuclide (Lutetium-177) and payload type (beta-emitter conjugated to somatostatin analog).

Diagnostic/Theranostic Agent.full_value: Therapeutic radioligand therapy (personalized dosimetry based on 68Ga-DOTATOC PET/CT imaging)

comment.validator_explanation: The current value 'Therapeutic (personalized treatment)' is accurate but lacks important specificity about the dual imaging approach. The START-NET trial is comparing personalized vs non-personalized radionuclide therapy with 177Lu-DOTATOC [5], and the context mentions 'personalized dosimetry based on dual imaging.' The personalization involves using 68Ga-DOTATOC PET/CT imaging for treatment planning [6] and [6]. While Lu-177-DOTATOC itself is purely therapeutic (not a theranostic pair), the trial's innovation is the personalized dosimetry approach using diagnostic imaging to optimize therapeutic delivery. The updated value clarifies this distinction while maintaining accuracy.
comment.qc_reasoning: Clarified that the agent itself is therapeutic, not a theranostic pair. The 'personalized dosimetry' is a trial methodology, not an agent property. Updated to match field examples and standard terminology.

Specific Biochemistry & Approach.full_value: Somatostatin receptor targeting peptide (DOTATOC analog) for therapeutic delivery of Lutetium-177; START-NET trial innovation: personalized dosimetry using 68Ga-DOTATOC PET/CT imaging to optimize treatment planning versus standard fixed-dose approach

comment.original_value: Somatostatin receptor targeting peptide for therapeutic delivery of Lutetium-177, personalized dosimetry based on dual imaging
comment.validator_explanation: The current value is accurate but can be enhanced with more specific details about the START-NET trial's unique approach. The biochemistry involves DOTATOC, a somatostatin analog that selectively binds to somatostatin receptors (particularly SSTR2 and SSTR5) expressed on GEP-NET cells [2]. The mechanism involves receptor-mediated internalization of the Lu-177-labeled peptide, delivering targeted beta radiation [4]. The START-NET trial's specific innovation is comparing personalized treatment based on dual imaging (68Ga-DOTATOC PET/CT for dosimetry planning) versus standard non-personalized fixed-dose therapy [5]. Studies show 68Ga-DOTATOC PET/CT is used to establish DOTATOC uptake-positive tumors and guide treatment [6]. The updated value provides more specific detail about the trial's unique personalized dosimetry approach.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: - Sep 5, 2025: FDA approval for SIR-Spheres Y-90 r...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- Sep 5, 2025: FDA approval for SIR-Spheres Y-90 resin microspheres for the treatment of patients with unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) announced, making it the first selective internal radiation therapy (radioembolization) approved in both metastatic colorectal cancer and HCC [1]
- Sep 8, 2025: Sirtex Medical website provides detailed product information, specifications, and indications for SIR-Spheres Y-90 resin microspheres [2]
- Jan 22, 2026: Updated Instructions for Use (IFU) document published by Sirtex Medical confirms indications for unresectable metastatic liver tumors from primary colorectal cancer with adjuvant intra-hepatic artery chemotherapy (IHAC) of FUDR [3]
- Jan 7, 2026: FDA labeling document (P990065S014D) confirms the same indications and provides detailed product information [4]

comment.original_value: -  Status unknown, expected completion by now, no results published

Multiple recent, authoritative sources (FDA, Sirtex Medical, news) confirm the product is FDA-approved and actively marketed, with recent updates in 2025-2026. The product is not in a 'status unknown' Phase III trial as previously indicated.

sources.title: About SIR-Spheres - Sirtex
sources.snippet:
SIR-Spheres Y-90 resin microspheres are a permanent implant and intended for single use only.
The high number of microspheres provides a uniform radiation distribution, enabling treatment of solitary and multifocal liver tumors.
1–3
With a specific density close to that of red blood cells,
1 they are carried by the blood flow, penetrating deep into the tumor microvasculature.
Yttrium-90 has two inherent safety features for staff and patients:
- the minimal penetration depth of beta radiation emissions through tissue and air
- the relatively short half-life of 64.1 hours
SIR-Spheres Y-90 resin microspheres can therefore be delivered in a slow, deliberate manner
- to achieve an even distribution
- to maximize the number of microspheres delivered
- to optimize tumor coverage - even in patients with extensive disease
||
|--|
|Manufacturer|Sirtex Medical Pty Ltd|
|Isotope|Yttrium-90|
|Decay product|Zirconium-90|
|Composition|Resin (sulfonated divinyl benzene-styrene copolymer)|
|Specific density|1.125 - 1.6 g/ml (comparable to red blood cells)|
|Diameter|32±10 µm|
|T 1/2|64.1 h (94% within 11 days)|
|Penetration in tissue|2.5 mm (mean)| ... **Indications for Use**: SIR-Spheres
®Y-90 resin microspheres are indicated for the local tumor control of unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) in patients with no macrovascular invasion, Child Pugh-A cirrhosis, well-compensated liver function, and good performance status. ... They are also indicated for the treatment of unresectable metastatic liver tumors from primary colorectal cancer with adjuvant intra-hepatic artery chemotherapy (IHAC) of FUDR (Floxuridine).

sources.title: Ibritumomab tiuxetan - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: **Ibritumomab tiuxetan** (pronounced /ɪbrɪˈtuːmoʊmæb taɪˈʌksɛtæn/), sold under the trade name **Zevalin**, is a monoclonal antibody radioimmunotherapy treatment for non-Hodgkin's lymphoma. The drug uses the monoclonal mouse IgG1 antibody ibritumomab in conjunction with the chelator tiuxetan, to which a radioactive isotope (either yttrium-90 or indium-111) is added.

sources.title: Radium-223 Therapy for Patients with Metastatic Castrate-Resistant Prostate Cancer: An Update on Literature with Case Presentation
sources.snippet: The *α*-particle Radium-223 is an elemental chemical in the alkaline metal family of the periodic table and was the first in its class to obtain FDA approval for the management of bone pain in patients with CRPC in May 2013. It has a half-life of 11.4 days. ... Radium-223 dichloride (Xofigo®, Bayer HealthCare Pharmaceuticals Inc.) is the first *α*-particle emitter therapeutic agent approved by the FDA, with benefits in overall survival and delay in symptomatic skeletal event for patients with metastatic castrate-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC).

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: - May 30, 2025: Full results from Phase III PEACE ...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- May 30, 2025: Full results from Phase III PEACE III trial (NCT02194842) published in Annals of Oncology, showing combination of XOFIGO with enzalutamide significantly improved radiological progression-free survival (rPFS) and overall survival (OS) in mCRPC patients with bone metastases [1][2]
- May 28, 2025: ASCO 2025 presentation of XOFIGO combination data from PEACE III trial, highlighting clinical benefits [3]
- PEACE III trial (NCT02194842) investigating combination with enzalutamide (ongoing) [4]
- DORA trial (NCT03574571) investigating combination with docetaxel (ongoing) [4]

comment.original_value: -  PEACE III trial (NCT02194842) investigating combination with enzalutamide (ongoing)
comment.validator_explanation: Multiple high-quality sources (Bayer press release, BusinessWire, Urology Times) confirm PEACE III trial results were published in May 2025, providing significant new data. Original value was incomplete and outdated.
comment.key_citation:
[V1] (radium-223 dichloride) Data in Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer from Phase III PEACE III Trial Published in (0.85): "Additionally, at a preplanned interim analysis the results demonstrated statistically significant overall survival (OS), with a 31% reduction in the risk of death (HR=0.69; 95% CI 0.52-0.90; p=0.0031), with a median OS of 35.0 months (95% CI 28.8-38.9) in the enzalutamide arm compared to 42.3 months (95% CI 36.8-49.1) for XOFIGO plus enzalutamide. ... The PEACE III trial is an international, randomized, open-label, Phase III trial designed to investigate the efficacy and safety of XOFIGO in combination with enzalutamide in patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) and bone metastases.
A total of 446 patients were randomized 1:1 to receive either XOFIGO 55 kBq/kg as an intravenous injection monthly for six cycles in combination with enzalutamide 160mg orally daily or enzalutamide 160mg orally daily." (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250530348750/en/New-XOFIGO-radium-223-dichloride-Data-in-Metastatic-Castration-Resistant-Prostate-Cancer-from-Phase-III-PEACE-III-Trial-Published-in-Annals-of-Oncology)

sources.snippet:
Additionally, at a preplanned interim analysis the results demonstrated statistically significant overall survival (OS), with a 31% reduction in the risk of death (HR=0.69; 95% CI 0.52-0.90; p=0.0031), with a median OS of 35.0 months (95% CI 28.8-38.9) in the enzalutamide arm compared to 42.3 months (95% CI 36.8-49.1) for XOFIGO plus enzalutamide. ... The PEACE III trial is an international, randomized, open-label, Phase III trial designed to investigate the efficacy and safety of XOFIGO in combination with enzalutamide in patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) and bone metastases.
A total of 446 patients were randomized 1:1 to receive either XOFIGO 55 kBq/kg as an intravenous injection monthly for six cycles in combination with enzalutamide 160mg orally daily or enzalutamide 160mg orally daily.

comment.validator_explanation: The ClinicalTrials.gov entry for the RaRe study (NCT03458559) explicitly states the indication is for patients with 'castration-resistant prostate cancer metastatic to bone' [2]. The 2003 Phase II study also treated patients with bone metastases from prostate cancer [1]. The current value 'mCRPC' is accurate but the primary therapeutic goal is palliative pain relief for bone metastases.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator improved specificity by adding bone metastases context and palliative purpose. Original 'mCRPC' was accurate but less specific.

sources.title: Therapeutic efficiency of rhenium-188-HEDP in human prostate cancer skeletal metastases - PubMed
sources.snippet: Rhenium-188-HEDP ((188)Re-HEDP) is a new and attractive radiopharmaceutical for the treatment of metastatic bone pain. As a product of (188)W/(188)Re generator, it is convenient for clinical therapeutic use with a short physical half-life of 16.9 h and a maximal beta-energy of 2.1 MeV. We investigated the effect of (188)Re-HEDP on pain relief, analgesic intake and impairment of bone marrow function in 27 patients with bone metastases induced from prostate cancer.
