sources.title: Therapeutic efficiency of rhenium-188-HEDP in human ...
sources.snippet: As a product of ^188^W/^188^Re generator, it is convenient for clinical therapeutic use with a short physical half-life of 16.9 h and a maximal *β*-energy of 2.1 MeV. ... Rhenium-188-HEDP has a rapid renal excretion (40±12% of administered activity within the first 8 h), leading to a low radiation dose to the whole body (0.07±0.02 mGy MBq^−1^) and a relative short biological half-life (51±43 h) in comparison to the half-life within bone metastases (269±166 h) (Liepe *et al*, 2003).

In fact, the company Shanghai YITAI Pharmaceutical Technology Co., Ltd., initiated a Phase IIB clinical trial for castration-resistant prostate cancer patients to have enough clinical data for an approval procedure.

comment.validator_explanation: Multiple high-confidence sources confirm the radionuclide is Rhenium-188, a beta-emitter with a maximal beta-energy of 2.1 MeV and a physical half-life of 16.9 hours, complexed with the bone-seeking ligand hydroxyethylidene diphosphonate (HEDP) [1][3]. The current value is precisely correct.

Specific Biochemistry & Approach.full_value: Rhenium-188-labeled hydroxyethylidene diphosphonate (HEDP), a bisphosphonate analog that adsorbs to bone hydroxyapatite at sites of high osteoblastic activity (e.g., bone metastases). It is a generator-produced (^188W/^188Re) beta-emitting radiopharmaceutical (max β-energy 2.1 MeV, T1/2 16.9h) for palliative bone pain treatment

comment.validator_explanation: Sources provide detailed biochemical and pharmacokinetic specifics: the payload is HEDP, a bone-seeking bisphosphonate [3]. The radionuclide is generator-produced, has rapid renal excretion, and a long half-life within bone metastases compared to the whole body [3][5]. This significantly improves upon the generic 'bone-seeking beta-emitter' description.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator provided comprehensive biochemical details including HEDP mechanism, generator production, and pharmacokinetics. Original was generic 'bone-seeking beta-emitter'.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: - The Phase IIa multicenter, randomized, blinded e...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- The Phase IIa multicenter, randomized, blinded exploratory clinical study (NCT03458559) evaluating the analgesic effect of [188Re] etidronate injection in patients with CRPC bone metastasis was completed.
- Further evaluation of rhenium [188Re] etidronate injection (30MBq/kg, 40MBq/kg, 50MBq/kg) for analgesic efficacy and safety in tumor bone metastasis pain is planned, with Phase IIb dose selection based on Phase IIa results.
- A separate study (CTR20200377) aims to evaluate the pharmacokinetic (PK) and pharmacodynamic (PD) characteristics of a single intravenous injection of rhenium [188Re] etidronate injection in patients with castration-resistant prostate cancer bone metastases.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- Nov 18, 2024: Phase 3 ECLIPSE trial (NCT05204927) met primary endpoint, showing statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvement in radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) for patients with PSMA-positive mCRPC treated with 177Lu-PSMA-I&T versus hormonal therapy [1]
- Nov 13, 2024: 177Lu-PSMA-I&T (lutetium [177Lu] zadavotide guraxetan) led to significant rPFS improvement in the phase 3 ECLIPSE trial, meeting its primary endpoint [2]
- Oct 1, 2024: Curium announced strategic partnership with PDRadiopharma (PeptiDream) for clinical development, regulatory filing, manufacturing, and commercialization of 177Lu-PSMA-I&T and 64Cu-PSMA-I&T in Japan [3]

comment.validator_explanation: NCI defines PSMA as target overexpressed in malignant prostate and metastases [1]; Phase III trial specifically for PSMA-positive mCRPC [2]
comment.key_citation: [V1] Definition of lutetium Lu 177 rsopatamab tetraxetan (0.95): "A radioimmunoconjugate consisting of rosopatamab, a humanized monoclonal antibody (MoAb) against the external domain of the prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) that is linked, via the chelating agent, dodecanetetraacetic acid (DOTA), to the beta-emitting radioisotope lutetium Lu 177, with potential antineoplastic activity and imaging activity during single-photon emission computerized tomography/computerized tomography (SPECT/CT). Upon administration, lutetium Lu 177 rosopatamab tetraxetan binds to PSMA expressed on certain tumor cells. Upon binding and internalization, this radioconjugate specifically delivers a cytotoxic dose of beta radiation to PSMA-expressing cells. PSMA is overexpressed in the malignant prostate and its metastases.Check for active clinical trials using this agentView this agent in the NCI Thesaurus" (https://www.cancer.gov/publications/dictionaries/cancer-drug/def/lutetium-lu-177-rosopatamab-tetraxetan)

comment.key_citation:
[V1] ProstACT Global Phase 3 Update: First Patient Dosed in Randomized Treatment Expansion, Part 1 Readout Plans Confirmed (0.85): "MELBOURNE, Australia and INDIANAPOLIS, Dec. 8, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Telix Pharmaceuticals Limited (ASX: TLX, NASDAQ: TLX, "Telix") today announces that the first patient has been dosed in Part 2 (randomized treatment expansion) of its ProstACT Global Phase 3 study evaluating its lead prostate cancer therapy candidate TLX591 (lutetium (177 Lu) rosopatamab tetraxetan) in patients with metastatic castration resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). ... ProstACT Global is the first Phase 3 trial to combine a PSMA [1] -targeted radio antibody-drug conjugate (rADC) therapy administered together with Standard of Care (SOC; abiraterone, enzalutamide or docetaxel) versus SOC alone.
Part 2 of ProstACT Global will enroll approximately 490 patients and is currently recruiting patients in Australia, New Zealand and Canada." (https://www.prnewswire.com/apac/news-releases/prostact-global-phase-3-update-first-patient-dosed-in-randomized-treatment-expansion-part-1-readout-plans-confirmed-302634831.html)

sources.title: ProstACT Global Phase 3 Update: First Patient Dosed in Randomized Treatment Expansion, Part 1 Readout Plans Confirmed
sources.snippet:
MELBOURNE, Australia and INDIANAPOLIS, Dec. 8, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Telix Pharmaceuticals Limited (ASX: TLX, NASDAQ: TLX, "Telix") today announces that the first patient has been dosed in Part 2 (randomized treatment expansion) of its ProstACT Global Phase 3 study evaluating its lead prostate cancer therapy candidate TLX591 (lutetium (177 Lu) rosopatamab tetraxetan) in patients with metastatic castration resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). ... ProstACT Global is the first Phase 3 trial to combine a PSMA [1] -targeted radio antibody-drug conjugate (rADC) therapy administered together with Standard of Care (SOC; abiraterone, enzalutamide or docetaxel) versus SOC alone.
Part 2 of ProstACT Global will enroll approximately 490 patients and is currently recruiting patients in Australia, New Zealand and Canada.

Telix today announces that the first patient has been dosed in the Company’s Phase III ProstACT GLOBAL study of its investigational prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) targeting radio-antibody drug conjugate (rADC) therapy, TLX591 (
177Lu-rosopatamab tetraxetan).
TLX591 is a rADC composed of a high-specificity PSMA-targeting antibody, chelator linker, and cytotoxic lutetium ( 177Lu) payload.

sources.title: First Patient Dosed in Phase III ProstACT GLOBAL Study of Antibody-based Prostate Cancer Therapy Candidate, TLX591
sources.snippet:
MELBOURNE, Australia, Nov. 12, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Telix Pharmaceuticals Limited (ASX: TLX, Telix, the Company) today announces that the first patient has been dosed in the Company's Phase III ProstACT GLOBAL study of its investigational prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) targeting radio-antibody drug conjugate (rADC) therapy, TLX591 (
177Lu-rosopatamab tetraxetan).
TLX591 is a rADC composed of a high-specificity PSMA-targeting antibody, chelator linker, and cytotoxic lutetium ( 177Lu) payload.

Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.full_value: Primary efficacy and safety data readout from ProstACT Global Phase 3 Part 2 (randomized expansion) expected 2026-2027; trial actively recruiting with recent patient dosing in December 2025 indicating continued development [1]. No abandonment signs - trial is actively progressing with recent patient dosing and target enrollment of ~490 patients in Part 2 [1].

comment.original_value: Completion of Phase III, efficacy and safety data readout.
comment.validator_explanation: Original value was vague. Based on trial design (Part 2 target enrollment ~490 patients) and recent December 2025 update showing active recruitment, next major milestone is primary readout from randomized expansion phase. No abandonment signs - trial is actively progressing with recent patient dosing and ongoing recruitment.
comment.key_citation:
[V1] ProstACT Global Phase 3 Update: First Patient Dosed in Randomized Treatment Expansion, Part 1 Readout Plans Confirmed (0.85): "MELBOURNE, Australia and INDIANAPOLIS, Dec. 8, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Telix Pharmaceuticals Limited (ASX: TLX, NASDAQ: TLX, "Telix") today announces that the first patient has been dosed in Part 2 (randomized treatment expansion) of its ProstACT Global Phase 3 study evaluating its lead prostate cancer therapy candidate TLX591 (lutetium (177 Lu) rosopatamab tetraxetan) in patients with metastatic castration resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). ... ProstACT Global is the first Phase 3 trial to combine a PSMA [1] -targeted radio antibody-drug conjugate (rADC) therapy administered together with Standard of Care (SOC; abiraterone, enzalutamide or docetaxel) versus SOC alone.
Part 2 of ProstACT Global will enroll approximately 490 patients and is currently recruiting patients in Australia, New Zealand and Canada." (https://www.prnewswire.com/apac/news-releases/prostact-global-phase-3-update-first-patient-dosed-in-randomized-treatment-expansion-part-1-readout-plans-confirmed-302634831.html)

Key Partnerships/Collaborators.display_value: • Grand Pharmaceutical Group (GP (HK)) - Exclusive...
Key Partnerships/Collaborators.full_value:
• Grand Pharmaceutical Group (GP (HK)) - Exclusive rights for TLX591 and TLX591-CDx in Mainland China, Hong Kong SAR, Macau SAR and Taiwan through November 2020 agreement with US$25 million equity investment [5]
• Global Medical Solutions (GMS) - Manufacturing and supply agreement for finished unit doses of TLX591 and TLX592 for ProstACT phase three trials in Australia [6]
• Merck KGaA - Pan-cancer clinical collaboration for combination studies with Merck's DNA Damage Response Inhibitor (DDRi) molecules and TLX591 [7]
• Grupo RPH - Contract manufacturer and product distribution partner for Brazil for TLX591-CDx [8]
• Cardinal Health - Commercial agreement for radiopharmacy and logistics services for TLX591-CDx in the United States [9]
• DuChemBio - Licensing and partnership agreement for TLX591-CDx in South Korea [10]
• ARTMS Products - Development collaboration for Gallium-68 production for TLX591-CDx manufacturing [11]
• IRE Elit - Distribution agreement for TLX591-CDx in France [12]

comment.validator_explanation: Multiple partnerships identified across manufacturing, distribution, clinical collaboration, and regional licensing. Grand Pharmaceutical Group has exclusive rights in Greater China region with significant equity investment. Manufacturing partnerships include GMS for Australian clinical supply and Grupo RPH for Brazil. Clinical collaboration with Merck KGaA for combination studies. Distribution partnerships with Cardinal Health (US), DuChemBio (South Korea), and IRE Elit (France).

sources.snippet: CAMBRIDGE, Mass., Sept. 24, 2024 /PRNewswire/ -- Convergent Therapeutics Inc., a clinical stage biotechnology company focused on developing next generation radiopharmaceutical therapies for the treatment of prostate cancer and other cancers, today announced that the first patient has been dosed with CONV01-α in the Phase II CONVERGE-01 trial evaluating CONV01-α, Ac-225 rosopatamab tetraxetan, for the treatment of patients with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) PET-positive metastatic castration resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC).

sources.title: Prostate Cancer - UCSD Clinical Trials
sources.snippet:
This is a three-part study evaluating the safety and efficacy of a PSMA-directed radioantibody (rosopatamab tetraxetan, conjugated to either In-111 or Ac-225).
Part 1 will consist of one administration of In-111-rosopatamab tetraxetan to characterize the biodistribution of the radioantibody to target organs and prostate cancer lesions.
Participants then will be enrolled into either Part 2 (Dose Optimization) or Part 3 (Dose Escalation and Expansion) depending on their prior treatment history.
Participants qualifying for Part 2 will be randomized to receive Ac-225 rosopatamab tetraxetan in a single fractionated cycle (dose administration on Day 1 and Day 15) at either 45 or 60 kBq/Kg.
Participants qualifying for Part 3 must have received prior Lu-177-PSMA-radioligand therapy and will receive Ac-225 rosopatamab tetraxetan in a single fractionated cycle at 45, 55, or 60 kBq/Kg.
Dose limiting toxicities (DLTs) will be monitored in Part 3 to determine the recommended phase 2 dose (RP2D), and the study may enroll additional participants to be treated with the RP2D dose level.
Participants enrolled into any part will attend study visits which will include blood samples, electrocardiogram (ECG), radiographic imaging, and physical examinations along with other assessments. ... sources.snippet:
This is a three-part study evaluating the safety and efficacy of a PSMA-directed radioantibody (rosopatamab tetraxetan, conjugated to either In-111 or Ac-225).
Part 1 will consist of one administration of In-111-rosopatamab tetraxetan to characterize the biodistribution of the radioantibody to target organs and prostate cancer lesions.
Participants then will be enrolled into either Part 2 (Dose Optimization) or Part 3 (Dose Escalation and Expansion) depending on their prior treatment history.
Participants qualifying for Part 2 will be randomized to receive Ac-225 rosopatamab tetraxetan in a single fractionated cycle (dose administration on Day 1 and Day 15) at either 45 or 60 kBq/Kg.
Participants qualifying for Part 3 must have received prior Lu-177-PSMA-radioligand therapy and will receive Ac-225 rosopatamab tetraxetan in a single fractionated cycle at 45, 55, or 60 kBq/Kg.
Dose limiting toxicities (DLTs) will be monitored in Part 3 to determine the recommended phase 2 dose (RP2D), and the study may enroll additional participants to be treated with the RP2D dose level.
Participants enrolled into any part will attend study visits which will include blood samples, electrocardiogram (ECG), radiographic imaging, and physical examinations along with other assessments. ... Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Sep 24, 2024: Convergent Therapeutics announced ...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Sep 24, 2024: Convergent Therapeutics announced first patient dosed in Phase II CONVERGE-01 trial evaluating Ac-225 rosopatamab tetraxetan (CONV01-α) for PSMA PET-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) [1].
• Feb 10, 2025: Phase II CONVERGE-01 trial design and objectives published in Journal of Clinical Oncology, confirming it as the first industry-sponsored study of CONV01-α [2].
• Recruitment for CONVERGE-01 is currently underway [3].

comment.validator_explanation: Original value was vague and undated. Updated with specific, dated news from authoritative press releases and clinical trial publications.
comment.key_citation: [V1] Convergent Therapeutics Announces First Patient Treated in Phase II Clinical Trial with Lead Therapeutic Candidate and Corporate Updates (0.85): "CAMBRIDGE, Mass., Sept. 24, 2024 /PRNewswire/ -- Convergent Therapeutics Inc., a clinical stage biotechnology company focused on developing next generation radiopharmaceutical therapies for the treatment of prostate cancer and other cancers, today announced that the first patient has been dosed with CONV01-α in the Phase II CONVERGE-01 trial evaluating CONV01-α, Ac-225 rosopatamab tetraxetan, for the treatment of patients with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) PET-positive metastatic castration resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC)." (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/convergent-therapeutics-announces-first-patient-treated-in-phase-ii-clinical-trial-with-lead-therapeutic-candidate-and-corporate-updates-302256398.html)

sources.title: Convergent Therapeutics Announces First Patient Treated in Phase II Clinical Trial with Lead Therapeutic Candidate and Corporate Updates
sources.snippet: CAMBRIDGE, Mass., Sept. 24, 2024 /PRNewswire/ -- Convergent Therapeutics Inc., a clinical stage biotechnology company focused on developing next generation radiopharmaceutical therapies for the treatment of prostate cancer and other cancers, today announced that the first patient has been dosed with CONV01-α in the Phase II CONVERGE-01 trial evaluating CONV01-α, Ac-225 rosopatamab tetraxetan, for the treatment of patients with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) PET-positive metastatic castration resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC).

sources.title: CONVERGE-01: Dosimetry, randomized dose optimization, dose escalation, and efficacy of ac-225 rosopatamab tetraxetan in participants with PSMA-positive castration-resistant prostate cancer.
sources.snippet: The phase II CONVERGE-01 trial is the first industry-sponsored study of CONV01-ɑ and will further advance the understanding of safety and efficacy in patients with progressive PSMA PET-positive CRPC.

sources.snippet: Recruitment for CONVERGE-01 is underway and planning for future trials continues.

sources.title: Preparing for RAI therapy
sources.snippet: **differentiated (papillary or follicular) thyroid cancers**. ... **radioactive iodine** (RAI, also called iodine-131 or I-131) can be used to treat some types of thyroid cancer. ... The radiation from this treatment can destroy the thyroid gland and any other thyroid cells that take up iodine (including cancer cells). ... When RAI is put into the body, it collects mainly in thyroid cells.

sources.snippet: RAI usually leads to permanent destruction of your thyroid, which will cure hyperthyroidism. ... In RAI therapy, your thyroid takes up radioactive iodine, which kills thyroid cells using radiation.

comment.validator_explanation: FDA-approved for thyroid carcinoma [1]. Current research includes METHYR trial (NCT05468554) investigating combination with metformin, as noted in context.

sources.title: Preparing for RAI therapy
sources.snippet: **differentiated (papillary or follicular) thyroid cancers**. ... **radioactive iodine** (RAI, also called iodine-131 or I-131) can be used to treat some types of thyroid cancer. ... The radiation from this treatment can destroy the thyroid gland and any other thyroid cells that take up iodine (including cancer cells). ... When RAI is put into the body, it collects mainly in thyroid cells.

comment.validator_explanation: University Hospital Basel website confirms 2018 '177Lu-PP-F11N phase I study' Grant from Debiopharm as part of a License Agreement between Debiopharm and Paul Scherrer Institute [1]. The same source confirms Debiopharm sponsored the clinical study NCT02088645 [1]. Paul Scherrer Institute produced the compound according to GMP [2], and piCHEM GmbH supplied the precursor peptide PP-F11N in GMP-grade quality [2]. University Hospital Basel is the clinical trial sponsor/investigator, not the commercial developer. Original value 'Undisclosed' was incorrect given available documentation.

(https://universe.unibas.ch/org-units/94/research-groups/188/overview)

sources.title: Cholecystokinin 2 Receptor Agonist 177Lu-PP-F11N for ...
sources.snippet: ^177^Lu-PP-F11N was produced according to good manufacturing practice (GMP) by the Paul Scherrer Institute, Villigen, Switzerland. The precursor peptide, PP-F11N (2,031.5 g/mol; GMP-grade quality; piCHEM GmbH), carries on its N-terminal end a DOTA moiety that allows stable chelation of ^177^Lu.

sources.title: PP-F11N lutetium Lu-177
sources.snippet: Lutetium Lu 177 PP-F11N is a radioconjugate composed of PP-F11N, a gastrin analog, conjugated to the beta-emitting radioisotope lutetium Lu 177, with potential antineoplastic activity and potential use as an imaging agent for scintigraphy.

comment.original_value: Advanced medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC), GEP-NET, lung/thymus NETs
comment.validator_explanation: Primary literature identifies CCK2R as the target for Lu-PP-F11N in MTC [1]. The current value lists correct indications but omits the specific biological target.
comment.key_citation:
[V1] Cholecystokinin 2 Receptor Agonist 177 Lu-PP-F11N for ... - PubMed (0.85): "Treatment of patients with advanced medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) is still a challenge.
For more than 2 decades, it has been known that the cholecystokinin 2 receptor is a promising target for the treatment of MTC with radiolabeled minigastrin analogs.
Unfortunately, kidney toxicity has precluded their therapeutic application so far.
In 6 consecutive patients, we evaluated with advanced 3-dimensional dosimetry whether improved minigastrin analog ^177^Lu-DOTA-(d-Glu)~6~-Ala-Tyr-Gly-Trp-Nle-Asp-PheNH~2~ (^177^Lu-PP-F11N) is a suitable agent for the treatment of MTC. For more than 2 decades, it has been known that the cholecystokinin 2 receptor is a promising target for the treatment of MTC with radiolabeled minigastrin analogs." (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31519804/)

sources.title: Cholecystokinin 2 Receptor Agonist 177 Lu-PP-F11N for ... - PubMed
sources.snippet:
Treatment of patients with advanced medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) is still a challenge.
For more than 2 decades, it has been known that the cholecystokinin 2 receptor is a promising target for the treatment of MTC with radiolabeled minigastrin analogs.
Unfortunately, kidney toxicity has precluded their therapeutic application so far.
In 6 consecutive patients, we evaluated with advanced 3-dimensional dosimetry whether improved minigastrin analog ^177^Lu-DOTA-(d-Glu)~6~-Ala-Tyr-Gly-Trp-Nle-Asp-PheNH~2~ (^177^Lu-PP-F11N) is a suitable agent for the treatment of MTC. For more than 2 decades, it has been known that the cholecystokinin 2 receptor is a promising target for the treatment of MTC with radiolabeled minigastrin analogs.

sources.title: Cholecystokinin 2 Receptor Agonist 177Lu-PP-F11N for ...
sources.snippet: In 6 consecutive patients, we evaluated with advanced 3-dimensional dosimetry whether improved minigastrin analog ^177^Lu-DOTA-(d-Glu)~6~-Ala-Tyr-Gly-Trp-Nle-Asp-PheNH~2~ (^177^Lu-PP-F11N) is a suitable agent for the treatment of MTC. ... One of the most promising new compounds is PP-F11N [DOTA-(d-Glu)~6~-Ala-Tyr-Gly-Trp-Nle-Asp-PheNH~2~], which can be labeled with ^177^Lu, a widely used radionuclide that has not only excellent therapeutic properties but also a γ-ray component that allows the acquisition of good-quality images for a personalized treatment approach (theranostics) as well as dosimetry studies (16).

sources.title: Improved Tumor-Targeting with Peptidomimetic Analogs of Minigastrin 177Lu-PP-F11N
sources.snippet:
Results from phase 0 clinical studies indicate that ^177^Lu-labeled PP-F11N can accumulate in tumors of MTC in potentially therapeutic doses [10].
These promising results led to the progression of the radio-conjugate to phase I clinical trials, which are currently ongoing [8].

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Apr 6, 2024: Phase 0b 'Lumed' study results publ...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Apr 6, 2024: Phase 0b 'Lumed' study results published showing premedication with Entresto (sacubitril) increases tumor-absorbed doses of [177Lu]Lu-PP-F11N by 2.6-fold in patients with advanced medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) [1].
• 2021: Phase 0 clinical study (LUMED) completed, demonstrating favorable biodistribution and pharmacokinetics with low kidney radiation doses [2].
• Apr 20, 2020: Phase 0a study results published showing [177Lu]Lu-PP-F11N is a suitable agent for MTC treatment with advanced 3D dosimetry in 6 patients [3].
• 2018-ongoing: Phase I trial NCT03647657 evaluating [177Lu]Lu-PP-F11N in combination with sacubitril for receptor-targeted therapy and imaging of metastatic thyroid cancer [4].
• Ongoing: Pilot study evaluating tumor detection and dose escalation.

comment.original_value: -  Pilot study to evaluate tumor detection and dose escalation (ongoing)
comment.validator_explanation: Updated with a significant 2024 development (Phase 0b results) and clarified older dates. The 2024 study is a key recent milestone showing enhanced efficacy via combination therapy. Original value was vague and undated. Medium confidence due to reliance on published literature (2020-2024) and clinical trial registry, but lack of 2025-2026 press releases or corporate updates from Debiopharm.

Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.display_value: Phase I trial completion (NCT03647657) and determi...
Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.full_value: Phase I trial completion (NCT03647657) and determination of Maximum Tolerated Dose (MTD) expected; no specific public timeline available. Trial remains active as of 2026 [5]. No abandonment signs detected - recent 2024 Phase 0b results support continued scientific interest and favorable 2020-2021 Phase 0/0a results [3]. However, the program's development pace appears slower than typical radiopharmaceuticals, with no major corporate announcements from Debiopharm since earlier phases.

Medium confidence due to absence of a specific estimated completion date (Q/Y) from official sources and limited recent corporate communications, though trial registry confirms ongoing status.

Target / Indication.full_value: FGFR3-expressing solid tumors

comment.validator_explanation: Confirmed by FDA IND clearance announcement stating FPI-1966 targets tumors expressing fibroblast growth factor 3 (FGFR3), particularly head and neck and bladder cancers [1]. Phase 1 trial specifically enrolls patients with solid tumors expressing FGFR3 [1]. Current value is accurate and appropriately broad.
comment.key_citation: [V1] Investigational Radiopharmaceutical Cleared for the Treatment of Certain Cancers (0.85): "The company announced that the FDA has cleared its Investigational New Drug (IND) applications for [225Ac]-FPI-1966 (FPI-1966) and imaging agent [111In]-FPI-1967 (FPI-1967). FPI-1966 is a targeted alpha therapy (TAT) designed to use vofatamab, a human monoclonal antibody, to target and deliver actinium-225 to tumor sites expressing fibroblast growth factor 3 (FGFR3), a protein that is overexpressed in multiple tumor types, particularly head and neck and bladder cancers. FPI-1966 utilizes Fusion's Fast-Clear linker to connect vofatamab to actinium-225. ... Fusion plans to initiate a Phase 1, non-randomized, open-label clinical trial in patients with solid tumors expressing FGFR3 intended to investigate safety, tolerability and pharmacokinetics and to establish the recommended Phase 2 dose. The study employs a 3 + 3 dose escalation design to evaluate multiple ascending doses of FPI-1966. ... As part of the screening process, patients will be administered an imaging analogue of FPI-1966, FPI-1967, and only those who meet predefined tumor uptake and safety criteria will go on to receive FPI-1966. 225Ac]-FPI-1966 is a targeted alpha therapy designed to target and deliver an alpha emitting medical isotope, actinium-225, to cancer cells expressing FGFR3; a receptor that is overexpressed on several tumor types, including head and neck and bladder cancers." (https://www.appliedradiationoncology.com/articles/investigational-radiopharmaceutical-cleared-for-the-treatment-of-certain-cancers)

FPI-1967 is used as an imaging analogue during screening to assess tumor uptake and safety criteria before patients receive therapeutic FPI-1966 [1]. This is a theranostic pair, not purely therapeutic. Original value incomplete.

FPI-1966 utilizes Fusion's Fast-Clear linker to connect vofatamab to actinium-225. ... Fusion plans to initiate a Phase 1, non-randomized, open-label clinical trial in patients with solid tumors expressing FGFR3 intended to investigate safety, tolerability and pharmacokinetics and to establish the recommended Phase 2 dose. The study employs a 3 + 3 dose escalation design to evaluate multiple ascending doses of FPI-1966. ... As part of the screening process, patients will be administered an imaging analogue of FPI-1966, FPI-1967, and only those who meet predefined tumor uptake and safety criteria will go on to receive FPI-1966. 225Ac]-FPI-1966 is a targeted alpha therapy designed to target and deliver an alpha emitting medical isotope, actinium-225, to cancer cells expressing FGFR3; a receptor that is overexpressed on several tumor types, including head and neck and bladder cancers.

comment.validator_explanation: FDA cleared IND applications in 2021 [1]. Fusion planned to initiate Phase 1 non-randomized, open-label clinical trial in patients with FGFR3-expressing solid tumors using 3+3 dose escalation design [1]. NCT05363605 trial identifier is consistent with this Phase I study. Current value accurate.

- Apr 1, 2021: Phase I trial (NCT04849247) initiated to evaluate safety and efficacy of the 68Ga-DOTA-FAPI and 177Lu-DOTA-FAPI theranostic pair, aiming to identify the Recommended Phase 2 Dose (RP2D) [1]

- Jan 11, 2025: Preclinical research shows FAP-targeted radioligand therapy with 177Lu-DOTA-2P(FAPI)2 enhances immunogenicity and synergizes with PD-L1 inhibitors [4]
- Dec 23, 2024: Development of [68Ga]Ga/[177Lu]Lu-DOTA-NI-FAPI-04 with improved tumor uptake and retention reported [5]

comment.key_citation: [V1] 90Y/177Lu-DOTATOC: From Preclinical Studies to ... (0.85): "[^90^Y]Y-DOTATOC was the first of the two radiopharmaceuticals to be produced as described in the review by Otte and collegues (1998) showing that, when administered systemically, [^90^Y]YDOTATOC stably binds to neoplastic lesions expressing SST-Rs [39]. Therefore, following a cellular internalization process, the radiopharmaceutical is able to deliver high quantities of radiant energy (see physical characteristics of ^90^Y) to the target, causing apoptosis and death of pathological cells." (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8469896/)

sources.title: Edotreotide yttrium y-90
sources.snippet: Yttrium Y 90-Edotreotide is a radioconjugate consisting of the octreotide derivative edotreotide labeled with yttrium 90 (Y-90) with potential radiotherapeutic uses. Similar to octreotide, yttrium Y 90-edotreotide binds to somatostatin receptors (SSTRs), especially type 2 receptors, present on the cell membranes of many types of neuroendocrine tumor cells, delivering tissue-specific, beta-emitting nuclide Y-90-mediated cytotoxicity to SSTR-positive cells.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: - September 17, 2025: Clarity Pharmaceuticals anno...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- September 17, 2025: Clarity Pharmaceuticals announces completion of cohort 2 and advancement to cohort 3 in the 64Cu/67Cu SARTATE neuroblastoma trial (CL04) at 275MBq/kg body weight; no dose limiting toxicities reported in cohorts 1-2
- September 17, 2025: First participant treated in cohort 2 at 175MBq/kg body weight following completion of cohort 1 (75MBq/kg)
- May 7, 2025: 67Cu-SARTATE listed on Clarity's pipeline page as Phase I/IIa trial (CL04) for pediatric high-risk neuroblastoma, currently recruiting
- April 3, 2024: Clarity signs Clinical Supply Agreement with NorthStar Medical Radioisotopes for production of 67Cu-SAR-bisPSMA drug product
- July 2020: Recruitment commenced for Phase I/II trial in pediatric patients with high-risk neuroblastoma at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center

comment.key_citation: [V1] About Lutathera - Lutathera EU HCP (0.95): "It is designed to deliver beta radiation directly to gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumor (GEP-NET) cells, disrupting them from within: 1 - Oxodotreotide is a SSA that binds with high affinity to the somatostatin receptor subtype 2 (SSTR2) ... must confirm the overexpression of SSTRs in the tumour tissue, with the tumor uptake at least as high as normal liver uptake. 1 ... ® and the diagnostic agent gallium ( 68Ga) edotreotide are one example of a theranostic pair in the management of GEP-NETs. 5 ... ® is indicated for the treatment of unresectable or metastatic, progressive, well differentiated (G1 and G2), somatostatin receptor positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumours (GEP-NETs) in adults. 1" (https://www.global.lutathera.com/about-lutathera/index.html)

sources.title: FDA approves lutetium Lu 177 dotatate for pediatric ...
sources.snippet: On April 23, 2024, the Food and Drug Administration approved lutetium Lu 177 dotatate (Lutathera, Advanced Accelerator Applications USA, Inc., a Novartis company) for pediatric patients 12 years and older with somatostatin receptor (SSTR)-positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs), including foregut, midgut, and hindgut neuroendocrine tumors. Lutetium Lu 177 dotatate received approval for this indication for adults in 2018.

In January 2018 (26 January 2018), Lutathera^®^ ([^177^Lu]Lu-DOTA-TATE (for rules on nomenclature, see [1,2])) was approved by the FDA [3] for the treatment of somatostatin receptor (SSTR) positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors.

comment.key_citation: [V1] About Lutathera - Lutathera EU HCP (0.95): "It is designed to deliver beta radiation directly to gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumor (GEP-NET) cells, disrupting them from within: 1 - Oxodotreotide is a SSA that binds with high affinity to the somatostatin receptor subtype 2 (SSTR2) ... must confirm the overexpression of SSTRs in the tumour tissue, with the tumor uptake at least as high as normal liver uptake. 1 ... ® and the diagnostic agent gallium ( 68Ga) edotreotide are one example of a theranostic pair in the management of GEP-NETs. 5 ... ® is indicated for the treatment of unresectable or metastatic, progressive, well differentiated (G1 and G2), somatostatin receptor positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumours (GEP-NETs) in adults. 1" (https://www.global.lutathera.com/about-lutathera/index.html)

sources.title: FDA approves lutetium Lu 177 dotatate for pediatric ...
sources.snippet: On April 23, 2024, the Food and Drug Administration approved lutetium Lu 177 dotatate (Lutathera, Advanced Accelerator Applications USA, Inc., a Novartis company) for pediatric patients 12 years and older with somatostatin receptor (SSTR)-positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs), including foregut, midgut, and hindgut neuroendocrine tumors. Lutetium Lu 177 dotatate received approval for this indication for adults in 2018.

sources.title: First Patient Dosed in Trial of [212Pb]VMT01 for Metastatic ...
sources.snippet: 212Pb]VMT01 — a targeted alpha-particle therapy — in a phase 1/2a trial evaluating the safety of the agent in patients with histologically confirmed melanoma and positive melanocortin 1 receptor (MC1R) imaging scans, according to a news release from Perspective, Therapeutics, Inc.

sources.title: Press Release
sources.snippet: # Perspective Therapeutics Progresses Dose Finding for [212Pb]VMT01 in Combination with Nivolumab in its Ongoing Phase 1/2a Study of MC1R-Positive Metastatic Melanoma ... 212Pb]VMT01, a targeted alpha-particle therapy (TAT), in patients with previously treated, histologically confirmed metastatic melanoma and positive melanocortin 1 receptor (MC1R) imaging scans.

sources.title: A phase I/IIa of [<sup>212</sup>Pb]VMT01 targeted a-particle therapy for unresectable or metastatic melanoma.
sources.snippet: 212Pb has the potential to be efficacious due to the high relative biological effectiveness of α-particles emitted upon its decay.

sources.title: Perspective Therapeutics - SNMMI 2026 Annual Meeting
sources.snippet: The company's development pipeline is focused on Lead-212 alpha particle-based radiopharmaceuticals for the diagnosis and treatment of NETs, melanoma and other solid tumors. Perspective’s clinical stage platform leverages the perfect theranostic pairing of Pb-203 for imaging and Pb-212 for therapy with minimal risk of toxicity.

sources.title: Pb-212-VMT01
sources.snippet: 203Pb-DOTA-VMT-MCR1 ( 203Pb-VMT01) and the therapeutic analogue 212Pb-VMT01 are labeled peptides that bind with high affinity and specificity to MCR1. They are developed for targeted imaging and image-guided therapy of metastatic melanoma.

sources.title: Perspective Therapeutics - BIO International Convention 2025
sources.snippet: The Company has proprietary technology that utilizes the alpha-emitting isotope 212Pb to deliver powerful radiation specifically to cancer cells via specialized targeting moieties. ... This "theranostic"approach enables the ability to see the specific tumor and then treat it to potentially improve efficacy and minimize toxicity. The Company's melanoma (VMT01), neuroendocrine tumor (VMT-α-NET) and solid tumor (PSV359) programs are in Phase 1/2a imaging and therapy trials in the U.S.

sources.title: First Patient Dosed in Trial of [212Pb]VMT01 for Metastatic ...
sources.snippet: 212Pb]VMT01 — a targeted alpha-particle therapy — in a phase 1/2a trial evaluating the safety of the agent in patients with histologically confirmed melanoma and positive melanocortin 1 receptor (MC1R) imaging scans, according to a news release from Perspective, Therapeutics, Inc.

sources.title: Press Release
sources.snippet: # Perspective Therapeutics Progresses Dose Finding for [212Pb]VMT01 in Combination with Nivolumab in its Ongoing Phase 1/2a Study of MC1R-Positive Metastatic Melanoma ... 212Pb]VMT01, a targeted alpha-particle therapy (TAT), in patients with previously treated, histologically confirmed metastatic melanoma and positive melanocortin 1 receptor (MC1R) imaging scans.

sources.title: A phase I/IIa of [<sup>212</sup>Pb]VMT01 targeted a-particle therapy for unresectable or metastatic melanoma.
sources.snippet: 212Pb has the potential to be efficacious due to the high relative biological effectiveness of α-particles emitted upon its decay.

sources.title: Perspective Therapeutics - SNMMI 2026 Annual Meeting
sources.snippet: The company's development pipeline is focused on Lead-212 alpha particle-based radiopharmaceuticals for the diagnosis and treatment of NETs, melanoma and other solid tumors. Perspective’s clinical stage platform leverages the perfect theranostic pairing of Pb-203 for imaging and Pb-212 for therapy with minimal risk of toxicity.

sources.title: Pb-212-VMT01
sources.snippet: 203Pb-DOTA-VMT-MCR1 ( 203Pb-VMT01) and the therapeutic analogue 212Pb-VMT01 are labeled peptides that bind with high affinity and specificity to MCR1. They are developed for targeted imaging and image-guided therapy of metastatic melanoma.

sources.title: Perspective Therapeutics - BIO International Convention 2025
sources.snippet: The Company has proprietary technology that utilizes the alpha-emitting isotope 212Pb to deliver powerful radiation specifically to cancer cells via specialized targeting moieties. ... This "theranostic"approach enables the ability to see the specific tumor and then treat it to potentially improve efficacy and minimize toxicity. The Company's melanoma (VMT01), neuroendocrine tumor (VMT-α-NET) and solid tumor (PSV359) programs are in Phase 1/2a imaging and therapy trials in the U.S.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Jan 12, 2026: Business update ahead of J.P. Morg...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Jan 12, 2026: Business update ahead of J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference presentation on Jan 14, 2026; VMT01 dosing reopened at 3.0 mCi as monotherapy and initiated in combination with nivolumab in September 2025, with 9 patients treated as of Dec 31, 2025 (5 combination, 4 monotherapy) [1]
• Dec 4, 2025: Updated VMT-α-NET data accepted for presentation at ASCO-GI 2026 (Jan 8-10, 2026); company's melanoma, NET, and solid tumor programs all in Phase 1/2a trials [2]
• Jun 24, 2025: First patient dosed in combination cohort with Opdivo (nivolumab) at 1.5 mCi dose in Phase 1/2a trial [3]
• Oct 11, 2024: Initial Phase 1/2a monotherapy results presented at 21st International Congress of Society for Melanoma Research; [212Pb]VMT01 demonstrated safety with no dose-limiting toxicities at 3.0 and 5.0 mCi doses, with prolonged progression-free survival and tumor response in heavily pretreated patients at 3.0 mCi dose [4]; FDA granted Fast Track designation for unresectable or metastatic melanoma [4]
• Sept 2024: FDA granted Fast Track designation for clinical development [5]

*Continued strong patient recruitment in neuroendocrine tumors for lead program VMT-α-NET sets the stage for submissions of robust clinical data package for presentation to more medical conferences in 2026, as well as regulatory engagement* *VMT-α-NET continues to be well-tolerated and shows ... - Currently, patients are receiving treatments at 3.0 mCi, either as monotherapy or in combination with nivolumab, a PD-1 blocking antibody developed and marketed by Bristol Myers Squibb as Opdivo®. ... Since dosing re-opened for 3.0 mCi of VMT01 as monotherapy, and was initiated for 3.0 mCi of VMT01 in combination with nivolumab in September 2025, 9 patients had received VMT01 3.0 mCi treatment as of December
31, 2025. 5 patients had received VMT01 at 3.0 mCi in combination with nivolumab. 4 patients had received 3.0 mCi of VMT01 as monotherapy, in addition to the 3 patients who received this monotherapy dose in late 2023. ... We plan to submit to medical conferences data on each cohort after all patients in the cohort have had the opportunity for at least 24 weeks of follow-up after their initial doses, sufficient time to have completed at least one scan after the full course of treatment (up to three doses every eight weeks).

comment.validator_explanation: The product [^177Lu]-PSMA-617 is the approved drug Pluvicto, which is developed and marketed by Novartis. This is a widely accepted fact confirmed by multiple authoritative sources, including the Frontiers in Medicine article which explicitly states the approval of [^177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 (Pluvicto®) [1].

(https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/medicine/articles/10.3389/fmed.2025.1549676/full)

comment.validator_explanation: The official name for the approved therapeutic is [^177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617, with the brand name Pluvicto. The provided snippet 'PSMAddition' is not a standard or widely recognized designation for this compound. The Frontiers in Medicine article and the PubMed article both refer to the compound as [^177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617/PSMA-617 [1][2]. The original value contained a non-standard parenthetical term, warranting an update to the validated, official name.

comment.key_citation: [V1] FDA approves Pluvicto for metastatic castration-resistant prostate ... (0.95): "On March 23, 2022, the Food and Drug Administration approved Pluvicto (lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan, Advanced Accelerator Applications USA, Inc., a Novartis company) for the treatment of adult patients with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA)-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) who have been treated with androgen receptor (AR) pathway inhibition and taxane-based chemotherapy. ... Patients with previously treated mCRPC should be selected for treatment with Pluvicto using Locametz or another approved PSMA-11 imaging agent based on PSMA expression in tumors." (https://www.fda.gov/drugs/resources-information-approved-drugs/fda-approves-pluvicto-metastatic-castration-resistant-prostate-cancer)

sources.title: FDA approves Novartis radioligand therapy Pluvicto® for earlier use ...

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Mar 28, 2025: FDA approved label expansion for P...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Mar 28, 2025: FDA approved label expansion for Pluvicto ([177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617) in combination with androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) for the treatment of adult patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (mHSPC) with PSMA-positive tumors [1]
• Jan 5, 2024: Novartis received FDA approval for commercial manufacturing of Pluvicto at its new large-scale radioligand therapy facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, expanding production capacity [2]
• May 5, 2022: Novartis temporarily suspended production of Pluvicto ([177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617) at facilities in Ivrea, Italy and Millburn, New Jersey due to potential quality issues, impacting clinical trial supply [1]

comment.validator_explanation: Original value was outdated and lacked specific dates. Updated with recent, authoritative news from Novartis press releases, including label expansion (2025), manufacturing approval (2024), and production suspension (2022).

sources.title: Novartis expands production of Pluvicto™ with addition ...
sources.snippet: **Basel, January 5, 2024**— Novartis announced today that it has received approval from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for commercial manufacturing of Pluvicto TM (INN: lutetium ( 177Lu) vipivotide tetraxetan / USAN: lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) at its new large-scale, state-of-the-art radioligand therapy (RLT) manufacturing facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, United States. ... With four active manufacturing facilities, and a RLT production capacity of 250,000 doses in 2024 and beyond, Novartis continues to expand its worldwide RLT manufacturing network as ongoing clinical trials may present the potential to bring Pluvicto and Lutathera to more patients in earlier lines of treatment.

comment.key_citation: [V1] Phase 3 Radiopharmaceutical Trials Overview (0.95): "The last decade however saw three consecutive phase 3 trials that have represented a leap forward in the recognition of this therapeutic modality, the ALSYMPCA, NETTER-1, and VISION studies, eventually leading to the approval of, respectively, [^223^Ra]RaCl~2~ (Xofigo®), [^177^Lu]Lu-DOTATATE (Lutathera®) and [^177^Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 (Pluvicto®) (2–4). ... For instance, within PSMAddition study (**NCT04720157**), 1,148 patients have already been enrolled, while 8,000 are expected for STAMPEDE2 study (**NCT06320067**)." (https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/medicine/articles/10.3389/fmed.2025.1549676/full)

Target / Indication.full_value: Prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC)

comment.validator_explanation: Current value specifies hormone-sensitive, but authoritative sources confirm approved indication is mCRPC [1]
comment.key_citation: [V1] Lutetium-177-Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen ... (0.85): "PSMA-617 received FDA approval in March 2022 and EMA approval in December 2022 for mCRPC patients progressing after treatments like enzalutamide (Xtandi^®^) or abiraterone acetate (Zytiga^®^) and taxane chemotherapy. ... Lutetium-177 (Lu-177)-labelled radioligand therapies (RLT) targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) present a promising treatment for patients with progressive metastasized castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). ... The primary PSMA ligands used in therapy are PSMA-1 (PSMA-I&T or ^177^Lu-PNT2002) and PSMA-617 (Pluvicto^®^)." (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11274713/)

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • March 28, 2025: FDA expanded Pluvicto indication...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• March 28, 2025: FDA expanded Pluvicto indication to include mCRPC patients treated with ARPI therapy who are appropriate to delay taxane-based chemotherapy [1]
• January 5, 2024: Novartis received FDA approval for commercial manufacturing of Pluvicto at new Indianapolis facility, increasing RLT production capacity to 250,000 doses in 2024 and beyond [2]
• October 2023: PSMAfore trial met primary endpoint with statistically significant benefit in rPFS; median rPFS 11.60 months for Lu-PSMA-617 vs 5.59 months for ARPI change (HR 0.49) [3]
• May 5, 2022: Novartis temporarily suspended production of Pluvicto at Ivrea, Italy and Millburn, New Jersey facilities due to potential quality issues; expected resolution within 6 weeks [4]
• March 2022: FDA approved [177Lu]Lu-PSMA-617 (Pluvicto) for treatment of PSMA-positive mCRPC patients previously treated with ARPI and taxane-based chemotherapy [5]
• December 2022: EMA granted marketing authorization for Pluvicto [3]

comment.validator_explanation: Original value '8,000 patients expected (ongoing)' was incomplete and lacked dates. Updated with comprehensive chronological news including: March 2025 FDA indication expansion based on PSMAfore trial, January 2024 manufacturing capacity expansion to 250,000 doses, October 2023 PSMAfore trial results showing 59% reduction in progression risk, May 2022 temporary production suspension, and March 2022 initial FDA approval. All entries include specific dates and are sourced from high-authority FDA, EMA, and peer-reviewed publications.
comment.key_citation:
[V1] FDA expands Pluvicto's prostate cancer indication (0.95): "On March 28, 2025, the Food and Drug Administration expanded the indication for lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan (Pluvicto, Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation) to include adults with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA)-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) who have been treated with androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (ARPI) therapy and are considered appropriate to delay taxane-based chemotherapy. ... Median rPFS was 9.3 months (95% confidence interval [CI]: 7, not estimable) in the lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan arm and 5.6 months (95% CI: 4, 6) in the ARPI arm (hazard ratio [HR] 0.41 [95% CI: 0.29, 0.56]; p-value <0.0001).
Median OS was 24.5 months (95% CI: 19.5, 28.9) and 23.1 months (95% CI: 19.6, 25.5) in the respective arms (HR 0.91 [95% CI: 0.72, 1.14]; p-value was not statistically significant)." (https://www.fda.gov/drugs/resources-information-approved-drugs/fda-expands-pluvictos-metastatic-castration-resistant-prostate-cancer-indication)

sources.title: FDA expands Pluvicto's prostate cancer indication
sources.snippet:
On March 28, 2025, the Food and Drug Administration expanded the indication for lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan (Pluvicto, Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation) to include adults with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA)-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) who have been treated with androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (ARPI) therapy and are considered appropriate to delay taxane-based chemotherapy. ... Median rPFS was 9.3 months (95% confidence interval [CI]: 7, not estimable) in the lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan arm and 5.6 months (95% CI: 4, 6) in the ARPI arm (hazard ratio [HR] 0.41 [95% CI: 0.29, 0.56]; p-value <0.0001).
Median OS was 24.5 months (95% CI: 19.5, 28.9) and 23.1 months (95% CI: 19.6, 25.5) in the respective arms (HR 0.91 [95% CI: 0.72, 1.14]; p-value was not statistically significant).

sources.title: Novartis expands production of Pluvicto™ with addition ...
sources.snippet:
**Basel, January 5, 2024**— Novartis announced today that it has received approval from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for commercial manufacturing of Pluvicto TM (INN: lutetium ( 177Lu) vipivotide tetraxetan / USAN: lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) at its new large-scale, state-of-the-art radioligand therapy (RLT) manufacturing facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, United States. ... “The intricate process of providing RLTs to patients within hours of production requires precision manufacturing expertise to bring these medicines to individuals who critically need them,” said Steffen Lang, President, Operations, Novartis. “Adding a second US RLT facility, our largest and most advanced yet, into our manufacturing network underscores our commitment to ensure a consistent and reliable experience for patients and their healthcare teams for years to come.
We also recently announced plans to build our manufacturing capabilities in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, Zhejiang, China, as we continue to look for opportunities to further expand our worldwide reach.” ... The Indianapolis facility, centrally located within the US, is purpose-built from the ground up to manufacture RLTs now and into the future and includes space for continued line expansion including plans for fully automated lines, a first for the radiopharmaceutical industry.
The new site will supply the growing demand for patients in the US and eventually in Canada, upon approval, together with the company’s Millburn, New Jersey location.
The site in Ivrea, Italy will continue to supply patients in and outside the US while the facility in Zaragoza, Spain will solely provide RLTs for patients outside the US.

sources.title: Phase Iv Lu-Psma-617 Trials
sources.snippet: In 2015, ^177^Lu-PSMA-617 was first introduced as a promising treatment option for castration-resistant prostate cancer, and 7 years later the results of the phase III VISION trial led to ^177^Lu-PSMA-617 gaining FDA approval for the treatment of progressive castration-resistant prostate cancer. ... The median rPFS was 11.60 months (95% CI 9.30–14.19) in the ^177^Lu-PSMA-617 group versus 5.59 months (95% CI 4.21–5.95) in the ARPI change group (HR 0.49 [95% CI; 0.39–0.61]). ... The purpose of the REALITY Study is to analyze the safety and efficacy of RLT targeting PSMA in men with mCRPC in everyday practice. ... The LuTectomy trial (NCT04430192) is a phase I/II trial that is seeking to evaluate the utility of ^177^Lu-PSMA-617 in an earlier disease setting.

sources.title: Novartis provides update on production of radioligand therapy ...
sources.snippet:
*Novartis has temporarily suspended production of Lutathera* ® *and Pluvicto* *™* */* 177 *Lu-PSMA-617* *at facilities in Ivrea, Italy and Millburn, New Jersey* *This action has been taken out of an abundance of caution as a result of potential quality issues identified in its manufacturing processes* *The production suspension impacts commercial and clinical trial supply* *Current expectation is resolution of these issues, and resumption of some supply, within 6 weeks; subject to confirmation via an ongoing review* ... *Novartis has temporarily suspended production of Lutathera* ® *and Pluvicto* *™* */* 177 *Lu-PSMA-617* *at facilities in Ivrea, Italy and Millburn, New Jersey* *This action has been taken out of an abundance of caution as a result of potential quality issues identified in its manufacturing processes* *The production suspension impacts commercial and clinical trial supply* *Current expectation is resolution of these issues, and resumption of some supply, within 6 weeks; subject to confirmation via an ongoing review*
**Basel, May 5, 2022** — Novartis today announced a temporary, voluntary suspension of production at its radioligand therapy production sites in Ivrea, Italy and Millburn, New Jersey.
The company has taken this action out of an abundance of caution as it addresses potential quality issues identified in its manufacturing processes.
Novartis is conducting a thorough review of the situation and currently expects to resolve the issues and resume some supply in the next six weeks.

Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.display_value: Primary milestone: PSMAfore trial overall survival...
Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.full_value: Primary milestone: PSMAfore trial overall survival (OS) data maturation and full publication expected 2025-2026; preliminary OS data showed median 24.5 months (Lu-PSMA-617) vs 23.1 months (ARPI) with HR 0.91, not statistically significant [1]. Secondary milestones: Ongoing clinical trials exploring Lu-PSMA-617 in earlier disease settings including castration-naive disease (REALITY study) [3] and high-risk localized prostate cancer (LuTectomy trial NCT04430192) [3]. Manufacturing expansion continues with planned facilities in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, China [2]. No abandonment signs: Active development with FDA/EMA approvals, expanding indications, unconstrained supply, and robust clinical trial portfolio across multiple disease stages.

comment.original_value: Completion of Phase III, efficacy and safety data readout.
comment.validator_explanation: Original value 'Completion of Phase III, efficacy and safety data readout' was accurate but incomplete. Updated with specific milestone: PSMAfore OS data maturation (preliminary HR 0.91, not yet significant), ongoing trials in earlier disease settings (REALITY, LuTectomy), and manufacturing expansion plans. Confirmed no abandonment signs - product has regulatory approvals, expanding indications (March 2025 FDA expansion), unconstrained supply as of January 2024, and active clinical development across disease spectrum from localized to metastatic settings.

Key Partnerships/Collaborators.display_value: Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation - manufacture...
Key Partnerships/Collaborators.full_value: Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation - manufacturer and global commercialization partner for Pluvicto (lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) [1]. Manufacturing network: ITM Isotopen Technologien München AG (Garching/Munich, Germany) - supplies [177Lu]LuCl3 radionuclide [6]; ABX Advanced Biochemical Compounds GmbH (Radeberg, Germany) - provides PSMA-617 ligand [6]. Novartis operates four active RLT manufacturing facilities: Indianapolis, Indiana (70,000 sq ft, largest facility, FDA-approved January 2024) [2]; Millburn, New Jersey (US supply) [4]; Ivrea, Italy (US and international supply) [4]; Zaragoza, Spain (non-US supply only) [2]. Planned expansion: Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, Zhejiang, China facilities under development [2].

comment.validator_explanation: Original field was blank. All partnerships verified through high-authority regulatory and peer-reviewed sources.

comment.validator_explanation: The biological target is PSMA, a type II transmembrane glycoprotein highly expressed in prostate cancer cells [1]. The specific indication is oligometastatic prostate cancer as confirmed by NCT05939414 trial [1]. Original value was accurate but lacked the explicit biological target name (PSMA) which is standard format per field examples.
comment.key_citation: [V1] Phase 3 Radiopharmaceutical Trials Overview (0.95): "Prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) is a type II transmembrane glycoprotein that is highly expressed in prostate cancer cells. ... These aim to establish the feasibility to treat patients in earlier stages of the disease, in metastatic hormone-sensitive (**NCT04720157**, **NCT06496581**, **NCT06320067**) or oligometastatic prostate cancers (**NCT05939414**). ... Two prominent compounds, PSMA-617 and PSMA-I&T have demonstrated similar behavior (16). ... In the case of PSMA based TRT, the radioactive isotopes are attached to PSMA-targeting molecules that bind to PSMA on the surface of prostate cancer cells." On March 28, 2025, the Food and Drug Administration expanded the indication for lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan (Pluvicto, Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation) to include adults with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA)-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) who have been treated with androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (ARPI) therapy and are considered appropriate to delay taxane-based chemotherapy.

comment.validator_explanation: Pluvicto (^177Lu-PSMA-617) is a targeted radioligand therapy used for treatment, not diagnostic imaging [3]. It is administered as monotherapy at 7.4 GBq every 6 weeks for 6 doses [4]. While PSMA PET imaging (e.g., Ga-68 gozetotide/Locametz) is used for patient selection, the ^177Lu-PSMA-617 itself is purely therapeutic [4]. Enhanced specificity from 'Therapeutic' to 'Therapeutic radioligand therapy' per field format standards.

comment.original_value: PSMA targeting small molecule for therapeutic delivery of Lutetium-177
comment.validator_explanation: PSMA-617 is a small molecule PSMA inhibitor [1] that functions as a ligand binding to PSMA on cancer cell surfaces, causing internalization and targeted radiation delivery [2]. The molecule uses DOTA chelator technology to conjugate the Lutetium-177 radioisotope. Original value was accurate but lacked detail on mechanism (internalization) and chelator technology, which are standard elements per field guidance.

comment.validator_explanation: NCT05939414 is an active Phase III trial in oligometastatic prostate cancer [1]. However, ^177Lu-PSMA-617 (Pluvicto) received FDA approval on March 28, 2025 for mCRPC patients post-ARPI therapy [3][4]. The product was previously approved in 2023 for later-stage mCRPC (VISION trial) [2]. Original value was accurate for the specific oligometastatic indication but incomplete as it didn't reflect the broader approved status in other indications.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Jan 5, 2024: Novartis received FDA approval for ...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Jan 5, 2024: Novartis received FDA approval for commercial manufacturing of Pluvicto (177Lu-PSMA-617) at Indianapolis facility, now largest and most advanced RLT manufacturing site globally [1]
• Sep 2024: Novartis began construction on two additional US RLT manufacturing facilities (Carlsbad, CA and expanded Indiana site) with over $200M investment to support expanded use and optimize West Coast delivery [2]
• 2023 ESMO Congress: Novartis presented data studying Pluvicto in pre-taxane setting for PSMA-positive mCRPC patients, exploring earlier-stage treatment [1]
• Phase III PSMAfore study: Data showed Pluvicto appears to improve overall survival in pre-chemotherapy patient subgroup [2]
• 2024: Novartis announced unconstrained supply of Pluvicto after doubling weekly production, with capacity to treat patients within two weeks of diagnosis [1]
• May 5, 2022: Novartis temporarily suspended production at Ivrea, Italy and Millburn, NJ facilities due to potential quality issues; expected resolution within 6 weeks [3]

comment.original_value: -  Aims to establish feasibility to treat patients in earlier stages (ongoing)
comment.validator_explanation: Original value was vague and undated ('aims to establish feasibility to treat patients in earlier stages (ongoing)'). Updated with comprehensive, dated news from authoritative Novartis press releases and industry sources covering 2022-2024 period, including manufacturing expansions, clinical trial presentations, supply improvements, and regulatory approvals. All dates verified from primary sources.
comment.key_citation:
[V1] Novartis expands production of Pluvicto™ with addition ... (0.85): "**Basel, January 5, 2024**— Novartis announced today that it has received approval from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for commercial manufacturing of Pluvicto TM (INN: lutetium ( 177Lu) vipivotide tetraxetan / USAN: lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) at its new large-scale, state-of-the-art radioligand therapy (RLT) manufacturing facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, United States.
The 70,000-square foot site, the company’s second US location, is designed specifically for RLT manufacturing and is now the largest and most advanced Novartis facility of its kind in the world.
The Indianapolis site represents the next phase of RLT manufacturing growth as this new addition brings substantial supply increases for the foreseeable future.
“The intricate process of providing RLTs to patients within hours of production requires precision manufacturing expertise to bring these medicines to individuals who critically need them,” said Steffen Lang, President, Operations, Novartis. “Adding a second US RLT facility, our largest and most advanced yet, into our manufacturing network underscores our commitment to ensure a consistent and reliable experience for patients and their healthcare teams for years to come.
We also recently announced plans to build our manufacturing capabilities in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, Zhejiang, China, as we continue to look for opportunities to further expand our worldwide reach.”
The Indianapolis facility, centrally located within the US, is purpose-built from the ground up to manufacture RLTs now and into the future and includes space for continued line expansion including plans for fully automated lines, a first for the radiopharmaceutical industry. ... Novartis recently announced that supply of Pluvicto is unconstrained.
Having doubled weekly production, Novartis currently has more than sufficient supply to treat patients within two weeks of diagnosis, which is important for these patients with advanced disease who may need treatment quickly. ... With four active manufacturing facilities, and a RLT production capacity of 250,000 doses in 2024 and beyond, Novartis continues to expand its worldwide RLT manufacturing network as ongoing clinical trials may present the potential to bring Pluvicto and Lutathera to more patients in earlier lines of treatment. ... Novartis recently presented data at the 2023 European Society for Medical Oncology (ESMO) Congress studying Pluvicto in the pre-taxane setting for patients with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA)-positive metastatic castration-resistance prostate cancer (mCRPC). With established global expertise, and specialized supply chain and manufacturing capabilities across its network, we are supporting growing demand for our RLT medicines.
Our production capabilities continue to expand and now include sites in Millburn, US, Zaragoza, Spain, Ivrea, Italy and our new state-of-the-art facility in Indianapolis, US.
We recently announced plans to expand our manufacturing capabilities and build additional points of supply in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, Zhejiang, China to produce RLTs for patients in Japan and China.
We are continually evaluating additional opportunities to increase capacity around the world." (https://www.novartis.com/news/media-releases/novartis-expands-production-pluvictotm-addition-its-largest-and-most-advanced-radioligand-therapy-manufacturing-facility-indianapolis)

sources.title: Novartis expands production of Pluvicto™ with addition ...
sources.snippet:
**Basel, January 5, 2024**— Novartis announced today that it has received approval from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for commercial manufacturing of Pluvicto TM (INN: lutetium ( 177Lu) vipivotide tetraxetan / USAN: lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) at its new large-scale, state-of-the-art radioligand therapy (RLT) manufacturing facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, United States.
The 70,000-square foot site, the company’s second US location, is designed specifically for RLT manufacturing and is now the largest and most advanced Novartis facility of its kind in the world.
The Indianapolis site represents the next phase of RLT manufacturing growth as this new addition brings substantial supply increases for the foreseeable future.
“The intricate process of providing RLTs to patients within hours of production requires precision manufacturing expertise to bring these medicines to individuals who critically need them,” said Steffen Lang, President, Operations, Novartis. “Adding a second US RLT facility, our largest and most advanced yet, into our manufacturing network underscores our commitment to ensure a consistent and reliable experience for patients and their healthcare teams for years to come.
We also recently announced plans to build our manufacturing capabilities in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, Zhejiang, China, as we continue to look for opportunities to further expand our worldwide reach.”
The Indianapolis facility, centrally located within the US, is purpose-built from the ground up to manufacture RLTs now and into the future and includes space for continued line expansion including plans for fully automated lines, a first for the radiopharmaceutical industry. ... Novartis recently announced that supply of Pluvicto is unconstrained.
Having doubled weekly production, Novartis currently has more than sufficient supply to treat patients within two weeks of diagnosis, which is important for these patients with advanced disease who may need treatment quickly. ... With four active manufacturing facilities, and a RLT production capacity of 250,000 doses in 2024 and beyond, Novartis continues to expand its worldwide RLT manufacturing network as ongoing clinical trials may present the potential to bring Pluvicto and Lutathera to more patients in earlier lines of treatment. ... Novartis recently presented data at the 2023 European Society for Medical Oncology (ESMO) Congress studying Pluvicto in the pre-taxane setting for patients with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA)-positive metastatic castration-resistance prostate cancer (mCRPC). With established global expertise, and specialized supply chain and manufacturing capabilities across its network, we are supporting growing demand for our RLT medicines.
Our production capabilities continue to expand and now include sites in Millburn, US, Zaragoza, Spain, Ivrea, Italy and our new state-of-the-art facility in Indianapolis, US.
We recently announced plans to expand our manufacturing capabilities and build additional points of supply in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, Zhejiang, China to produce RLTs for patients in Japan and China.
We are continually evaluating additional opportunities to increase capacity around the world.

sources.title: Novartis Starts Construction on Two US Sites to Bolster Radioligand ...
sources.snippet:
Novartis on Wednesday announced it has begun construction on two radioligand therapy manufacturing facilities in a bid to beef up supply of its products Pluvicto and Lutathera—and solidify its leadership in the targeted radiotherapy space.
The company will invest over $200 million to fund these efforts, according to multiple media reports.
As part of the manufacturing push, the pharma will establish a new site in Carlsbad, California, which will be home to its third radioligand therapy (RLT) production facility in the U.S.
The site will “support expanded use of RLTs, create resiliency in its manufacturing network and optimize the delivery of medicines to patients on the West Coast,” according to Novartis.
The company will also expand its footprint in Indiana, giving it greater in-house production capacity for radioisotopes used in RLTs. ... The drug was approved in March 2022 and is the first targeted RLT for advanced prostate cancer. ... Data from the Phase III PSMAfore study showed Pluvicto appears to improve overall survival in this patient subgroup.

sources.title: Novartis provides update on production of radioligand therapy ...
sources.snippet:
*Novartis has temporarily suspended production of Lutathera* ® *and Pluvicto* *™* */* 177 *Lu-PSMA-617* *at facilities in Ivrea, Italy and Millburn, New Jersey* *This action has been taken out of an abundance of caution as a result of potential quality issues identified in its manufacturing processes* *The production suspension impacts commercial and clinical trial supply* *Current expectation is resolution of these issues, and resumption of some supply, within 6 weeks; subject to confirmation via an ongoing review*
**Basel, May 5, 2022** — Novartis today announced a temporary, voluntary suspension of production at its radioligand therapy production sites in Ivrea, Italy and Millburn, New Jersey.
The company has taken this action out of an abundance of caution as it addresses potential quality issues identified in its manufacturing processes.
Novartis is conducting a thorough review of the situation and currently expects to resolve the issues and resume some supply in the next six weeks. ... ® (USAN: lutetium Lu 177 dotatate; INN: lutetium (177Lu) oxodotreotide) in the US and Canada, and 177Lu-PSMA-617 (INN: lutetium (177Lu) vipivotide tetraxetan), marketed as Pluvicto™ (lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) in the US.

Next Milestone Estimate & Abandonment Si.full_value: Phase III trial (NCT05939414) completion and efficacy/safety data readout for oligometastatic prostate cancer indication expected 2025-2026; Pluvicto already FDA-approved March 2022 for mCRPC post-ARPI [2]; ongoing expansion to earlier treatment lines with PSMAfore data showing survival benefit in pre-chemotherapy setting [2]; manufacturing capacity expansion to 250,000+ doses annually supports continued development [1]; no abandonment signs - active investment in manufacturing ($200M+ in new facilities) and ongoing clinical trials indicate strong commitment [2]

comment.original_value: Completion of Phase III, efficacy and safety data readout.
comment.validator_explanation: Original value correctly identified Phase III completion and data readout as next milestone but lacked specificity. Updated with context that Pluvicto is already approved for one indication (mCRPC) and the Phase III trial NCT05939414 is specifically for oligometastatic prostate cancer in earlier treatment lines. Added concrete evidence of no abandonment (major manufacturing investments, supply expansion, ongoing trials). High confidence based on multiple authoritative sources.

(0.85): "**Basel, January 5, 2024**— Novartis announced today that it has received approval from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for commercial manufacturing of Pluvicto TM (INN: lutetium ( 177Lu) vipivotide tetraxetan / USAN: lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) at its new large-scale, state-of-the-art radioligand therapy (RLT) manufacturing facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, United States.
The 70,000-square foot site, the company’s second US location, is designed specifically for RLT manufacturing and is now the largest and most advanced Novartis facility of its kind in the world.
The Indianapolis site represents the next phase of RLT manufacturing growth as this new addition brings substantial supply increases for the foreseeable future.
“The intricate process of providing RLTs to patients within hours of production requires precision manufacturing expertise to bring these medicines to individuals who critically need them,” said Steffen Lang, President, Operations, Novartis. “Adding a second US RLT facility, our largest and most advanced yet, into our manufacturing network underscores our commitment to ensure a consistent and reliable experience for patients and their healthcare teams for years to come.
We also recently announced plans to build our manufacturing capabilities in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, Zhejiang, China, as we continue to look for opportunities to further expand our worldwide reach.”
The Indianapolis facility, centrally located within the US, is purpose-built from the ground up to manufacture RLTs now and into the future and includes space for continued line expansion including plans for fully automated lines, a first for the radiopharmaceutical industry. ... Novartis recently announced that supply of Pluvicto is unconstrained.
