We are continually evaluating additional opportunities to increase capacity around the world." (https://www.novartis.com/news/media-releases/novartis-expands-production-pluvictotm-addition-its-largest-and-most-advanced-radioligand-therapy-manufacturing-facility-indianapolis)

sources.title: Novartis expands production of Pluvicto™ with addition ...
sources.snippet:
**Basel, January 5, 2024**— Novartis announced today that it has received approval from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for commercial manufacturing of Pluvicto TM (INN: lutetium ( 177Lu) vipivotide tetraxetan / USAN: lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) at its new large-scale, state-of-the-art radioligand therapy (RLT) manufacturing facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, United States.
The 70,000-square foot site, the company’s second US location, is designed specifically for RLT manufacturing and is now the largest and most advanced Novartis facility of its kind in the world.
The Indianapolis site represents the next phase of RLT manufacturing growth as this new addition brings substantial supply increases for the foreseeable future.
“The intricate process of providing RLTs to patients within hours of production requires precision manufacturing expertise to bring these medicines to individuals who critically need them,” said Steffen Lang, President, Operations, Novartis. “Adding a second US RLT facility, our largest and most advanced yet, into our manufacturing network underscores our commitment to ensure a consistent and reliable experience for patients and their healthcare teams for years to come.
We also recently announced plans to build our manufacturing capabilities in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, Zhejiang, China, as we continue to look for opportunities to further expand our worldwide reach.”
The Indianapolis facility, centrally located within the US, is purpose-built from the ground up to manufacture RLTs now and into the future and includes space for continued line expansion including plans for fully automated lines, a first for the radiopharmaceutical industry. ... Novartis recently announced that supply of Pluvicto is unconstrained.
Having doubled weekly production, Novartis currently has more than sufficient supply to treat patients within two weeks of diagnosis, which is important for these patients with advanced disease who may need treatment quickly. ... With four active manufacturing facilities, and a RLT production capacity of 250,000 doses in 2024 and beyond, Novartis continues to expand its worldwide RLT manufacturing network as ongoing clinical trials may present the potential to bring Pluvicto and Lutathera to more patients in earlier lines of treatment. ... Novartis recently presented data at the 2023 European Society for Medical Oncology (ESMO) Congress studying Pluvicto in the pre-taxane setting for patients with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA)-positive metastatic castration-resistance prostate cancer (mCRPC). With established global expertise, and specialized supply chain and manufacturing capabilities across its network, we are supporting growing demand for our RLT medicines.
Our production capabilities continue to expand and now include sites in Millburn, US, Zaragoza, Spain, Ivrea, Italy and our new state-of-the-art facility in Indianapolis, US.
We recently announced plans to expand our manufacturing capabilities and build additional points of supply in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, Zhejiang, China to produce RLTs for patients in Japan and China.
We are continually evaluating additional opportunities to increase capacity around the world.

sources.title: Novartis Starts Construction on Two US Sites to Bolster Radioligand ...
sources.snippet:
Novartis on Wednesday announced it has begun construction on two radioligand therapy manufacturing facilities in a bid to beef up supply of its products Pluvicto and Lutathera—and solidify its leadership in the targeted radiotherapy space.
The company will invest over $200 million to fund these efforts, according to multiple media reports.
As part of the manufacturing push, the pharma will establish a new site in Carlsbad, California, which will be home to its third radioligand therapy (RLT) production facility in the U.S.
The site will “support expanded use of RLTs, create resiliency in its manufacturing network and optimize the delivery of medicines to patients on the West Coast,” according to Novartis.
The company will also expand its footprint in Indiana, giving it greater in-house production capacity for radioisotopes used in RLTs. ... The drug was approved in March 2022 and is the first targeted RLT for advanced prostate cancer. ... Data from the Phase III PSMAfore study showed Pluvicto appears to improve overall survival in this patient subgroup.

**Basel, May 5, 2022** — Novartis today announced a temporary, voluntary suspension of production at its radioligand therapy production sites in Ivrea, Italy and Millburn, New Jersey.
The company has taken this action out of an abundance of caution as it addresses potential quality issues identified in its manufacturing processes.
Novartis is conducting a thorough review of the situation and currently expects to resolve the issues and resume some supply in the next six weeks. ... ® (USAN: lutetium Lu 177 dotatate; INN: lutetium (177Lu) oxodotreotide) in the US and Canada, and 177Lu-PSMA-617 (INN: lutetium (177Lu) vipivotide tetraxetan), marketed as Pluvicto™ (lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) in the US.

sources.title: FDA expands Pluvicto's prostate cancer indication
sources.snippet: On March 28, 2025, the Food and Drug Administration expanded the indication for lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan (Pluvicto, Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation) to include adults with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA)-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) who have been treated with androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (ARPI) therapy and are considered appropriate to delay taxane-based chemotherapy. ... comment.key_citation: [V1] Novartis expands production of Pluvicto™ with addition ... (0.85): "**Basel, January 5, 2024**— Novartis announced today that it has received approval from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for commercial manufacturing of Pluvicto TM (INN: lutetium ( 177Lu) vipivotide tetraxetan / USAN: lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) at its new large-scale, state-of-the-art radioligand therapy (RLT) manufacturing facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, United States. ... We also recently announced plans to build our manufacturing capabilities in Sasayama, Japan and Haiyan, Zhejiang, China, as we continue to look for opportunities to further expand our worldwide reach.” ... The new site will supply the growing demand for patients in the US and eventually in Canada, upon approval, together with the company’s Millburn, New Jersey location. The site in Ivrea, Italy will continue to supply patients in and outside the US while the facility in Zaragoza, Spain will solely provide RLTs for patients outside the US. Novartis recently announced that supply of Pluvicto is unconstrained. Having doubled weekly production, Novartis currently has more than sufficient supply to treat patients within two weeks of diagnosis, which is important for these patients with advanced disease who may need treatment quickly." (https://www.novartis.com/news/media-releases/novartis-expands-production-pluvictotm-addition-its-largest-and-most-advanced-radioligand-therapy-manufacturing-facility-indianapolis)

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Jan 19, 2024: Novartis presented Phase III NETTE...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Jan 19, 2024: Novartis presented Phase III NETTER-2 trial results at ASCO GI showing Lutathera plus octreotide LAR reduced risk of disease progression or death by 72% vs high-dose octreotide LAR alone as first-line therapy in SSTR+ grade 2/3 advanced GEP-NETs [1].
• Jan 22, 2024: NETTER-2 trial results published showing Lutathera plus octreotide extended median progression-free survival to 22.8 months vs 8.5 months with octreotide alone, with no new safety findings [2].
• Mar 1, 2024: NCI Cancer Currents highlighted NETTER-2 as practice-changing, with patients receiving Lutathera plus octreotide living nearly 3x longer without cancer progression (median 23 months vs 8.5 months) [3].
• 2025: Lutathera HCP website indicates NETTER-2 data led to NCCN Guidelines recommendations for first-line use in GEP-NETs, with ongoing OS follow-up [4].

sources.snippet: COMPOSE is ITM’s second phase III trial with its lead candidate n.c.a. 177Lu-edotreotide, a Targeted Radionuclide Therapy consisting of the high-quality radioisotope no-carrier-added lutetium-177 fused with an innovative somatostatin analogue to specifically target GEP-NETs.

sources.title: COMPOSE - GTE - Groupes d'étude des tumeurs endocrines
sources.snippet: 177Lu-Edotreotide (synonyms: Lutetium (177Lu) Edotreotide; 177Lu-DOTATOC) was first reported in 2005 (Forrer et al., 2005) as an innovative PRRT agent with a favourable safety profile and promising efficacy. 177Lu-Edotreotide is currently under development for the treatment of neuroendocrine tumours (NETs) with a focus on well-differentiated SSTR+, gastro-entero-pancreatic neuroendocrine tumours (GEP-NETs).

comment.validator_explanation: Official company designation is n.c.a. 177Lu-edotreotide; 177Lu-DOTATOC is a synonym used in literature [1][2]

Target / Indication.full_value: Somatostatin Receptor 2 (SSTR2) in Grade 2 and 3 GEP-NETs

comment.validator_explanation: The current value correctly identifies the indication (Grade 2 and 3 GEP-NETs) as confirmed by multiple high-authority sources [1] and [2]. However, the biological target (SSTR2 - somatostatin receptor) should be explicitly stated per field requirements. The FDA approval specifies 'somatostatin receptor-positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors' [2], and the mechanism involves targeting tumor cells expressing somatostatin receptors [3]. Updated to include both biological target and indication as per examples.

sources.title: First-Line 177Lu-Dotatate Plus Octreotide LAR Significantly Extends PFS in Patients with Grade 2 or 3 Advanced GEP NETs
sources.snippet: The study met its primary objective, with ¹⁷⁷Lu-Dotatate plus octreotide 30 mg long-acting repeatable (LAR) reducing the risk of disease progression or death by around 72% compared with high-dose octreotide 60 mg LAR. ... 177Lu-Dotatate plus octreotide LAR significantly extended median PFS by 14 months in patients with grade 2 or 3 advanced GEP NETs. 177Lu-Dotatate should be considered a new standard-of-care in first-line treatment in this population.

sources.title: FDA approves lutetium Lu 177 dotatate for treatment of ...
sources.snippet: On January 26, 2018, the Food and Drug Administration approved lutetium Lu 177 dotatate (LUTATHERA, Advanced Accelerator Applications USA, Inc.) a radiolabeled somatostatin analog, for the treatment of somatostatin receptor-positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs), including foregut, midgut, and hindgut neuroendocrine tumors in adults.

sources.title: Neuroendocrine Tumor Lu-177-Dotatate Therapy
sources.snippet: Lutetium-177 ( 177Lu)-dotatate therapy or peptide receptor radioligand therapy (PRRT) is indicated for treating gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors and NET of the prostate gland. ... The combination of radionucleotide 177Lu with somatostatin analog dotatate delivers ionizing radiation targeting tumor cells that express somatostatin receptors, causing radiation-induced single and double-stranded DNA breaks leading to apoptosis.

sources.title: Lutetium oxodotreotide (177Lu-Dotatate) for the treatment of ...
sources.snippet: Findings from the phase III NETTER-1 trial showed that treatment of unresectable/metastatic progressive gastrointestinal (GI) NETs with ^177^Lu-Dotatate resulted in a significant improvement in progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) compared with best supportive care (BSC) with high dose octreotide long-acting repeatable (LAR) 60 mg. ... ^177^Lu-Dotatate is the first PRRT medicine with marketing authorization for the treatment of GEP-NETs in adults and addresses the unmet clinical need for effective and well-tolerated treatments for NETs [11].

Therapeutic Agent (Radionuclide & Payloa.full_value: Lutetium-177 (^177Lu) beta-emitter conjugated to DOTATATE somatostatin analog peptide

comment.validator_explanation: The current value correctly identifies Lutetium-177 as the beta-emitter and somatostatin analog as the payload type. However, sources consistently refer to the compound as ^177Lu-DOTATATE (not DOTATOC) [1][2][4]. The FDA approval and clinical trials reference DOTATATE specifically. The previous validation noted '177Lu-edotreotide (177Lu-DOTATOC)' suggesting these may be related compounds, but authoritative sources for this specific product consistently use DOTATATE. Updated to reflect accurate nomenclature and specify peptide payload type.

Development Stage.full_value: Approved (FDA 2018); Phase III ongoing for first-line treatment (NCT04919226)

comment.validator_explanation: The current value identifies Phase III trial NCT04919226, which is accurate for ongoing first-line evaluation [1]. However, ^177Lu-DOTATATE (LUTATHERA) received FDA approval on January 26, 2018 for treatment of somatostatin receptor-positive GEP-NETs [2]. The product is already approved, with additional Phase III trials evaluating expanded indications (first-line vs. later-line treatment). Updated to reflect both approved status and ongoing Phase III development for new indications.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Mar 28, 2025: Retrospective analysis of 141 NET ...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Mar 28, 2025: Retrospective analysis of 141 NET patients receiving PRRT with [177Lu]Lu-DOTATOC showed median OS of 55.2 months, DCR 72%, and ORR 12% [1].
• Jan 26, 2018: FDA approved lutetium Lu 177 dotatate (LUTATHERA) for treatment of somatostatin receptor-positive GEP-NETs [2].
• 2018: FDA granted priority review and Orphan Drug designation for lutetium Lu 177 dotatate for GEP-NETs treatment [2].
• 2016-2019: Phase III NETTER-1 trial demonstrated efficacy with median follow-up of 24 months, showing myelodysplastic syndrome in 2.7% of patients [2].

comment.original_value: -  Ambition to evaluate efficacy and safety (ongoing)
comment.validator_explanation: Original value was vague. Updated with specific, dated news. The 2025 retrospective analysis is directly relevant to 177Lu-DOTATOC [1]. The 2018 FDA approval and NETTER-1 data are for the related compound 177Lu-DOTATATE (LUTATHERA), which is the approved standard for the same indication [2]. No recent press releases or trial updates specific to the COMPOSE trial (NCT04919226) for 177Lu-edotreotide were found.

sources.title: overall survival and prognostic insights from [¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-DOTATOC ...
sources.snippet: Monocentric, retrospective analysis of 141 patients with NET (grading: 21% G1, 71% G2, 4% G3, 4% grading unknown; primary: 48% small intestine (SI-NET); 27% pancreas (P-NET); 9% colon/rectum; 1% stomach, 7% lung; 9% CUP-NET) receiving PRRT with [^177^Lu]Lu-DOTATOC. ... The ongoing COMPETE (NCT03049189) and COMPOSE (NCT04919226) trials are expected to provide much-needed information on outcomes with [^177^Lu]Lu-DOTATOC and could redefine the role of PRRT in the treatment sequence for NET. ... Patients had a median OS of 55.2 months from the start of PRRT, while DCR and ORR were 72% and 12%, respectively.

sources.title: FDA approves lutetium Lu 177 dotatate for treatment of ...
sources.snippet: On January 26, 2018, the Food and Drug Administration approved lutetium Lu 177 dotatate (LUTATHERA, Advanced Accelerator Applications USA, Inc.) a radiolabeled somatostatin analog, for the treatment of somatostatin receptor-positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs), including foregut, midgut, and hindgut neuroendocrine tumors in adults. ... In NETTER-1, with a median follow-up of 24 months, myelodysplastic syndrome was reported in 2.7% of patients receiving lutetium Lu 177 dotatate with long-acting octreotide; no patients receiving high-dose octreotide LAR developed myelodysplastic syndrome. ... FDA granted priority review for this application and previously granted Orphan Drug designation to lutetium Lu 177 dotatate for treatment of GEP-NETs.

Target / Indication.full_value: Somatostatin receptor-positive (SSTR+) gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs) in adults and pediatric patients 12 years and older, including foregut, midgut, and hindgut NETs [1]. Current value 'Neuroendocrine neoplasms other than G1/G2 GEP-NETs, lung and thymic carcinoids' is incorrect - Lutathera is approved for G1/G2 GEP-NETs, not other NENs [2]. Compendial uses may include lung/thymus NETs but not FDA-approved [3].

comment.original_value: Neuroendocrine neoplasms other than G1/G2 GEP-NETs, lung and thymic carcinoids
comment.validator_explanation: FDA label and Novartis press releases confirm approved indication is SSTR+ GEP-NETs in adults and pediatric patients ≥12 years. Current value misstates the approved indication.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Jan 19, 2024: Positive Phase III NETTER-2 trial ...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Jan 19, 2024: Positive Phase III NETTER-2 trial results presented at ASCO GI showing Lutathera plus octreotide LAR reduced risk of disease progression or death by 72% as first-line therapy in newly diagnosed grade 2/3 advanced GEP-NETs [1]
• Jan 22, 2024: NETTER-2 trial results published showing Lutathera plus octreotide extended median progression-free survival to 22.8 months vs 8.5 months with octreotide alone in first-line setting [2]
• Apr 23, 2024: FDA approved Lutathera for pediatric patients aged 12 years and older with SSTR-positive GEP-NETs [3]
• Jan 26, 2018: Initial FDA approval for adults with SSTR-positive GEP-NETs [4]

comment.key_citation: [V1] FDA expands Pluvicto's prostate cancer indication (0.85): "On March 28, 2025, the Food and Drug Administration expanded the indication for lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan (Pluvicto, Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation) to include adults with prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA)-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) who have been treated with androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (ARPI) therapy and are considered appropriate to delay taxane-based chemotherapy." (https://www.fda.gov/drugs/resources-information-approved-drugs/fda-expands-pluvictos-metastatic-castration-resistant-prostate-cancer-indication)

sources.snippet: **Basel, March 23, 2022** — Novartis announced today that the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved Pluvicto TM (lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) (formerly referred to as 177Lu-PSMA-617) for the treatment of adult patients with a certain type of advanced cancer called prostate-specific membrane antigen–positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (PSMA-positive mCRPC) that has spread to other parts of the body (metastatic) 1.

Target / Indication.full_value: Prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC)

comment.original_value: High PSMA-expressing high-risk localized or locoregional advanced prostate cancer
comment.validator_explanation: The current value specifies 'high-risk localized or locoregional advanced prostate cancer' with pre-surgical treatment context. However, all authoritative sources consistently describe Lu-177 PSMA therapy (Pluvicto) as approved and indicated for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) that has progressed after other therapies [1], [2], [3]. The biological target is PSMA (prostate-specific membrane antigen) [1]. The trial NCT04430192 mentioned in the current value appears to be a specific investigational study for pre-surgical use, which is NOT the standard approved indication. The original value conflates a specific trial context with the broader product indication. Updated to reflect the standard approved indication for Lu-177 PSMA therapy.

sources.title: Lutetium-177 PSMA Therapy for Prostate Cancer (Pluvicto)
sources.snippet: If you have prostate cancer that has spread beyond the prostate and progressed after other therapies (including hormonal therapies and at least one chemotherapy), you may be eligible. ... It’s a type of radiation that specifically targets a molecule on the surface of prostate cancer cells called PSMA. ... Lutetium-177 vipivotide tetraxetan PSMA therapy (Pluvicto) is a new theranostic medicine for advanced metastatic prostate cancer. ... This therapy is used with the companion diagnostic gallium-68 gozetotide (Locametz), which is more commonly known as a PSMA PET scan. ... Both lutetium-177 PSMA and gallium-68 PSMA were approved by the FDA in March 2022 and are proven to significantly improve prostate cancer survival rates and quality of life, as well as extend the time it takes for the disease to progress. ... Like using a GPS, the radio tracers travel through the body to target and bind to specific cancer cells, which are then visible on a PET scan. ... During treatment, the PSMA grabs on to the lutetium-177 PSMA; the radiation it carries is absorbed into the cancer cell, damaging its DNA and ultimately causing cell death.

sources.title: Lutetium-177 shows significant rPFS benefit for metastatic prostate ...
sources.snippet: Mayo Clinic researchers found that lutetium-177 (^177^Lu)-PSMA-617 prolonged radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) in patients with PSMA-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) who were taxane-naive and had progressive disease after receiving androgen receptor pathway inhibition (ARPI) therapy compared with those who received a second ARPI therapy.

Therapeutic Agent (Radionuclide & Payloa.full_value: Lutetium-177 beta-emitter conjugated to PSMA-targeting small molecule (PSMA-617 or similar PSMA ligands)

comment.validator_explanation: The current value correctly identifies Lutetium-177 as a beta-emitter conjugated to a PSMA inhibitor. Sources confirm Lu-177 is a beta-emitting isotope [4] that targets PSMA on prostate cancer cells [1]. The specific ligands used are PSMA-617 (Pluvicto) and PSMA-I&T (Lu-PNT2002) [3]. The payload is a small molecule PSMA ligand, not just an 'inhibitor' - it's more accurately described as a PSMA-targeting small molecule. Minor refinement for precision while maintaining the core accuracy of the original value.

Diagnostic/Theranostic Agent.full_value: Theranostic agent: Therapeutic Lu-177 PSMA paired with diagnostic Ga-68 PSMA PET imaging

comment.validator_explanation: The current value states 'Therapeutic (pre-surgery)' which is incomplete. Lu-177 PSMA therapy is explicitly described as a theranostic medicine [1] used with the companion diagnostic gallium-68 gozetotide (Locametz) PSMA PET scan [1]. Both Lu-177 PSMA and Ga-68 PSMA were FDA approved in March 2022 [1]. The theranostic approach uses Ga-68 for diagnostic imaging to identify PSMA-positive tumors, then Lu-177 for therapeutic delivery [1]. The 'pre-surgery' context in the original value appears specific to one trial and does not represent the standard theranostic classification.

Specific Biochemistry & Approach.full_value: PSMA-targeting small molecule radioligand (PSMA-617 or PSMA-I&T) that binds to prostate-specific membrane antigen (a transmembrane glutamate carboxypeptidase) on cancer cells, delivering beta radiation from Lu-177 to induce DNA damage and cell death

comment.original_value: PSMA targeting small molecule for therapeutic delivery of Lutetium-177, pre-surgical treatment
comment.validator_explanation: The current value correctly identifies PSMA-targeting small molecule for Lu-177 delivery but lacks biochemical detail. PSMA is specifically a transmembrane glutamate carboxypeptidase highly expressed on prostate cancer cells [4]. The mechanism involves the PSMA ligand binding to PSMA on the cell surface, the radiation is absorbed into the cancer cell, damaging DNA and causing cell death [1]. The primary PSMA ligands are PSMA-617 (Pluvicto) and PSMA-I&T (Lu-PNT2002) [3]. Beta particles from Lu-177 deliver the therapeutic effect [4]. Enhanced to include specific biochemical mechanism and molecular class details.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • March 2022: FDA approved Pluvicto (lutetium Lu 1...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• March 2022: FDA approved Pluvicto (lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan, ^177Lu-PSMA-617) for PSMA-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) after androgen receptor pathway inhibitor and taxane-based chemotherapy [1]
• December 2022: EMA approved PSMA-617 for mCRPC patients progressing after enzalutamide or abiraterone acetate and taxane chemotherapy [2]
• October 2023: Phase 3 PSMAfore trial results presented at ESMO Congress showing ^177Lu-PSMA-617 prolonged radiographic progression-free survival (12 months vs 5.59 months, HR 0.43) in taxane-naive mCRPC patients [3]
• Ongoing: Phase II trial NCT04430192 assessing safety, effectiveness, and appropriate dosage of ^177Lu-PSMA before radical prostatectomy in high-risk localized or locoregional advanced prostate cancer [4]

Target / Indication.full_value: Somatostatin Receptor 2 (SSTR2) in gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (GEP-NETs)

comment.validator_explanation: The original value 'GEP-NETs (comparison with everolimus)' is incomplete as it describes the trial context rather than the biological target and indication. The product [68Ga]-DOTATOC is FDA-approved for PET imaging of somatostatin receptor (SSTR) positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors [1]. Most GEP-NETs express somatostatin receptors, predominantly SSTR2 and SSTR5 [2], with SSTR2 being highly expressed on various tumors including NETs and serving as an attractive therapeutic target [3]. The biological target is SSTR2, and the indication is GEP-NETs. The comparison with everolimus mentioned in the original value refers to the COMPETE trial context but is not part of the product's target/indication specification.

sources.title: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-TOC: The First FDA-Approved ... - PubMed Central

comment.validator_explanation: The original field was blank. [68Ga]-DOTATOC is a diagnostic imaging agent, not a therapeutic agent [1]. It is indicated for use with PET for localization of somatostatin receptor positive neuroendocrine tumors [1]. While it can be part of a theranostic approach where diagnostic imaging precedes therapeutic radionuclide treatment [4], the product itself does not contain a therapeutic radionuclide. The therapeutic counterpart would be agents like [177Lu]Lu-DOTATOC (mentioned in the trial context), but [68Ga]-DOTATOC itself is purely diagnostic. Therefore, this field is appropriately marked as N/A for this specific product.

Diagnostic/Theranostic Agent.full_value: Diagnostic PET imaging agent (Gallium-68 labeled somatostatin analog DOTA-TOC)

comment.original_value: PET imaging tracer (Gallium-68 labeled somatostatin analog)
comment.validator_explanation: The original value 'PET imaging tracer (Gallium-68 labeled somatostatin analog)' is accurate and high confidence. Enhanced for completeness: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-TOC was approved by the FDA in 2019 as the first 68Ga-radiopharmaceutical for imaging of SSTR-positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors using PET [4]. The radionuclide gallium-68 is complexed by the bifunctional chelator DOTA (1,4,7,10-tetraazacyclododecane-1,4,7,10-tetraacetic acid), which coordinates gallium-68 to four nitrogen atoms and two carboxylic groups [4]. While it can be used in a theranostic approach as the diagnostic component paired with therapeutic radionuclides [4], the product itself is classified as a diagnostic agent.

Specific Biochemistry & Approach.full_value: Somatostatin analog (DOTA-TOC) targeting SSTR2/SSTR5 receptors; DOTA chelator (1,4,7,10-tetraazacyclododecane-1,4,7,10-tetraacetic acid) complexes Ga-68 via hexadentate coordination with octahedral geometry

comment.original_value: Somatostatin receptor targeting peptide for diagnostic imaging
comment.validator_explanation: The original value 'Somatostatin receptor targeting peptide for diagnostic imaging' is accurate but lacks technical detail. Enhanced with specific biochemistry: The radionuclide gallium-68 is complexed by the bifunctional chelator DOTA, which acts as a hexadentate chelator with octahedral geometry, coordinating gallium-68 to four nitrogen atoms and two carboxylic groups [4]. This radiopharmaceutical combines the radionuclide 68Ga with the somatostatin analogue DOTA-TOC for specific imaging of tumor cells expressing SSTRs [4]. Most GEP-NETs express somatostatin receptors, predominantly SSTR2 and SSTR5 [2]. The product can be prepared on-site using an approved kit preparation (SomaKit TOC) together with an authorized 68Ge/68Ga-generator [4].

comment.validator_explanation: The original value 'Phase III (NCT03049189)' refers to the COMPETE trial comparing [177Lu]Lu-DOTATOC with everolimus, not the development stage of [68Ga]-DOTATOC itself. The diagnostic agent [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-TOC was approved by the FDA on August 21, 2019 [4] as the first 68Ga-radiopharmaceutical for imaging of SSTR-positive gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors [4]. In Europe, it was already approved in 2016 in Austria, Germany, and France [4]. The product is commercially available and has been used clinically in Europe for over a decade [5]. The NCT03049189 trial mentioned in the original value is a separate Phase III study of the therapeutic agent [177Lu]Lu-DOTATOC, not the diagnostic [68Ga]-DOTATOC product.

Target / Indication.display_value: Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) in locally adv...
Target / Indication.full_value: Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) in locally advanced or metastatic cancer (multiple solid tumors including pancreatic, gastric, colorectal, lung, breast, head and neck, esophageal, and ovarian cancer)

comment.validator_explanation: The biological target is FAP (Fibroblast Activation Protein), which is selectively produced by cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) and highly expressed in various epithelial cancers [1]. The clinical trial NCT04849247 specifically evaluates [^68Ga]Ga-DOTA-FAPI in patients with various types of locally advanced or metastatic cancer [2]. Published studies confirm FAP-targeted imaging has been evaluated across multiple solid tumor types including pancreatic, head and neck, colon, lung, breast cancer [3], as well as gastric, colorectal, esophageal, and ovarian cancer [1]. The original value was too generic; the updated value specifies the biological target (FAP) and provides comprehensive cancer type coverage based on clinical evidence.
comment.key_citation:
[V1] Comparison of [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-FAPI-04 and [18F]FDG PET/MRI in the Preoperative Diagnosis of Gastric Cancer (0.85): "Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) are the main components of the matrix around epithelial cancer cells and can selectively produce fibroblast activation protein (FAP).
FAP is highly expressed in a variety of epithelial cancers, such as gastric cancer, colorectal cancer, esophageal cancer, and ovarian cancer, but it is almost not expressed in the matrix of normal tissues [1, 2]." (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10118892/)

sources.title: Comparison of [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-FAPI-04 and [18F]FDG PET/MRI in the Preoperative Diagnosis of Gastric Cancer
sources.snippet:
Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) are the main components of the matrix around epithelial cancer cells and can selectively produce fibroblast activation protein (FAP).

comment.validator_explanation: [^68Ga]Ga-DOTA-FAPI-04 is a diagnostic PET imaging tracer, not a therapeutic agent. The original field was blank, which was appropriate since this specific product is diagnostic; however, clarification about the theranostic pair is important for context.

Specific Biochemistry & Approach.full_value: Small-molecule FAP inhibitor (FAPI-04) with DOTA chelator for radionuclide conjugation; targets FAP expressed on cancer-associated fibroblasts in tumor stroma; rapid tracer kinetics with low background uptake in liver, oral mucosa, and brain; imaging possible <1 hour post-injection

comment.validator_explanation: The biochemical approach uses a small-molecule FAP inhibitor (specifically FAPI-04 based on the product name [^68Ga]Ga-DOTA-FAPI-04) conjugated to a DOTA chelator that enables radionuclide labeling [3]. FAP is selectively produced by cancer-associated fibroblasts and provides a relatively tumor-specific target [3]. The tracer exhibits favorable pharmacokinetics including fast tracer kinetics appropriate for imaging <1 hour post-injection, low background uptake in liver, oral mucosa, and brain, and independence from blood sugar levels [3]. Physiological uptake is observed in liver, kidneys, pancreas, heart contents, and to a lesser extent in lacrimals, oral mucosa, salivary glands, and thyroid glands [4]. The effective radiation dose is approximately 3-4 mSv for a routine 200 MBq scan [3]. The original value was accurate but limited; the updated value provides comprehensive biochemical and pharmacokinetic details.

comment.validator_explanation: The clinical trial NCT04849247 is a Phase I, first-in-human study evaluating the safety and efficacy of the ^68Ga-DOTA-FAPI and ^177Lu-DOTA-FAPI theranostic pair [2]. The study was initiated in April 2021 [S1.1.4]. The primary objectives are to identify dose-limiting toxicity (DLT) and recommended phase 2 dose (RP2D) for ^177Lu-DOTA-FAPI therapy [2]. The study hypotheses include that ^68Ga-DOTA-FAPI will detect lesions in patients with various types of locally advanced or metastatic cancer, that the theranostic pair will be safe and well-tolerated, and that a RP2D can be identified for subsequent Phase II trials [2]. The original value correctly identified the trial number but the updated value provides more comprehensive context about the study design and objectives. Note: The original reference to NCT04939610 appears to be an error; the correct trial number is NCT04849247.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Mar 12, 2024: SOFIE and GE Healthcare announce l...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Mar 12, 2024: SOFIE and GE Healthcare announce licensing agreement for global rights to [⁶⁸Ga]FAPI-46 and outside-US rights for [¹⁸F]FAPI-74, with SOFIE retaining US rights for [¹⁸F]FAPI-74 and continuing its clinical development program [1].
• 2024: Multiple clinical studies published demonstrating superiority of FAPI PET/CT over ¹⁸F-FDG PET/CT in various cancers, including breast cancer staging (19.8% of patients restaged) [2] and predicting progressive pulmonary fibrosis in interstitial lung disease [3].
• 2023-2025: Extensive research confirms FAPI as a pan-cancer target with high target-to-background ratio across multiple solid tumors, enabling improved staging and treatment planning [4][5].
• 2023: First proof-of-concept patient studies with [¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-DOTAGA.Glu.(FAPi)₂ demonstrate improved tumor retention and faster excretion kinetics for therapeutic applications [6].

We are looking forward to a fruitful 2024. SOFIE and GE Healthcare (GEHC) continue to work closely on the clinical development of Gallium-68 and Fluorine-18 Fibroblast Activation Protein Inhibitor (FAPI) radiopharmaceutical tracers.
Last October, we entered a licensing agreement for GEHC to take on global rights for [
68Ga]FAPI-46 and outside-US rights for [ 18F]FAPI-74, while SOFIE will continue its clinical development and commercialization program with [ 18F]FAPI-74 in the U.S.
We completed our first Joint Steering Meeting in person to kick start our strategic efforts on the FAPI program.

sources.title: FAPI PET/CT Improves Staging of Newly Diagnosed Breast Cancer
sources.snippet:
Toronto, Ontario—A comparative analysis between FAPI and 18F-FDG PET/CT for the systemic staging of newly diagnosed breast cancer shows that FAPI PET/CT is more accurate in staging patients.
Nearly 20 percent of patients were restaged by FAPI PET/CT, indicating the radiotracer’s efficacies and benefits for clinical practice.
This research was presented at the 2024 Society of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging Annual Meeting.

sources.title: FAPI PET/CT Bests FDG in Predicting Progressive Lung Disease
sources.snippet:
Toronto, Ontario—In a head-to-head study, FAPI PET/CT was found to be more predictive of progressive pulmonary fibrosis in interstitial lung disease (ILD) patients than 18F-FDG PET/CT.
With heightened and extensive uptake compared to 18F-FDG, FAPI PET/CT can play an important role in identifying patients who should be monitored closely or begin preventive treatment.

sources.title: FAPI PET/CT Imaging—An Updated Review - PMC - NIH
sources.snippet:
FAP holds the promise to be a pan-cancer target, owing to its selective over-expression in a vast majority of neoplasms, particularly epithelial cancers.
Several radiolabeled FAP inhibitors (FAPI) have been developed for molecular imaging and potential theranostic applications.
Preliminary data on FAPI PET/CT remains encouraging, with extensive multi-disciplinary clinical research currently underway.
This review summarizes the existing literature on FAPI PET/CT imaging with an emphasis on diagnostic applications, comparison with FDG, pitfalls, and future directions.

sources.title: Fibroblast Activation Protein Inhibitor (FAPI)
sources.snippet:
Despite these challenges, FAPI PET is a promising imaging modality with significant clinical potential.
Further research is needed to develop alternative FAPI tracers that offer an improved specificity, cost-effectiveness, and broader clinical utility [31,32,33].
To maximize its clinical impact, large-scale trials with diverse patient cohorts are required to validate its role across different malignancies.

Beyond that, first proof-of-concept patient investigations were carried out with [^177^Lu]Lu-DOTAGA.Glu.(FAPi)~2~, which demonstrated faster excretion kinetics and therefore reduced the radiation dose for healthy organs such as the colon and liver.

sources.title: Clinical and research applications of fibroblast activation ...
sources.snippet:
FAPI-RLT is still investigational and has not been commercialized yet.
As such, accessibility is through clinical trials.
It is also used sporadically for compassion in some centres.
It is not yet included in oncological algorithms from major societies, mostly because larger phase II/III clinical trials are needed to support their effectiveness.
Moreover, the lack of commercial availability and regulatory approval hampers its clinical use.

sources.title: SOFIE and GE HealthCare Enter Licensing Agreement ...
sources.snippet:
CHALFONT ST GILES, E--(뉴스와이어)--GE HealthCare today announced an exclusive global licensing agreement with SOFIE Biosciences (SOFIE), a Virginia, U.S.-based company, to develop, manufacture and commercialize SOFIE’s Gallium-68 and Fluorine-18 labelled diagnostics targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP).
Financial terms were not disclosed.
Based on this agreement, GE HealthCare will take on global rights for [⁶⁸Ga]FAPI-46 and outside-US rights for [¹⁸F]FAPI-74, originally developed at Heidelberg University in Germany, and both currently in Phase II clinical trials in the U.S.
([⁶⁸Ga]FAPI-46 (NCT05262855)[2] and [¹⁸F]FAPI-74 (NCT05641896)[3]). ... SOFIE and GE HealthCare will collaborate on both the development and commercialization processes through a joint steering committee.

sources.title: FAPI PET/CT Improves Staging of Newly Diagnosed Breast Cancer
sources.snippet:
Toronto, Ontario—A comparative analysis between FAPI and 18F-FDG PET/CT for the systemic staging of newly diagnosed breast cancer shows that FAPI PET/CT is more accurate in staging patients.
Nearly 20 percent of patients were restaged by FAPI PET/CT, indicating the radiotracer’s efficacies and benefits for clinical practice.
This research was presented at the 2024 Society of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging Annual Meeting.

sources.title: FAPI PET/CT Bests FDG in Predicting Progressive Lung Disease
sources.snippet:
Toronto, Ontario—In a head-to-head study, FAPI PET/CT was found to be more predictive of progressive pulmonary fibrosis in interstitial lung disease (ILD) patients than 18F-FDG PET/CT.
With heightened and extensive uptake compared to 18F-FDG, FAPI PET/CT can play an important role in identifying patients who should be monitored closely or begin preventive treatment.

sources.title: FAPI PET/CT Imaging—An Updated Review - PMC - NIH
sources.snippet:
FAP holds the promise to be a pan-cancer target, owing to its selective over-expression in a vast majority of neoplasms, particularly epithelial cancers.
Several radiolabeled FAP inhibitors (FAPI) have been developed for molecular imaging and potential theranostic applications.
Preliminary data on FAPI PET/CT remains encouraging, with extensive multi-disciplinary clinical research currently underway.
This review summarizes the existing literature on FAPI PET/CT imaging with an emphasis on diagnostic applications, comparison with FDG, pitfalls, and future directions.

sources.title: Fibroblast Activation Protein Inhibitor (FAPI)
sources.snippet:
Despite these challenges, FAPI PET is a promising imaging modality with significant clinical potential.
Further research is needed to develop alternative FAPI tracers that offer an improved specificity, cost-effectiveness, and broader clinical utility [31,32,33].
To maximize its clinical impact, large-scale trials with diverse patient cohorts are required to validate its role across different malignancies.

sources.title: Novel Generation of FAP Inhibitor-Based Homodimers for ...
sources.snippet: The new FAPi dimers were tested for their in vitro affinity for FAP as well as PREP, DPP4, DPP8, and DPP9. Beyond that, first proof-of-concept patient investigations were carried out with [^177^Lu]Lu-DOTAGA.Glu.(FAPi)~2~, which demonstrated faster excretion kinetics and therefore reduced the radiation dose for healthy organs such as the colon and liver.

sources.snippet: This is a Phase I, first-in-human study to evaluate the safety and efficacy of the 68Ga-DOTA-FAPI and 177Lu-DOTA-FAPI theranostic pair in patients with various types of cancer.

sources.title: Clinical and research applications of fibroblast activation ...
sources.snippet:
FAPI-RLT is still investigational and has not been commercialized yet.
As such, accessibility is through clinical trials.
It is also used sporadically for compassion in some centres.
It is not yet included in oncological algorithms from major societies, mostly because larger phase II/III clinical trials are needed to support their effectiveness.
Moreover, the lack of commercial availability and regulatory approval hampers its clinical use.

sources.title: SOFIE and GE HealthCare Enter Licensing Agreement ...
sources.snippet:
CHALFONT ST GILES, E--(뉴스와이어)--GE HealthCare today announced an exclusive global licensing agreement with SOFIE Biosciences (SOFIE), a Virginia, U.S.-based company, to develop, manufacture and commercialize SOFIE’s Gallium-68 and Fluorine-18 labelled diagnostics targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP).
Financial terms were not disclosed.
Based on this agreement, GE HealthCare will take on global rights for [⁶⁸Ga]FAPI-46 and outside-US rights for [¹⁸F]FAPI-74, originally developed at Heidelberg University in Germany, and both currently in Phase II clinical trials in the U.S.
([⁶⁸Ga]FAPI-46 (NCT05262855)[2] and [¹⁸F]FAPI-74 (NCT05641896)[3]). ... SOFIE and GE HealthCare will collaborate on both the development and commercialization processes through a joint steering committee.

Key Partnerships/Collaborators.display_value: GE Healthcare - Exclusive global licensing agreeme...
Key Partnerships/Collaborators.full_value:
GE Healthcare - Exclusive global licensing agreement for development, manufacture, and commercialization of [⁶⁸Ga]FAPI-46 and outside-US rights for [¹⁸F]FAPI-74 [9].
SOFIE Biosciences - Clinical development and commercialization partner for [¹⁸F]FAPI-74 in the U.S., collaborates with GE Healthcare on FAPI program through a joint steering committee [9].
Heidelberg University - Original developer of the FAPI radiopharmaceutical tracers [9].

comment.validator_explanation: Partnership information confirmed from multiple high-confidence press releases and company communications. The licensing agreement between SOFIE and GE Healthcare is well-documented with specific terms regarding rights distribution. Heidelberg University is identified as the original developer.
comment.key_citation:
[V1] Focus on FAPI Newsletter - Winter 2024 (0.65): "We are looking forward to a fruitful 2024. SOFIE and GE Healthcare (GEHC) continue to work closely on the clinical development of Gallium-68 and Fluorine-18 Fibroblast Activation Protein Inhibitor (FAPI) radiopharmaceutical tracers.
Last October, we entered a licensing agreement for GEHC to take on global rights for [
68Ga]FAPI-46 and outside-US rights for [ 18F]FAPI-74, while SOFIE will continue its clinical development and commercialization program with [ 18F]FAPI-74 in the U.S.
We completed our first Joint Steering Meeting in person to kick start our strategic efforts on the FAPI program." (https://sofie.com/2024/03/12/focus-on-fapi-newsletter-winter-2024/)

We completed our first Joint Steering Meeting in person to kick start our strategic efforts on the FAPI program.

sources.title: Investigational Radiopharmaceutical Cleared for the Treatment of Certain Cancers
sources.snippet:
Two investigational drugs from Fusion Pharmaceuticals have been cleared for the treatment of some head and neck and bladder cancers.
The company announced that the FDA has cleared its Investigational New Drug (IND) applications for [225Ac]-FPI-1966 (FPI-1966) and imaging agent [111In]-FPI-1967 (FPI-1967). ... FPI-1966 is a targeted alpha therapy (TAT) designed to use vofatamab, a human monoclonal antibody, to target and deliver actinium-225 to tumor sites expressing fibroblast growth factor 3 (FGFR3), a protein that is overexpressed in multiple tumor types, particularly head and neck and bladder cancers. ... 225Ac]-FPI-1966 is a targeted alpha therapy designed to target and deliver an alpha emitting medical isotope, actinium-225, to cancer cells expressing FGFR3; a receptor that is overexpressed on several tumor types, including head and neck and bladder cancers. FPI-1966 utilizes Fusion's Fast-Clear linker to connect vofatamab, the human monoclonal antibody that targets FGFR3, with actinium-225. Vofatamab was previously evaluated as a therapeutic agent in a Phase 1b/2 trial and was reportedly well-tolerated.

Therapeutic Agent (Radionuclide & Payloa.full_value: Actinium-225 alpha-emitter conjugated to anti-FGFR3 antibody (vofatamab)

comment.validator_explanation: Multiple sources describe [225Ac]-FPI-1966 as a targeted alpha therapy using actinium-225 conjugated to the vofatamab antibody.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator correctly identified the therapeutic agent. Confidence raised to HIGH as [V1] (p95) from ClinicalTrials.gov is authoritative. The original was blank.

sources.title: FDA Grants Fast Track Designation to Vofatamab for ...

This week the FDA granted a fast track designation to the novel targeted therapy vofatamab for the treatment of patients with advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma who harbor an
*FGFR3*alteration, according to Rainier Therapeutics, the company developing vofatamab. *FGFR3*alteration, according to Rainier Therapeutics, the company developing vofatamab. ... The agent is currently being tested in 2 phase I/II clinical trials with a third trial expected to begin recruiting later in the year.

sources.title: Rainier Therapeutics Announces Fast Track Designation Granted by ...
sources.snippet: “This Fast Track designation underscores the great unmet medical need that exists for the treatment of bladder cancer,” said Scott Myers, Chairman and CEO of Rainier Therapeutics. “As the only antibody specifically targeted to FGFR3 we know to be in clinical development, we believe vofatamab offers a promising therapeutic option.

sources.title: Definition of actinium Ac 225 vofatamab
sources.snippet: A radioimmunoconjugate containing vofatamab, a human immunoglobulin G1 (IgG1) monoclonal antibody directed against the fibroblast growth factor receptor type 3 (FGFR3) labeled, via a chelating agent, with the alpha-emitting radioisotope actinium Ac 225, with potential antineoplastic activity.

sources.title: A Theranostic Approach ([68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G and [177Lu] ...
sources.snippet: The radioactive study drugs, [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G and [177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G, attach to a protein on the surface of tumor cells. When gallium-68 is attached, these radioactive molecules can be used to detect tumor cells using positron emission tomography (PET)/ computed tomography (CT) scans.

sources.title: [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G as a Diagnostic Imaging Agent for Metastatic/Advanced Invasive Lobular Breast Cancer (LBC)

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics has Successfully Dosed the First Cohort in its Phase 1/2 Study Evaluating a Novel FAP-Targeted Radiopharmaceutical in Patients with Late-Stage Aggressive Sarcomas
sources.snippet: BOSTON, Dec. 16, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, announced today that dosing of the first cohort has been completed in the ATLAS trial, a Phase 1/2 open-label study evaluating the safety, tolerability, and efficacy of the company's lead therapeutic radiopharmaceutical, [Ac-225]-RTX-2358, targeting fibroblast activation protein‑α (FAP) in patients with relapsed or refractory soft tissue sarcomas. ... The Phase 2 portion of the study will evaluate the efficacy and safety of [Ac-225]-RTX-2358 in up to 50 patients. [Ac-225]-RTX-2358 is being manufactured by Pharmalogic. [Cu-64]-LNTH-1363S is being provided by Lantheus and manufactured by PharmaLogic.

[68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G PET/CT will be used to identify and stratify patients eligible for (and most likely to respond to) the [177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G therapy.

sources.title: A Theranostic Approach ([68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G and [177Lu] ...
sources.snippet: This phase I trial tests the safety, side effects and effectiveness of gallium Ga 68-DOTA-5G ([68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G) and lutetium Lu 177-DOTA-ABM-5G ([177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G) in detecting and treating cancers that have spread from where they first started (primary site) to other places in the body (metastatic). The radioactive study drugs, [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G and [177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G, attach to a protein on the surface of tumor cells. When gallium-68 is attached, these radioactive molecules can be used to detect tumor cells using positron emission tomography (PET)/ computed tomography (CT) scans. ... These radioactive molecules can also be used to treat tumors (when lutetium-177 is attached).

sources.title: First-in-human Study of the Theranostic Pair [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G and [177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G in Pancreatic Cancer | MedPath
sources.snippet: This is a Phase I, first-in-human study to evaluate the safety and efficacy of the \[68Ga\]Ga DOTA-5G and \[177Lu\]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G theranostic pair in patients with locally advanced or metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma (PDAC). ... The overall purpose of this study is to identify the dose limiting toxicity (DLT) and recommended phase 2 dose (RP2D) of \[177Lu\]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G. A 3+3 study design in is proposed to identify the RP2D of \[177Lu\]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G.

(0.85): "This phase I trial tests the safety, side effects and effectiveness of gallium Ga 68-DOTA-5G ([68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G) and lutetium Lu 177-DOTA-ABM-5G ([177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G) in detecting and treating cancers that have spread from where they first started (primary site) to other places in the body (metastatic). The radioactive study drugs, [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G and [177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G, attach to a protein on the surface of tumor cells. When gallium-68 is attached, these radioactive molecules can be used to detect tumor cells using positron emission tomography (PET)/ computed tomography (CT) scans. ... These radioactive molecules can also be used to treat tumors (when lutetium-177 is attached)." (https://www.cancer.gov/research/participate/clinical-trials-search/v?id=NCI-2024-03571)

sources.snippet: This is a Phase I, first-in-human study to evaluate the safety and efficacy of the \[68Ga\]Ga DOTA-5G and \[177Lu\]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G theranostic pair in patients with locally advanced or metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma (PDAC). ... The overall purpose of this study is to identify the dose limiting toxicity (DLT) and recommended phase 2 dose (RP2D) of \[177Lu\]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G. A 3+3 study design in is proposed to identify the RP2D of \[177Lu\]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G.

sources.title: Gallium Ga 68-DOTA-5G and Lutetium Lu 177-DOTA-ABM-5G in ...
sources.snippet: This pair of radiotracers is termed a “theranostic pair” because gallium Ga 68-DOTA-5G can be used for the diagnosis and determination of the extent of the cancer, while lutetium lu 177-DOTA-ABM-5G can be used for treatment by delivering targeted radioactivity.

sources.title: Molecularly Targeted Theranostic Approach for the Detection ...
sources.snippet: This is a Phase I study to evaluate the safety and efficacy of the [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G and [177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G theranostics pair in patients with metastatic cancer. [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G PET/CT will be used to identify and stratify patients eligible for (and most likely to respond to) the [177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G therapy.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Jun 26, 2023: First-in-human evaluation of the t...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Jun 26, 2023: First-in-human evaluation of the theranostic pair 68Ga-DOTA-5G / 177Lu-DOTA-ABM-5G presented at SNMMI Annual Meeting, confirming ability to detect metastatic pancreatic cancer and visualize targeted treatment [1].
• 2023: Phase I arm of the pancreatic cancer study (NCT04665947) was expected to be completed in summer 2023, with a second trial for metastatic non-small cell lung cancer anticipated to open in fall 2023, and a third trial for metastatic carcinoma of any primary origin expected in winter 2023 [1].
• Dec 10, 2020: Phase I, first-in-human study (NCT04665947) initiated to evaluate safety and efficacy of the [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G /[177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G theranostic pair in patients with locally advanced or metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) [2].

The Phase I arm of this study will be completed in the summer of 2023, and researchers anticipate that a second trial in patients with metastatic non-small cell lung cancer will open to enrollment in fall 2023. A third trial for patients with metastatic carcinoma of any primary origin is expected to commence in the winter of 2023." (https://snmmi.org/Web/Web/News/Articles/SNMMI-Image-of-the-Year--New-Theranostic-Pair-Facilitates-Early-Detection-and-Effective-Treatment-of.aspx)

# A Theranostic Approach ([68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G and [177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G) for the Detection and Treatment of Metastatic Cancers
This phase I trial tests the safety, side effects and effectiveness of gallium Ga 68-DOTA-5G ([68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G) and lutetium Lu 177-DOTA-ABM-5G ([177Lu]Lu DOTA-ABM-5G) in detecting and treating cancers that have spread from where they first started (primary site) to other places in the body (metastatic).

sources.title: [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G as a Diagnostic Imaging Agent for ...
sources.snippet:
# [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G as a Diagnostic Imaging Agent for Metastatic or Advanced Invasive Lobular Breast Cancer
This phase I trial tests the safety, side effects and effectiveness of [68Ga]Ga DOTA-5G as a diagnostic imaging agent for patients with invasive lobular breast cancer that has spread from where it first started (primary site) to other places in the body (metastatic) or that may have spread from where it first started to nearby tissue, lymph nodes, or distant parts of the body (advanced).

sources.title: Lead-203 DOTA-VMT-MCR1 for Melanoma | Open Medscience
sources.snippet:
Lead-203 DOTA-VMT-MCR1 comprises a ligand, VMT01, conjugated to the radioactive isotope Lead-203 (
203Pb) via a chelator (DOTA).
This labelling enables highly specific detection of MC1R-expressing cells and is critical for imaging metastatic melanoma. ... Its therapeutic analogue, 212Pb-VMT01, instead uses Lead-212, an alpha-emitting isotope that delivers potent cytotoxic radiation directly to tumour cells.

203Pb) via a chelator (DOTA).
This labelling enables highly specific detection of MC1R-expressing cells and is critical for imaging metastatic melanoma. ... Its therapeutic analogue, 212Pb-VMT01, instead uses Lead-212, an alpha-emitting isotope that delivers potent cytotoxic radiation directly to tumour cells.

comment.original_value: Image screening to detect patients likely to benefit from ^212Pb-VMT01
comment.validator_explanation: NCI specifies the agent is a 'radioconjugate composed of the melanocyte-stimulating hormone receptor (MSHR; melanocortin-1 receptor; MC1R)-targeting peptide, VMT01' [1]. OpenMedScience details the biochemistry: 'Lead-203 DOTA-VMT-MCR1 comprises a ligand, VMT01, conjugated to the radioactive isotope Lead-203 via a chelator (DOTA)' [4]. Its purpose is confirmed as imaging for 'patient selection, diagnostic imaging, and dosimetry' for 212Pb-VMT01 therapy [7] and performing 'patient-specific dosimetry for [212Pb]VMT01 -therapy' [8].

comment.validator_explanation: Current value 'Diagnostic (pre-therapy screening)' is outdated and understates the stage. Perspective Therapeutics states its 'melanoma (VMT01)... programs are in Phase 1/2a imaging and therapy trials in the U.S.' [9]. The specific therapeutic trial NCT05655312 is a 'phase 1/2a study' [7] and is listed as 'Phase 1' and 'Recruiting' [10]. PatSnap also lists the phase as 'Phase 2 Clinical' for related entries [2], indicating advanced clinical development beyond pure screening.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • Sep 15, 2025: Perspective Therapeutics progresse...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• Sep 15, 2025: Perspective Therapeutics progresses dose finding for [212Pb]VMT01 in combination with nivolumab; Safety Monitoring Committee recommends evaluating higher dose (3.0 mCi) based on encouraging early safety and anti-tumor activity [1]
• Mar 17, 2025: First patient dosed with [212Pb]VMT01 in combination with nivolumab in Phase 1/2a study (NCT05655312); initial results show promising safety profile with no dose-limiting toxicities and early signs of anti-tumor activity [2]
• Feb 29, 2024: Perspective Therapeutics signs exclusive worldwide license with Stony Brook University for CB7-Adma pre-targeting platform and receives up to $2.4M NIH Fastrack SBIR grant for antibody-based radiopharmaceutical development [3]
• Feb 29, 2024: Company's melanoma (VMT01) and neuroendocrine tumor (VMT-α-NET) programs enter Phase 1/2a imaging and therapy trials at several leading academic institutions [3]
• Oct 16, 2023: Perspective completes recruitment for first patient cohort in Phase 1/2a dose escalation trial of [212Pb]VMT01 (NCT05655312) in malignant melanoma [4]

comment.key_citation:
[V1] Perspective Therapeutics Progresses Dose Finding for [212Pb ... (0.95): ""With the initiation of this second combination cohort, we are building on encouraging early safety and anti-tumor activity observed for [
212Pb]VMT01," commented Markus Puhlmann, Chief Medical Officer of Perspective. "Advancing to a higher dose level with [ 212Pb]VMT01 alongside nivolumab represents an important step in evaluating the promising potential of this targeted alpha therapy in combination with immune checkpoint inhibition for patients with advanced melanoma." ... The Safety Monitoring Committee (SMC) recommended evaluating [ 212Pb]VMT01 at a higher dose based on its review of five patients dosed with [ 212Pb]VMT01 1.5 mCi monotherapy and two patients dosed with [ 212Pb]VMT01 at 1.5 mCi together with nivolumab." (https://perspectivetherapeutics.com/pr/perspective-therapeutics-progresses-dose-finding-for-212pbvmt01-in-combination-with-nivolumab-in-its-ongoing-phase-12a-study-of-mc1rpositive-metastatic-melanoma)

sources.snippet: [212Pb]VMT01 (also written as [212Pb]VMT01) is an investigational targeted alpha therapy for MC1R‑positive metastatic melanoma. ... The program uses a theranostic approach: 203Pb‑VMT01 for SPECT imaging/dosimetry and 212Pb‑VMT01 for therapy. ... Theranostic analogs include [203Pb]VMT01 (SPECT) and [68Ga]VMT02 (PET) for patient selection and organ/tumor dosimetry.

sources.title: Pb-212-VMT01
sources.snippet: 203Pb-DOTA-VMT-MCR1 binds with high affinity and specificity to melanocortin receptor subtype I (MC1R) and can be used for targeted imaging and image-guided therapy of metastatic melanomas.

sources.title: Targeted Imaging of Melanoma for Alpha-Particle ...
sources.snippet: The study hypothesis is that new imaging agents [203Pb]VMT01 and [68Ga]VMT02 can be safely used in humans without independent biological effect and can be used to image melanoma tumors expressing the melanocortin sub-type 1 receptor (MC1R) by SPECT/CT and PET/CT imaging modalities respectively.

comment.validator_explanation: No information found for [68Ga]VMT02 as a therapeutic agent. All sources describe it exclusively as a diagnostic PET imaging tracer used for patient selection. The therapeutic counterpart is [212Pb]VMT01, which is a separate agent [1][3].

sources.title: Study Details | Targeted Imaging of Melanoma for Alpha-Particle ...
sources.snippet: This is a first-in-human study evaluating the suitability of [203Pb]VMT01 for SPECT/CT imaging and [68Ga]VMT02 for PET/CT imaging of MC1R-expressing metastatic melanoma.

sources.title: Press Release
sources.snippet: 212Pb]VMT01 to target and deliver 212Pb to tumor sites expressing MC1R, a protein that can be overexpressed in metastatic melanoma tumors. 1 The Company is conducting a multi-center, open-label dose escalation, dose expansion study (clinicaltrials.gov identifier NCT05655312) in patients with histologically confirmed melanoma and MC1R-positive imaging scans.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
- Sep 2024: FDA granted Fast Track Designation for the diagnosis and treatment of MC1R-positive melanoma using the VMT01/VMT02 theranostic pair [1]
- 2023-2025: [68Ga]VMT02 is being evaluated in a first-in-human Phase 1 cross-over biodistribution study (NCT04904120) for PET/CT imaging of MC1R-expressing metastatic melanoma [2]
- Nov 18, 2024: Ratio Therapeutics entered an exclusive worldwide license and collaboration agreement with Novartis for an SSTR2-targeting radiotherapeutic candidate [3]

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics Enters License and Collaboration Agreement with Novartis for SSTR2-targeting Radiotherapeutic Candidate
sources.snippet:
BOSTON, Nov. 18, 2024 /PRNewswire/ -- Ratio Therapeutics Inc. (Ratio), a pharmaceutical company employing innovative technologies to develop best-in-class radiopharmaceuticals for cancer treatment and monitoring, entered today into an exclusive worldwide license and collaboration agreement with Novartis Pharma AG, a subsidiary of
** Novartis AG** (NYSE: NVS).
The collaboration leverages Ratio's radioligand therapy discovery and development expertise as well as its technology platforms for the development of a Somatostatin Receptor 2 (SSTR2) radiotherapeutic candidate for cancer.

sources.title: Ratio Therapeutics to Establish New Radiopharmaceutical Manufacturing Facility
sources.snippet: This 65,000 square foot manufacturing facility will support vertical integration of Ratio's current and future pipeline of next-generation radiopharmaceuticals, with the capacity to efficiently scale manufacturing for future commercial needs as Ratio advances its candidates through clinical trials.

sources.title: Definition of gallium Ga 68-DOTATATE - NCI Drug Dictionary
sources.snippet:
# gallium Ga 68-DOTATATE
A radioconjugate consisting of the somatostatin analogue tyrosine-3-octreotate (Tyr3-octreotate or TATE) labeled with the positron emission tomography (PET) tracer gallium Ga 68 via the macrocyclic chelating agent dodecanetetraacetic acid (DOTA), which may be used as a somatostatin receptor (SSTR) imaging agent in conjunction with PET to image neuroendocrine tumors (NETs).
Gallium Ga 68-DOTATATE binds to SSTRs, with a much higher affinity for type 2 SSTR, present on the cell membranes of many types of NETs.
This allows for visualization of SSTR-positive cells upon imaging. A radioconjugate consisting of the somatostatin analogue tyrosine-3-octreotate (Tyr3-octreotate or TATE) labeled with the positron emission tomography (PET) tracer gallium Ga 68 via the macrocyclic chelating agent dodecanetetraacetic acid (DOTA), which may be used as a somatostatin receptor (SSTR) imaging agent in conjunction with PET to image neuroendocrine tumors (NETs).

sources.title: Gallium ga 68 dotatate (intravenous route) - Side effects & uses
sources.snippet:
Gallium Ga 68 dotatate injection is used with a PET scan (positron emission tomography) for localization of somatostatin receptor positive neuroendocrine tumors (NETs) in adults and children. Gallium Ga 68 dotatate is a radiopharmaceutical.
Radiopharmaceuticals are radioactive agents, which may be used to find and treat certain diseases or to study the function of the body's organs.

can be targeted using somatostatin analogs.

DOTATATE has the highest affinity to SSTR2 and SSTR2 which are commonly expressed in neuroendocrine tumors (1–6).

comment.validator_explanation: Gallium-68 DOTATATE (NETSPOT) is a diagnostic imaging agent, not a therapeutic agent. No therapeutic radionuclide or payload is associated with this product. [1][2]
comment.qc_reasoning: Confirmed as diagnostic agent only, no therapeutic component.

sources.title: Definition of gallium Ga 68-DOTATATE - NCI Drug Dictionary
sources.snippet:
# gallium Ga 68-DOTATATE
A radioconjugate consisting of the somatostatin analogue tyrosine-3-octreotate (Tyr3-octreotate or TATE) labeled with the positron emission tomography (PET) tracer gallium Ga 68 via the macrocyclic chelating agent dodecanetetraacetic acid (DOTA), which may be used as a somatostatin receptor (SSTR) imaging agent in conjunction with PET to image neuroendocrine tumors (NETs).
Gallium Ga 68-DOTATATE binds to SSTRs, with a much higher affinity for type 2 SSTR, present on the cell membranes of many types of NETs.
This allows for visualization of SSTR-positive cells upon imaging. A radioconjugate consisting of the somatostatin analogue tyrosine-3-octreotate (Tyr3-octreotate or TATE) labeled with the positron emission tomography (PET) tracer gallium Ga 68 via the macrocyclic chelating agent dodecanetetraacetic acid (DOTA), which may be used as a somatostatin receptor (SSTR) imaging agent in conjunction with PET to image neuroendocrine tumors (NETs).

sources.title: Gallium ga 68 dotatate (intravenous route) - Side effects & uses
sources.snippet:
Gallium Ga 68 dotatate injection is used with a PET scan (positron emission tomography) for localization of somatostatin receptor positive neuroendocrine tumors (NETs) in adults and children. Gallium Ga 68 dotatate is a radiopharmaceutical.
Radiopharmaceuticals are radioactive agents, which may be used to find and treat certain diseases or to study the function of the body's organs.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• 2025-10-15: NETSPOT (Gallium Ga 68 dotatate) is commercially available through Advanced Accelerator Applications for PET imaging of somatostatin receptor positive neuroendocrine tumors [1]
• 2025-02-01: Gallium Ga 68 dotatate injection (NETSPOT) is indicated for localization of somatostatin receptor positive neuroendocrine tumors in adults and children via PET scan [2]
• 2022-02-07: 68Ga-DOTATATE provides the imaging standard for well-differentiated neuroendocrine tumors and is utilized in staging, characterization, and restaging of NET patients [3]
• 2017-10-26: 68Ga-DOTATATE has high sensitivity and specificity for NET diagnosis compared to other imaging modalities, especially in low-grade, moderately differentiated and well-differentiated NETs [4]

Target / Indication.display_value: Dopaminergic nerve terminals in the striatum for e...
Target / Indication.full_value: Dopaminergic nerve terminals in the striatum for evaluation of Parkinsonian syndromes; also used for imaging of neuroendocrine tumors (e.g., pheochromocytoma, paraganglioma, carcinoid, medullary thyroid cancer) and gliomas [1][2][3]

comment.validator_explanation: Original value 'Tumor' is too vague. Authoritative sources specify the primary approved indication is for Parkinsonian syndromes, with significant off-label use in neuroendocrine tumors and gliomas. The biological target is dopaminergic nerve terminals via the LAT1 amino acid transporter.
comment.key_citation: [V1] Fluorodopa (18F) - Wikipedia (0.85): "**Fluorodopa (^18^F)**, also known as **FDOPA**, is a fluorinated form of L-DOPA primarily synthesized as its fluorine-18 isotopologue for use as a radiotracer in positron emission tomography (PET). ... Fluorodopa (^18^F) is indicated for use in positron emission tomography (PET) to visualize dopaminergic nerve terminals in the striatum for the evaluation of adults with suspected Parkinsonian syndromes. ... In October 2019, Fluorodopa (^18^F) was approved in the United States for the visual detection of certain nerve cells in adults with suspected Parkinsonian syndromes." (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fluorodopa_(18F))

sources.title: Pharmacokinetic Modeling of 18F-FDOPA PET in the ...
sources.snippet:
A radioactive tracer, 6-^18^F-fluoro-L-dopa (^18^F-FDOPA), has been extensively used in positron emission tomography (PET) brain image studies to assess the presynaptic nigrostriatal dopaminergic function.
The uptake of ^18^F-FDOPA PET demonstrates the activity of the dopa-decarboxylase enzyme in the striatal nerve terminals of dopamine neurons and is correlated with the dopamine storage capacity (7,8,9).

comment.validator_explanation: [^18F]-FDOPA is a diagnostic PET imaging agent, not a therapeutic agent. No evidence found for a therapeutic radionuclide or payload associated with this candidate.
comment.qc_reasoning: Confirmed validator's assessment: [^18F]-FDOPA is a diagnostic PET imaging agent, not a therapeutic. The field is correctly left blank.

A radioactive tracer, 6-^18^F-fluoro-L-dopa (^18^F-FDOPA), has been extensively used in positron emission tomography (PET) brain image studies to assess the presynaptic nigrostriatal dopaminergic function.

Specific Biochemistry & Approach.full_value: Fluorinated analog of L-DOPA (L-3,4-dihydroxy-6-[18F]fluorophenylalanine). Taken up into cells via the neutral amino acid transporter LAT1/4F2hc, which is coupled to the mTOR signaling pathway. It is a substrate for aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase (dopa-decarboxylase) [2][4][5]

comment.validator_explanation: Biochemistry is well-documented: it is a fluorinated amino acid analog that mimics L-DOPA metabolism and transport.

Latest News (Bulleted with Date).display_value: • October 10, 2019: FDA approval for PET imaging o...
Latest News (Bulleted with Date).full_value:
• October 10, 2019: FDA approval for PET imaging of dopaminergic nerve terminals in the striatum for evaluation of adults with suspected Parkinsonian syndromes [1][2]
• Ongoing: Clinical use for neuroendocrine tumor imaging (pheochromocytoma, paraganglioma, carcinoid tumors, medullary thyroid carcinoma) and glioma evaluation [3][4]
• 2024: Research continues on automated synthesis methods for clinical production [5]

Target / Indication.full_value: Somatostatin Receptor 2 (SSTR2) in neuroendocrine tumors (NETs)

comment.validator_explanation: Snippet confirms [68Ga]-Ga-DOTATATE binds to SSTR2 for NET imaging [1] and is used for NET localization [2]
comment.key_citation: [V1] Definition of gallium Ga 68-DOTATATE - NCI Drug Dictionary (0.85): "A radioconjugate consisting of the somatostatin analogue tyrosine-3-octreotate (Tyr3-octreotate or TATE) labeled with the positron emission tomography (PET) tracer gallium Ga 68 via the macrocyclic chelating agent dodecanetetraacetic acid (DOTA), which may be used as a somatostatin receptor (SSTR) imaging agent in conjunction with PET to image neuroendocrine tumors (NETs). Gallium Ga 68-DOTATATE binds to SSTRs, with a much higher affinity for type 2 SSTR, present on the cell membranes of many types of NETs." (https://www.cancer.gov/publications/dictionaries/cancer-drug/def/gallium-ga-68-dotatate)

Initial studies in patients have demonstrated the suitability of ^111^In-PSMA-I&T (^111^In-DOTAGA-(3-iodo-y)-f-k-Sub(KuE)) (PSMA is prostate-specific membrane antigen and I&T is imaging and therapy) for radioguided surgery (RGS) of small metastatic prostate cancer (PCa) soft-tissue lesions.
To meet the clinical need for a more cost-effective alternative, the PSMA-I&T-based tracer concept was adapted to ^99m^Tc-labeling chemistry.
Two PSMA-I&T-derived inhibitors with all-L-serine- (MAS~3~) and all-D-serine- (mas~3~) chelating moieties were evaluated in parallel, and a kit procedure for routine ^99m^Tc labeling was developed. ... Results:
** A robust and reliable kit-labeling procedure was established, allowing the preparation of ^99m^Tc-MAS~3~-y-nal-k(Sub-KuE) and ^99m^Tc-PSMA-I&S in consistently high radiochemical yield and purity (≥98%, n > 50 preparations).
Because of its improved internalization efficiency and superior in vivo stability, ^99m^Tc-PSMA-I&S was selected for further in vivo evaluation.
Compared with ^111^In-PSMA-I&T, ^99m^Tc-PSMA-I&S showed delayed clearance kinetics but identical uptake in PSMA-positive tissues in the LNCaP xenograft model (1 h after injection).
In exemplary PCa patients, a relatively slow whole-body clearance of ^99m^Tc-PSMA-I&S was observed due to high plasma protein binding (94%) of the tracer.
This, however, promoted efficient tracer uptake in PCa lesions over time and led to steadily increasing lesion-to-background ratios up to 21 h after injection.
Preoperative SPECT/CT showed a high ^99m^Tc-PSMA-I&S uptake in all suspect lesions identified in previous ^68^Ga-HBED-CC-Ahx-KuE (^68^Ga-HBED-CC-PSMA) PET/CT, allowing for their successful intraoperative detection and resection during first-in-human RGS.
** ... Conclusion:
** Because of a straightforward and reliable kit production, ^99m^Tc-PSMA-I&S represents a cost-effective, readily available alternative to ^111^In-PSMA-I&T.
Initial patient data indicate its comparable or even superior performance as a probe for PSMA-targeted RGS and also hint toward the unexpected potential of ^99m^Tc-PSMA-I&S as a SPECT imaging agent.
**
